
# THE BOOK OF JOHN{.heading_16}

Introduction {.heading_74}

The Gospel of St. John is the fourth biography of the earthly life of Jesus Christ by a Christian biographer who was an eyewitness to His ministry from John the Baptist's ministry (John 1:6; Matt. 3) up until His return to Glory (Luke 24:31; Acts 1:22).

Of the four Gospels, it is by far the most hated and rejected account of the life of Jesus Christ found in literature by evolutionists, Buddhists, humanists, atheists, Moslems, liberals, left-wing extremists, "modernistic" radicals, infidels, skeptics, and other college-educated asses (Job 11:12).

The reason for this is "crystal clear" (to use an ancient cliché) if anyone has any understanding of [human nature.]{.class_4501} No self-righteous, religious sinner, engaged in [working his way to Heaven,]{.class_4501} is going to appreciate a Judean Jew saying that He is the [only way]{.class_4501} to the true God and if you don't believe on [Him]{.class_4501} you will wind up in [Hell]{.class_4501} (see John 3:18,36, 5:24, 8:24,47, 10:26).

Such things are "verboten" in all religious circles who think their religions are just as good as anyone else's religion, since "man is the measure of all things" and "men call God by different names but it is the same God," since we are all "working to get to the same place" while trying to "make the world a better hog pen (Luke 15:15) to live in." At least that has been the "party line" of every self-righteous unregenerate since A.D. 90 who has exchanged Romans 4:5, Ephesians 2:8--9, Acts 13:39--40, and Titus 3:5 for a "faith" or a "religion" (Hinduism, Taoism, Buddhism, Satanism, Islam, Protestant, Catholic, Greek Orthodox, etc.).

In the Gospel of John, Jesus claims to be ["THE Son of God" ]{.class_4500}(John 9:35), and He also claims to be ["God...manifest in the flesh"]{.class_4500} (1 Tim. 3:16). These verses have caused all unsaved liberals and "modernists" to swear on a stack of dictionaries, lexicons, concordances, collations of manuscripts, and Greek grammars that all of the words in them should be [rejected]{.class_4501} as [false reporting]{.class_4501} on John's part.

["No man hath seen God at any time; the only begotten Son, which is in the bosom of the Father, he hath declared him"]{.class_4500}[]{#6g89t5Y_Rh2_sEm6hxUWsg68.xhtml_page_4} (John 1:18).

["Jesus saith unto her, I that speak unto thee am he"]{.class_4500} (John 4:26).

["Jesus said unto them, Verily, verily, I say unto you, Before Abraham was, I am"]{.class_4500} (John 8:58).

"[I and my Father are one"]{.class_4500} (John 10:30).

["Jesus saith unto him, Have I been so long time with you, and yet hast thou not known me, Philip? he that hath seen me hath seen the Father; and how sayest thou then, Shew us the Father?"]{.class_4500} (John 14:9).

But there they stand, as they can be found for nineteen centuries. Every verse shows [exactly]{.class_4501} what Jesus claimed about Himself and what His disciples found out to be the truth.

John's gospel was unable to get by the faculties of Princeton, Harvard, Yale, and even Cambridge and Oxford as an acceptable [historical account,]{.class_4501} for he admitted, in print (see John 20:31), that his PURPOSE in writing his gospel was so that sinners could obtain EVERLASTING LIFE (see John 3:16,36, 5:24, 11:25, 14:6).

Such things are "no-nos" for [historians.]{.class_4501} Historians are not supposed to write anything for the purpose of proving any Biblical or theological belief; [John does just that.]{.class_4501}

This explains why Matthew, Mark, and Luke are called "synoptic gospels" and the Gospel of John is isolated from them as a genuine [history.]{.class_4501} At least that is what has been taught in nearly every theological seminary for more than 150 years. John is a "loner." He isolated himself in his account before he had written one chapter, or even ten verses, for instead of tracing Jesus Christ's origins to "man" through the first "man" (Adam), like Luke did (see Luke 3:23--38), or tracing His origins to the "father" of the Jews as Matthew did (Matt. 1:1), John traces Jesus Christ's origin to a time BEFORE Genesis 1:1. He does it, again, in his first epistle (see [The Bible Believer's Commentary on the General Epistles, ]{.class_4501}Vol. 2).

Weizsacker denies that any apostle wrote it. Holtzmann agrees with him. Schürer says it is not a historical account at all but just John trying to show you how God revealed Himself in Christ.

These three unsaved German rationalists are typical of the "scholarship" behind Nestle's Greek New Testament text.

These and other apostates (Gaius and Marcion) suggest that Cerinthus wrote it.

Tatian (160) quotes it as John's writing. Polycarp (110 A.D.) does the same. If John wrote it, he is giving pre-4000 B.C. as history.

That is another "no-no" for historians. No historian should be allowed to describe what happened [before]{.class_4501} 4000 B.C. unless he adheres to the monkey men (Darwin, Huxley, Spencer, Chambers, Paley, Lyell, Haeckel, Marx, Freud, Churchill, Einstein, Leaky, and the [National Geographic ]{.class_4501}magazine) with their little cartoon Disneyworld "charts" through 1,000--2,000,000,000 years of [philosophical guesswork.]{.class_4501} (See the 400 standard scientific proofs against evolution in [The Evolution Cruncher,]{.class_4501} Vance Ferrell, 2001, EF Publishers.)

H. G. Wells (1866--1946) speaks for several million college-educated monkey men when he writes "WE do not know how life began on earth." The "editorial WE" used in this sentence should be limited to monkey men, [only,]{.class_4501} who are stupid enough to believe their relatives were tarsiers, lemurs, apes, great apes, gorillas, monkeys, orangutans, and black Africans ("Lucy").

Speaking for several million Bible-believing Christians, I hasten to say---in the interests of "minority rights"---that "WE" know [exactly]{.class_4501} how human life began on this earth and have an [historical account]{.class_4501} of it that gives us a date near 4000 B.C., for "man" to show up (check out the years listed chronologically in Gen. 4--6, 10--12, etc).

The Gospel of John contains 21 chapters, 879 verses, and 19,099 words in the English text: [Holy Bible, Authorized Version.]{.class_4501}

The explanation for its uniqueness (when compared to Matthew, Mark, and Luke) lies in a fact so simple that only a seminary-educated idiot, educated beyond his intelligence, could miss it. When John begins to write, he has all fourteen of Paul's epistles on the table in front of him, or he has seen and read them previously. []{#6g89t5Y_Rh2_sEm6hxUWsg68.xhtml_page_6}When Luke writes (see Acts 1:1--2; Luke 1:1--4) he has, at a maximum, access only to Galatians, Romans, 1 and 2 Corinthians, and (maybe) Thessalonians.

Matthew and Mark did not have [that]{.class_4501} when they wrote. They might have seen Galatians and that would be about it.

But that is not all. Luke wrote his gospel and the Acts [before the destruction of Jerusalem.]{.class_4501} When John writes he is writing nearly twenty years [after]{.class_4501} the Jews ceased to be a nation. That explains perfectly the "disparities" between John and the "Synoptics."

Why would his biography resemble Matthew's at all? The theme of Matthew's gospel was ["the King of the Jews"]{.class_4500} (see [The Bible Believer's Commentary on Matthew,]{.class_4501} 1970, Bible Baptist Bookstore).

Even Luke, with this "Gentile touch," going clear back to the first NON-JEW (Adam: Luke 3:38), does not have the universal outlook that John has (John 1:3,9, 3:16,36, 4:21--42, 12:31--32, etc.).

This explains the "synoptic problem" that the highly-educated Christian scholars in England, Europe, and America professed to have found between 1700 and 2003. Being crooked as well as [stupid]{.class_4501}---see a list of 320 of them in [The Christian Liars' Library]{.class_4501}, 1997---they erected a twenty-foot-high "straw dummy" and then took turns throwing cream puffs at it for a couple of centuries: standard, SOP; absolutely predictable, absolutely standard for every Bible-correcting, brainwashed, programmed clone in the lot.

For a similar performance, obtain a copy of [The Mythological Septuagint]{.class_4501} (1996) and watch the same bunch (same crowd, with the same mentors and role models) trying to get you to think that Matthew, Mark, Luke, and John (and Paul!) corrected the Hebrew Old Testament with a Gentile Greek "Septuagint" that had no more existence before A.D. 120 than the Gettysburg Address or the UN charter.

# Chapter 1 {.heading_74}

1:1 In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God.

2 The same was in the beginning with God.

3 All things were made by him; and without him was not any thing made that was made.

The first verse begins as the Bible began:[ "In the beginning."]{.class_4500} First John 1:1 almost matches it: ["that which was from the beginning."]{.class_4500} John does not hesitate to equate Jesus Christ with [Deity.]{.class_4501} Paul doesn't hesitate either (["For by him were all things created, that are in heaven, and that are in earth, visible and invisible, whether they be thrones, or dominions, or principalities, or powers: all things were created by him, and for him," ]{.class_4500}Col. 1:16). A sinless birth. 2. A sinless life. 3. The ability to hear 900,000,000 sinners all praying at the same time.

They carefully [avoid]{.class_4501} using the Scriptural term (["scripture"]{.class_4500}) when referring to God's WORDS. ["For there are three that bear record in heaven, the Father, the Word, and the Holy Ghost: and these three are one"]{.class_4500} (1 John 5:7).

In eternity, the term ["Word"]{.class_4500} was used to describe Jesus Christ instead of the word ["SON"]{.class_4500} (as in the Trinity in Matt. 28:19). Nestle's (and Aland and Metzger) read, Gk.: [kai choris autou egeneto oude hen]{.class_4501}, and then attached the next two words, Gk.: [ho gegonen]{.class_4501} to verse 4. This is the [NRSV]{.class_4501} reading.

4 In him was life; and the life was the light of men.

5 And the light shineth in darkness; and the darkness comprehended it not.

6 There was a man sent from God, whose name was John.

7 The same came for a witness, to bear witness of the Light, that all men through him might believe.

8 He was not that Light, but was sent to bear witness of that Light.

"The baptism of fire" is reserved for [Christ-rejecting sinners in Hell]{.class_4501} which is evident on both sides of the verse in Matthew (3:10,12) that [define]{.class_4501} it. You need that baptism like you need third degree burns between your legs. [It is the Charismatics who wanted it!]{.class_4501}

Verse 34 is self explanatory.

35 Again the next day after John stood, and two of his disciples;

36 And looking upon Jesus as he walked, he saith, Behold the Lamb of God!

37 And the two disciples heard him speak, and they followed Jesus.

38 Then Jesus turned, and saw them following, and []{#6g89t5Y_Rh2_sEm6hxUWsg128.xhtml_page_50}saith unto them, What seek ye? They said unto him, Rabbi, (which is to say, being interpreted, Master,) where dwellest thou?

39 He saith unto them, Come and see. They came and saw where he dwelt, and abode with him that day: for it was about the tenth hour.

40 One of the two which heard John speak, and followed him, was Andrew, Simon Peter's brother.

41 He first findeth his own brother Simon, and saith unto him, We have found the Messias, which is, being interpreted, the Christ.

42 And he brought him to Jesus. And when Jesus beheld him, he said, Thou art Simon the son of Jona: thou shalt be called Cephas, which is by interpretation, A stone.

We have already commented on verses 35--36.[ "One of the two,"]{.class_4500} and ["the TWO disciples"]{.class_4500} was Andrew (vs. 37 cf. vss. 40--41).

Andrew, Simon Peter's brother, beats "Pete" to the draw when it comes to identifying Jesus as the Messiah (vs. 41). Peter doesn't make his profession until Caesarea Philippi (Matt. 16:16)[ "He saith unto them, Come and see"]{.class_4500} (vs. 39).

These are Christ's first utterances in the Gospel of John. His second utterance is ["Follow me"]{.class_4500} (vs. 43). Those two simple, quiet statements---one is an invitation and the other is a command---pretty well sum up New Testament salvation. The invitation to ["COME"]{.class_4500} is obviously an invitation for any sinner to come to HIM (see Matt. 11:28; John 6:37, 5:40; Rev. 22:14--17); not to a church or a faith or a religion or a sacrament or Mary or Rome or Mecca or Medina [or any other demoniac dump.]{.class_4501}

The command to follow HIM obviously eliminates Mohammed, Buddha, Moses, Elijah (see the Father's comments in Matt. 17:5) the "Vicar of Christ," some priest, Cardinal, bishop, guru, shaman, imam, witch doctor, ulama, Darwin, or anyone else. Paul tells his disciples to follow him but is careful to add ["as I also am ]{.class_4500}\[a follower\][ of Christ."]{.class_4500}

The Infantry School not only borrowed our Lord's sleeping place (Luke 9:58) but His command: ["FOLLOW ME."]{.class_4500}[]{#6g89t5Y_Rh2_sEm6hxUWsg128.xhtml_page_51} That is the motto of the Infantry School in Fort Benning, Georgia.

You "follow" Jesus into Heaven, but you follow the Infantry School into the nearest thing to Hell there has ever been on this earth. "War is God's judgment on sin here; Hell is God's judgment on sin hereafter."

["About the tenth hour."]{.class_4500} This will be Roman time as in John 19:14, with the "day" beginning at 12:01 a.m. ["Where he dwelt,"]{.class_4500} at this time, is not located, for it certainly was not his hometown (Nazareth) or Bethany.

Andrew immediately goes and witnesses to his brother. It is remarkable when you consider that the first "brother and brother" incident in the New Testament completely negates the first brother and brother incident in the Old Testament. You talk about ["the law by Moses, but\...etc."]{.class_4500} and the contrast given in Romans 10:5--8! Here, one brother takes to himself the responsibility of being his ["brother's keeper"]{.class_4500} (see Gen. 4). He goes and finds him, not to kill him as Cain did, but to introduce him to Jesus Christ (vs. 41). Andrew is one of the "wise men" Solomon spoke of in Proverbs 11:30. Andrew is a "fisher of men." He is a soulwinner.

["And he brought him to Jesus"]{.class_4500} (vs. 42).

My, what a statement! ["And he brought him to Jesus."]{.class_4500} That was the right place to bring any man according to verses 1, 3, 4, 9, and 12. If every disciple of Jesus Christ had won just [one soul]{.class_4501} to Christ per year---say, Paul forty in forty years and each one of them fifty in the next fifty years, etc.---there would have been 235,000,000 saved people on this earth before the destruction of Jerusalem (A.D. 70). Figure it out. If every sinner who got saved led one other sinner to Jesus Christ every year after he was saved, the entire world would have been converted to Christ long BEFORE Columbus discovered America.

The Body of Christ simply failed miserably to carry out her orders (see Acts 1:8 and Luke 24:47). All dispensations end in apostasy, including the Church Age (2 Tim. 3--4).

["Thou are Simon\...Cephas\...a stone"]{.class_4500} (vs. 42).

Thus it turns out that there are two "stones" (Peter and Christ; 1 Pet. 2:4--6) who are called "rocks" (Matt. 16:18; 1 Pet. 2:8). But []{#6g89t5Y_Rh2_sEm6hxUWsg128.xhtml_page_52}unfortunately for ALL Catholics, their leaders picked the wrong ["rock,"]{.class_4500} for Peter in Matthew 16:18 is not "Cephas" (Aramaic): he is "petros." Jesus Christ, in the same verse, is "petra," a [huge rock]{.class_4501}---say, the size of Stone Mountain in Georgia.

Again! In Latin, Peter is "Petrus," but Jesus Christ is "Petram" in the same verse. Again! The English says Peter is ["Peter"]{.class_4500} and Jesus Christ is[ "THE ROCK"]{.class_4500} (1 Cor. 10:4; Deut. 32:31). The Popes evidently (like Mohammed) have "rocks" for brains (or they've gotten "stoned" on "white lightin'").

43 The day following Jesus would go forth into Galilee, and findeth Philip, and saith unto him, Follow me.

44 Now Philip was of Bethsaida, the city of Andrew and Peter.

45 Philip findeth Nathanael, and saith unto him, We have found him, of whom Moses in the law, and the prophets, did write, Jesus of Nazareth, the son of Joseph.

46 And Nathanael said unto him, Can there any good thing come out of Nazareth? Philip saith unto him, Come and see.

47 Jesus saw Nathanael coming to him, and saith of him, Behold an Israelite indeed, in whom is no guile!

48 Nathanael saith unto him, Whence knowest thou me? Jesus answered and said unto him, Before that Philip called thee, when thou wast under the fig tree, I saw thee.

49 Nathanael answered and saith unto him, Rabbi, thou art the Son of God; thou art the King of Israel.

50 Jesus answered and said unto him, Because I said unto thee, I saw thee under the fig tree, believest thou? thou shalt see greater things than these.

51 And he saith unto him, Verily, verily, I say unto you, Hereafter ye shall see heaven open, and the angels of God ascending and descending upon the Son of man.

By comparing John 1:44 with Mark 1:21--29, all of the commentators [assume]{.class_4501} a contradiction or [invent]{.class_4501} two different "Bethsaidas" to fix things up. It never occurs to these ["blind leaders of the blind"]{.class_4500} that Peter could have been raised in Bethsaida and bought him a house, later, in Capernaum. ["The CITY of Peter and Andrew"]{.class_4500} could have been just a reference to Peter's birthplace; and if he had been [raised]{.class_4501} there and not left it until he was eighteen to twenty-one, it certainly would have been called "his city," especially if he had just bought the house a month or two before running into John the Baptist.

["Philip findeth Nathaniel"]{.class_4500} (vs. 45).

Here is another soulwinner. They are traveling from Bethabara near the south end of Jordan, northward up into Galilee (vs. 43) and pick up Nate on the way (vs. 45).

["Jesus of Nazareth, the son of Joseph"]{.class_4500} (vs. 45).

Notice that Philip is not saying "we have found the man that Moses and the prophets said was the son of Joseph." Neither Moses or the Law said the Messiah would come from any man named ["Joseph."]{.class_4500} Philip is merely saying, "We have checked on this Jesus of Nazareth, and he is the son of a man in Nazareth named Joseph."

His statement on the Messiah therefore is "We have at last found the man that qualifies to be the Messiah that Moses and the prophets spoke about."

["Can there any good thing come out of Nazareth?"]{.class_4500} Nathaniel is from Cana, a neighboring town of Nazareth. His remark is sarcastic. In the twenty-first century, I find delight in saying "Can anything good come out of Las Vegas?" or "Can any good thing come out of California?" or "Can any good thing come out of New York?" Well, the answer would be "Certainly, of course; SOME good thing might come out of them---[but not much!"]{.class_4501}[]{#6g89t5Y_Rh2_sEm6hxUWsg128.xhtml_page_54} Nathaniel is not thinking theologically about the Messiah coming from anywhere. He is without guile. He is speaking his mind as any guileless man would (see Job and Jonah 4:9 for good examples. Jeremiah is another excellent one; look at Jeremiah 15:18).

You want to see a "guileless" man in action? Watch Simon Peter in Matthew 16:22 and John 13:8!

["Knowing what is in men"]{.class_4500} (see John 2:24--25), the Lord Jesus reads Nathaniel's character without talking with him. His words in verse 47 are spoken before Nathaniel gets near enough to Him for Him to hear the remark about Nazareth.

["Whence knowest thou me?"]{.class_4500} They had never met before, never had been introduced before, never carried on a conversation before, and as far as Nate knew, nobody had ever told Jesus what kind of an "Israelite" he was.

Jesus Christ "knew" him when He saw him (vs. 48). He was sitting under the only tree that Jesus ever cursed. That tree was a symbol of unconverted national Israel---as a nation without their King (Matt. 24:32--33). Its first appearance is in a play we call "The Fig Leaf Factor" (Gen. 3). It was the original universal symbol for a sinner trying to hide his sins behind SELF-RIGHTEOUSNESS.

["Thou art the Son of God; thou art the King of Israel" ]{.class_4500}(vs. 49).

And there is the quickest conversion Jesus Christ ever had. Nate recognizes what the dying thief recognized: Jesus Christ was a King. He also recognized what Mary [had to be told:]{.class_4501} that Jesus Christ was a Jewish king who would reign over twelve tribes (Luke 1:31--33). He further recognized what the most highly educated and religious leaders of his day never figured out: he realized that the "Son of [David"]{.class_4501} had to be the "Son of [God"]{.class_4501} (see Matt. 22:45).

That is some grasp of Biblical truth to get in less than sixty seconds! It would demonstrate that if any man is really genuine, honest, straight-forward, and sincere (["without guile"]{.class_4500}), he could find more truth (Prov. 8:6--9, 22:17--21) in the Bible in one reading than a Bible-revising, Bible-correcting, "Scholarship Only" advocate could get out of thirty readings.

["Hereafter ye shall see\...upon the Son of man" ]{.class_4500}[]{#6g89t5Y_Rh2_sEm6hxUWsg128.xhtml_page_55}(vs. 50).

"A ladder reaching from the deepest abyss of earth \[you just said the earth was the "gate to [heaven"!]{.class_4501}\] of an earth submerged in sin to the highest heaven of PURITY." Where in hades did THAT come from?\] is not founded by earthly power or force \[Luke 19:14,27\] but by what is especially HUMAN, sympathy, REASON, and self sacrifice."

This "kingdom" reigned over by the ["King of kings"]{.class_4500} is a literal, physical, visible, [Jewish political kingdom:]{.class_4501} an international theocracy whose subjects have nothing to do with its coming into being (Luke 19:14,27) and had everything to do with [preventing it from showing up]{.class_4501} (Rev. 19:19--20; Psa. 2:1--4).

# Chapter 2 {.heading_74}

2:1 And the third day there was a marriage in Cana of Galilee; and the mother of Jesus was there:

2 And both Jesus was called, and his disciples, to the marriage.

3 And when they wanted wine, the mother of Jesus saith unto him, They have no wine.

4 Jesus saith unto her, Woman, what have I to do with thee? mine hour is not yet come.

5 His mother saith unto the servants, Whatsoever he saith unto you, do it.

In the chronology that preceded chapter 2, you should take note that the "three days" mentioned in John 1:29, 35, and 43 all take place AFTER Christ's temptation (recorded in Matt. 4 and Luke 4). This is apparent from Luke 4:1 where Jesus returns ["from Jordan"]{.class_4500} and THEN goes into the wilderness. This means that Jesus is not getting baptized [anywhere ]{.class_4501}in John 1:29, 35, or 43. His baptism (Matt. 3; Luke 3) [was past.]{.class_4501} The scholars who fell into this snare and tried to find a "contradiction" in the [AV ]{.class_4501}text simply broke their stupid necks, again, on verse 32. John was reciting verse 32 as a [past event.]{.class_4501} When he says ["Behold the Lamb of God"]{.class_4500} in verse 29, he is pointing out a Messiah whom he had [already baptized;]{.class_4501} Christ shows up in 1:29 ready to start His public ministry (look at Luke 4:14. It is the wedding you are about to study).

["The third day"]{.class_4500} (John 2:1) is one, two, three from John 1:35. ["The next day"]{.class_4500} of John 1:29 is just the next day after the Levites showed up from Jerusalem; not the ["next day"]{.class_4500} after Christ's baptism.

["A marriage in Cana of Galilee"]{.class_4500} (vs. 1).

"The Holy Sacrament of Marriage" (Roman Catholic) is just one more anti-biblical whitewash job to make you think that no couple is JOINED together unless it takes place in a [church building]{.class_4501} where some black-robed bachelor blabbers some [words.]{.class_4501}

You were "joined" to every woman you ever "lay" with. ("Had sex with" is the news media, NEA "double speak" for it.)

That includes [virgins]{.class_4501} (see Deut. 22:28--29) and [whores]{.class_4501} (1 Cor. 6:16--17).

God doesn't join saved women to unsaved men (2 Cor. 6:14). YOU do.

God doesn't join saved men to harlots (2 Cor. 6:15). YOU do.

In spite of the "historic position" of 900,000,000 million Romans Catholics, 400,000,000 Greek Orthodox "communicants," and more than 300,000,000 Baptists, you still cannot join your body to any woman without [getting "married,"]{.class_4501} and you cannot get [rid]{.class_4501} of any woman without biblical grounds for doing it. In the Book you CAN get rid of a wife with divorce since God did it [Himself]{.class_4501} (Hosea 2:2; Isa. 50:1).

Whenever you fornicate with any woman, you become ["ONE FLESH"]{.class_4500} with her just like she was your wife (see 1 Cor. 6:16--17).

[This means that 2,000 years of "historic positions" aren't worth a grain of sand on a beach.]{.class_4501} The woman in Romans 7:1--4 was not "divorced" at all; she was an [adulteress]{.class_4501} stepping out on her husband. ["And the mother of Jesus was there"]{.class_4500} (vs. 1).

This is her "first mention" in John's gospel. All of the details found in Matthew 1 and 2 are missing, as are all of the details of Luke 1 and 2. ["Jesus saith unto her, Woman, what have I to do with thee? mine hour is not yet come" ]{.class_4500}(vs. 4).

And to add ghastliness to blasphemy, the only time anyone dares [apply Mary's prophecy]{.class_4501} (found in Luke 1:48), Jesus [corrects]{.class_4501} them by saying, "You made a mistake, sister, I can tell you someone who is more blessed than Mary."

"And it came to pass, as he spake these things, a certain woman of the company lifted up her voice, and said unto him, Blessed is the womb that bare thee, and the paps which thou hast sucked.

["Mine hour is not yet come"]{.class_4500} (vs. 4). Jesus Christ's response to Mary's complaint (["they have no wine"]{.class_4500}) is absolutely enigmatic. No one within "hearin' range" of His voice could have made the wildest guess as to why He said what He said. You and I could not have if we had been rabbinical doctors of the Law (Acts 5:34) with thirty years of formal education.

He is talking about supplying the wedding guests (Matt. 22:2--3,9) [with His blood.]{.class_4501} He buys His own bride [with blood]{.class_4501} (Acts 20:28; Eph. 5).

Since the resurrection is connected with His ["hour,"]{.class_4500} He extends it to the resurrection of the dead (see John 5:25, 29). But the beginning of that ["hour"]{.class_4500} is clearly His coming death on the cross (see John 12:23, 17:1, 13:1, 16:32).

The first New Testament miracle is Christ turning [water to wine]{.class_4501} (a type of [blood:]{.class_4501} see Deut. 32:14; Num. 6:3--4, and comments on Gen. 3:17 in that commentary). 3. Since WATER is the first [natural birth]{.class_4501} (John 3:5) and since Christ got His blood from His FATHER (Acts 20:28)---a supernatural birth---John's ["blood and water"]{.class_4500} (1 John 5:8) point out Christ's [two natures]{.class_4501} when He was here as a [MAN.]{.class_4501}

# Chapter 3 {.heading_74}

3:1 There was a man of the Pharisees, named Nicodemus, a ruler of the Jews:

2 The same came to Jesus by night, and said unto him, Rabbi, we know that thou art a teacher come from God: for no man can do these miracles that thou doest, except God be with him.

3 Jesus answered and said unto him, Verily, verily, I say unto thee, Except a man be born again, he cannot see the kingdom of God.

4 Nicodemus saith unto him, How can a man be born when he is old? can he enter the second time into his mother's womb, and be born?

5 Jesus answered, Verily, verily, I say unto thee, Except a man be born of water and of the Spirit, he cannot enter into the kingdom of God.

6 That which is born of the flesh is flesh; and that which is born of the Spirit is spirit.

7 Marvel not that I said unto thee, Ye must be born again.

This familiar scene and conversation follows "head on heels" the statement that ["he knew what was in man"]{.class_4500} (John 2:25). It says, ["THERE WAS A MAN"]{.class_4500} etc. (vs.1).

29 He that hath the bride is the bridegroom: but the friend of the bridegroom, which standeth and heareth him, rejoiceth greatly because of the bridegroom's voice: this my joy therefore is fulfilled.

30 He must increase, but I must decrease.

["Aenon"]{.class_4500} and ["Salim"]{.class_4500} have never been exactly located, but John is baptizing in that place, and it is near enough to the northeast corner of Judea for the people to compare his baptism with the ones []{#6g89t5Y_Rh2_sEm6hxUWsg729.xhtml_page_97}being carried on by Peter, James, John, and company (see 4:1--2).

Verse 25. We have discussed this matter thoroughly under John 2:6, which see. John knew his place. He may have been filled with the Holy Ghost from his mother's womb and have been the greatest prophet ever[ "born of a woman"]{.class_4500} but he was no prig, no boaster, no strutter, or braggart. His reply is:

"He (Jesus) got His power from God to do what He is doing (["from heaven"]{.class_4500}), and if he passes me by in reported baptisms, it is because He is the Messiah (vs. 28) [and I am not.]{.class_4501} He is [above]{.class_4501} me (vs. 31) and always has been and always will be (vs. 30)."

He is ["the friend of the bridegroom."]{.class_4500} He NEVER WAS in the [Body of Christ,]{.class_4501} for Christ forms no "body" till [AFTER]{.class_4501} Acts 1 (see John 7:38--39).

The "family of God" (Eph. 3:15) contains:

1\. A [Father]{.class_4501} (God the Father) and a Son---who is a ["bridegroom"]{.class_4500} (Psa. 19:5).

31 He that cometh from above is above all: he that is of the earth is earthly, and speaketh of the earth: he that cometh from heaven is above all.

Verse 31 hearkens back to 1 Corinthians 15 (written twenty-five years before John wrote his gospel), where Paul said, ["the first man is of the earth, earthy"]{.class_4500} (1 Cor. 15:47).[ "The second man"]{.class_4500} (the second ["Adam"]{.class_4500}) is ["above all"]{.class_4500} (John 3:31), for He is ["the Lord from HEAVEN"]{.class_4500} (1 Cor. 15:47). ["And no man receiveth his testimony"]{.class_4500} is not 100 percent true, for John notes the exceptions immediately: ['HE THAT HATH RECEIVED HIS TESTIMONY"]{.class_4500} (vs. 33).

["Speaketh the WORDS OF GOD"]{.class_4500} (vs. 34); not "the Word of God" or the "principles" or "teachings" or "fundamentals of the faith." # Chapter 4 {.heading_74}

4:1 When therefore the Lord knew how the Pharisees had heard that Jesus made and baptized more disciples than John,

2 (Though Jesus himself baptized not, but his disciples,)

3 He left Judaea, and departed again into Galilee.

4 And he must needs go through Samaria.

Again, we notice John's "flow of thought" as he writes, for he has stated that the wrath of God ABIDES on a Christ rejector, so in verse 1 of the very next chapter, up pops the Pharisees. When they pop up, John reaches back into the last verse of chapter 2 (which tied into the first verse of chapter 3) and pulls out an example of Christ "knowing what is IN man." ["He left Judaea\...and he must needs go through Samaria"]{.class_4500} (vss. 3--4). Galilee is northern Israel, Judea is southern Israel, and Samaria is right in the middle. Galilee ["of THE NATIONS"]{.class_4500} (Matt. 4:15 cf. Isa. 9:1). This is the order of the post-resurrection commission (Acts 1:8).

This is a good pattern for "personal work" and soulwinning. Every racist in America will try to get you tied up with "Do blacks join your church?" "How many blacks do you have in your school?" Her mind is on physical relief. He has something else in mind. She is nearer to [real]{.class_4501} water than she thinks. Back in the nineteenth century, a paddle wheel ship came alongside a "dead in the water" schooner. About a dozen men were screaming for a drink of water. They were dying at sea near the mouth of the Amazon River.

The bigger ship simply moved past them and yelled "LOWER YOUR BUCKETS!" They did and found although they were a quarter of a mile out in the Atlantic the water was fresh enough to drink.

The woman is thinking of drawing up water out of a well of water in front of her. Knowing her Bible a good bit better than most college graduates in America today know it, she cites what went on in Joshua 24:32. Shechem is in central Samaria, not twenty miles from "Shiloh" were the Ark of the Covenant wound up (Judg. 18:31). Shechem was the place where Joshua gathered Israel for his "last will and testament" (Josh 24:30). The ["mountain"]{.class_4500} that the Samaritan woman is about to mention (vs. 20) is Mt. Ephraim, where Joshua (["JESUS"!]{.class_4500} Acts 7:45) was buried.

An old fashioned Alabama preacher who had been a friend of mind for many years was called "Red" Robinson. I will never forget an incident he told me about during his old Air Corps days (1942--1945). Out in Arizona, three of his buddies were sent out into the wastelands to look for a bomber that had overflown a runway. They had the exact location on a map for finding the grounded bomber, but no radio communications were working. They found nothing, and then their Jeep ran out of gasoline. It was about 125 degrees in the "shade," and there was no shade.

Eight hours later a rescue party sent out to track them found all three of them dead. Two of them had wandered off from the Jeep, and sunstroke had finished them. The third one had evidently woken up to the fact that the only water they had gone to find was in the radiator of their Jeep, several quarts of it. The "rescue party" found him dead, under the Jeep, with his mouth eight inches from the draincock. He had been too weak to turn the small "stopper."

13 Jesus answered and said unto her, Whosoever drinketh of this water shall thirst again:

14 But whosoever drinketh of the water that I shall give him shall never thirst; but the water that I shall give him shall be in him a well of water springing up into everlasting life.

["But the water that I]{.class_4500} \[a Judean Jew\][ shall give him shall NEVER thirst"]{.class_4500} (vs. 14). It goes like this: "All my lifelong I had panted for a drought from some clear spring that I hoped would quench the burning of the thirst I felt within. 15 The woman saith unto him, Sir, give me this water, that I thirst not, neither come hither to draw.

16 Jesus saith unto her, Go, call thy husband, and come hither.

17 The woman answered and said, I have no husband. Jesus said unto her, Thou hast well said, I have no []{#6g89t5Y_Rh2_sEm6hxUWsg954.xhtml_page_110}husband:

18 For thou hast had five husbands; and he whom thou now hast is not thy husband: in that saidst thou truly.

19 The woman saith unto him, Sir, I perceive that thou art a prophet.

20 Our fathers worshipped in this mountain; and ye say, that in Jerusalem is the place where men ought to worship.

The woman says, ["Sir, give me this water,"]{.class_4500} but of course, she still has in mind physical H[2]{.class_4515}O which you take orally (["that I thirst not, neither come hither to draw"]{.class_4500}).

As another "wit" remarked, "She came to the well as thirsty as a St. Bernard in Alabama on the fourth of July."

["Go, call thy husband"]{.class_4500} (vs. 16).

Now isn't that a remarkable thing to insert into this conversation? Where did that come from? Well, ["He knew what was in the man"]{.class_4500} for ["he knew all men"]{.class_4500} (2:24--25).

["I have no husband"]{.class_4500} (vs. 17). This sinner will tell the truth. One dark-skinned "brother" said to his soul brother, "Dey done fined him fo' somethin' or other. De jedge said since he wuz married to three different women folk he was guilty of polygamy." "Shoot, man!" replied the other, "dat ain't polygamy! Anyone knows dat am TRIGINOMETRY!"

["Thou hast had five husbands"]{.class_4500} ([past tense]{.class_4501}: she is going from one to another, "taking turns").[ "And he whom thou now hast is NOT thy husband"]{.class_4500} (vs. 18). 31 In the mean while his disciples prayed him, saying, Master, eat.

32 But he said unto them, I have meat to eat that ye know not of.

33 Therefore said the disciples one to another, Hath any man brought him ought to eat?

34 Jesus saith unto them, My meat is to do the will of him that sent me, and to finish his work.

35 Say not ye, There are yet four months, and then cometh harvest? behold, I say unto you, Lift up your eyes, and look on the fields; for they are white already to harvest.

36 And he that reapeth receiveth wages, and gathereth fruit unto life eternal: that both he that soweth and he that reapeth may rejoice together.

37 And herein is that saying true, One soweth, and another reapeth.

38 I sent you to reap that whereon ye bestowed no labour: other men laboured, and ye are entered into their labours.

The disciples now offer Him some lunch (it is a little after high noon---vs. 6), but He says ["I have meat to eat that ye know not of"]{.class_4500} (vs. 32). Compare this with John 6:57 and see how impossible it would be for Jesus Christ to be [cannibalizing]{.class_4501} His FATHER. That is []{#6g89t5Y_Rh2_sEm6hxUWsg954.xhtml_page_118}what the Catholics did with His statements in John 6:57 if you applied John 6:53--56.

When the disciples start talking to each other, Jesus gives them some relief by explaining what He had just said; He defines "the meat" (vs. 34): it is [doing God's will]{.class_4501} (see Heb. 10:5--9; Matt. 26:39, 42). Note ["to finish His work."]{.class_4500} This work is ["finished"]{.class_4500} in John 19:30).

Verses 35--38 constitute part of a great sermon on [farming.]{.class_4501} They should be read with the following verses in mind (Gal. 6:7--9; 1 Cor. 3:6--8, 15:23, 35--37; 2 Cor. 9:6; Ecc. 11; Mark 4:3--20).

No man called to preach should slight these verses or overlook them. [They describe a Biblical ministry.]{.class_4501} I will only give here the briefest outline of what lies hidden in these verses.

The resurrection is in [three parts]{.class_4501} as all crops that grow are harvested in [three stages:]{.class_4501} firstfruits, harvest, gleanings (see comments on 1 Cor. 15:23 in that Commentary).

Every minister is to SOW, and he is sow [good seed]{.class_4501} (Matt. 13:3,24), which is the [word of God]{.class_4501} (1 Pet. 1:25). He cannot let troubles, trials, testings, problems, or opposition stop him from SOWING (Ecc. 11:4). A true witness will sow the word when he is twelve to twenty years old, thirty to sixty years old, and seventy to eighty years old (Ecc. 11:6). If he wants a fruitful harvest, he must [WEEP over souls]{.class_4501} (Psa. 126:5--6; Luke 19:41; 1 Cor. 2:3; Phil. 3:18). He will spend more time plowing, tilling, and hoeing than he will [planting.]{.class_4501} Sometimes he must pick up rocks or "blast" (or "stump") to prepare a field for crops. He will reap sometimes where he did not [sow,]{.class_4501} and sometimes he will sow [without]{.class_4501} reaping (1 Cor. 3:7--8). Reaping comes at the Judgment Seat of Christ (Gal. 6:8) as well as down here (John 4:36), and it is called "laying HOLD on eternal life" (1 Tim. 6:19).

Want some more? There is more "aplenty."

There will be bugs and animals trying to mess up your vegetable garden (or fruit trees: Song of Sol. 2:15). You are entirely dependent upon the [weather]{.class_4501} for a good crop (see Joel 2; Amos 4:6--11; 1 Kings 17--18), and God [controls the weather]{.class_4501} (Job 37--38; Psa. 147). You have to ["lift up your eyes"]{.class_4500} and [look]{.class_4501} at the field (vs. 35), for ["where there is no vision, the people perish."]{.class_4500}

You are working a field with [other workers]{.class_4501}[]{#6g89t5Y_Rh2_sEm6hxUWsg954.xhtml_page_119} (vss. 37--38), and you will be paid for your work according to what [you do]{.class_4501} (Matt. 20), not what [someone else did]{.class_4501} (see 2 Cor. 10:12--16). You enter into ["their labours,"]{.class_4500} and they enter into yours: hence, "fellow laborer" (Luke 10:2; Matt. 9:37--38; 1 Cor. 3:9; Phil. 4:3; 1 Thess. 3:2).

Sometimes you have to "thin out" the plants so others can grow. Sometimes you have to "snap" off blossoms so other fruit on the same plant will grow bigger; sometimes you have to keep the top of an underground plant covered (carrots and potatoes) so they don't get poisonous; but sometimes you have to pull dirt away from them off the top so the plant will grow bigger (onions); sometimes...!

(Sorry; we are supposed to be writing a Commentary. Too acid, not enough nitrogen, too [much]{.class_4504} nitrogen, bad [early ]{.class_4504}frost, bad [late]{.class_4504} frost, too much [wind, ]{.class_4504}too much [water,]{.class_4504} not [enough]{.class_4504} water, too much lime, not enough [sunlight,]{.class_4504} too high temperatures, too cool at [night,]{.class_4504} not enough [potassium,]{.class_4504} manure too hot, not enough mulch, [blossom rot,]{.class_4504} black rot, end rot, etc.)

You could put together [two dozen good sermons]{.class_4501} on the ministry where every illustration (seven per sermon) could come from [truck farming.]{.class_4501}

["One soweth, and another reapeth"]{.class_4500} (vs. 37). Verse 38 in this light is self explanatory. ["Go work to day in my vineyard!"]{.class_4500} (Matt. 21:28).

46 So Jesus came again into Cana of Galilee, where he made the water wine. And there was a certain nobleman, whose son was sick at Capernaum.

47 When he heard that Jesus was come out of Judaea into Galilee, he went unto him, and besought him that he would come down, and heal his son: for he was at the point of death.

48 Then said Jesus unto him, Except ye see signs and wonders, ye will not believe.

49 The nobleman saith unto him, Sir, come down ere my child die.

50 Jesus saith unto him, Go thy way; thy son liveth. And the man believed the word that Jesus had spoken unto him, and he went his way.

51 And as he was now going down, his servants met him, and told him, saying, Thy son liveth.

Here is a young man who is ["at the point of death"]{.class_4500}[]{#6g89t5Y_Rh2_sEm6hxUWsg954.xhtml_page_123} (vs. 47). If he had died, the Lord could have brought him back to life, which is apparent by Jarius' daughter (Mark 5:35) and the widow of Nain's son (Luke 7:11--12). Isn't it interesting to note that the first human being who ever died on this earth was a YOUNG MAN? Abel was killed before Adam or Eve died of old age.

According to orthodox Jews, Jesus healed all of the people in the gospels by using a secret "charm" which He stole from the Temple. He used this with the "Tetragrammaton" (Jehovah: Yod, He, Vau, He) to do these miracles.

If this traditional, Talmudic fairy tale were true, one wonders why the custodians of the temple (see Matt. 9:18,23; John 3:1, 7:48) didn't allow the scribes, Pharisees, and Sadducees to use the same "charm" at least 100 times a day so no one would stay sick or die in Israel.

Verse 48 is a comment on 1 Corinthians 1:22, 14:22; Exodus 4:30--31; Deuteronomy 34:11; and Luke 24:25, 27, 44.

Verses 49 and 50 are self explanatory. ["THY SON LIVETH"]{.class_4500} is all that has to be said. When the Lord speaks (Gen. 1:3, 6, 9, 11, 14, 20, 24), it comes to pass (Ezek. 7:7--9, 12:25--28).

["The Lord God hath spoken, who can but prophesy?"]{.class_4500} (Amos 3:8).

The nobleman doesn't get home before his servants meet him; they saw the boy recover and were running to bring him the good news.

Jesus, on earth, did not have to be physically present to keep someone from dying, but no one ever died in His presence (including the dying thief). He could heal "at a distance," which is apparent here. He did the same type of healing in the case of the Canaanite-Greek woman (Mark 7:30).

52 Then enquired he of them the hour when he began to amend. And they said unto him, Yesterday at the seventh hour the fever left him.

53 So the father knew that it was at the same hour, in the which Jesus said unto him, Thy son liveth: and himself believed, and his whole house.

54 This is again the second miracle that Jesus did, when he was come out of Judaea into Galilee.

Verse 54 is self explanatory.

# Chapter 5 {.heading_74}

5:1 After this there was a feast of the Jews; and Jesus went up to Jerusalem.

2 Now there is at Jerusalem by the sheep market a pool, which is called in the Hebrew tongue Bethesda, having five porches.

3 In these lay a great multitude of impotent folk, of blind, halt, withered, waiting for the moving of the water.

4 For an angel went down at a certain season into the pool, and troubled the water: whosoever then first after the troubling of the water stepped in was made whole of whatsoever disease he had.

No one can prove that the ["feast" ]{.class_4500}(Gk.: [heortay]{.class_4504}) of verse 1 is another Passover, but it is usually accepted as that and for a good reason. If John 2:23 is the first Passover of Christ's ministry there has to be THREE more to fulfill the requirements of a forty-two month (3½ years) ministry, which certainly was the length of Christ's ministry. He is ["about thirty"]{.class_4500} (Luke 3:23) when John baptized Him, so He is thirty (see Gen. 41:46 and comments in that Commentary) when he begins His ministry (John 1; Luke 4:14). There are about forty-four days between His baptism and His first miracle (see John 1--2).

So if you make this ["feast"]{.class_4500} the second Passover, you will find the third one in John 6:4 and the fourth one in John 12:1 where the Lord Jesus Himself turns out to be the "Passover lamb." The other two feasts (Tabernacles and Dedication) are found in John 7:2 and 10:22, between the Passovers. Christ's ministry is Tabernacle to Tabernacle to Tabernacle and then to Passover: three and one-half years exactly. Therefore, He was born on TABERNACLES (see comments on John 1:14), died on THE PASSOVER, and will return [on TABERNACLES]{.class_4501} (read Zech. 14; Psa. 19:4; Matt. 17:4; Acts 15:16).

Some say that the ["feast"]{.class_4500} is that of Purim (Esther 9:17--18), and that is only one month before the Passover. But the ["great multitude"]{.class_4500} in verses 3 and 13 would indicate one of the three major feasts (Deut. 16:16)---Passover, Pentecost, and Tabernacles.

The sheep market is by the ["sheep gate"]{.class_4500} (see Neh. 3:1). ["Bethesda"]{.class_4500} means "House of Mercy."

What follows (vss. 3--4) is so strongly doubted by the apostate, Laodicean Alexandrians (Lachmann, Hort, Griesbach, Lightfoot, Warfield, Robertson, Nestle, Aland, Metzger, et al.) that they do one of three things: 1. D), and "Bethesda" (C) while "[Aleph]{.class_4501}" reads "Bethzatha."

In verse 3, manuscript "D" has added "paralytics" (Gk.: [paralutixon]{.class_4501}) to the text while Vaticanus, "B," demands that you have no one[ "waiting for the moving of the water" ]{.class_4500}(ditto Sinaiticus and Alexandrinus) in that same verse.

The apostates want [thirty words]{.class_4501} changed or erased (five in vs. 3 and 27 in vs. 4). The Alexandrian corruptions fuss over the passage in this way. "A" retains the last 27 words but erases five from verse 3. The cursive manuscript 157 reads with Sinaiticus and Vaticanus, but cursive 314 follows A and L, while cursive 33 and 124 follow "D."

All other uncials (E, F, G, H, I, J, K, etc., through W) and all other [cursives]{.class_4501}---over 200 of them---follow the [King James ]{.class_4501}text, along with the old Syriac, the Peshitta, the majority of old Latin manuscripts, Tertullian, Chrysostom, Augustine, and Jerome.

5 And a certain man was there, which had an infirmity thirty and eight years.

6 When Jesus saw him lie, and knew that he had been now a long time in that case, he saith unto him, Wilt thou be made whole?

7 The impotent man answered him, Sir, I have no man, when the water is troubled, to put me into the pool: but while I am coming, another steppeth down before me.

8 Jesus saith unto him, Rise, take up thy bed, and walk.

9 And immediately the man was made whole, and took up his bed, and walked: and on the same day was the sabbath.

10 The Jews therefore said unto him that was cured, It is the sabbath day: it is not lawful for thee to carry thy bed.

11 He answered them, He that made me whole, the same said unto me, Take up thy bed, and walk.

12 Then asked they him, What man is that which said unto thee, Take up thy bed, and walk?

13 And he that was healed wist not who it was: for Jesus had conveyed himself away, a multitude being in that []{#6g89t5Y_Rh2_sEm6hxUWsg1155.xhtml_page_128}place.

14 Afterward Jesus findeth him in the temple, and said unto him, Behold, thou art made whole: sin no more, lest a worse thing come unto thee.

15 The man departed, and told the Jews that it was Jesus, which had made him whole.

16 And therefore did the Jews persecute Jesus, and sought to slay him, because he had done these things on the sabbath day.

17 But Jesus answered them, My Father worketh hitherto, and I work.

Verse 5 is self explanatory.

Verse 6 is self explanatory, as is verse 7. The Lord selects the man so the miracle will be obvious; He knew about the impotent man's length of suffering before John wrote the fact down.

The reason why the sick man does not answer the question with "Yes! Yes, I want to be whole!" is because, after thirty-eight years of trying to be[ "made whole,"]{.class_4500} anyone is going to be so "down in the mouth" ("depressed" is the modern term) that they would have given up all hope of being ["made whole"]{.class_4500}; so the man simply explains WHY he is not whole.

["Rise, take up thy bed and walk"]{.class_4500} (cf. Mark 2:11--12). ["The man was made whole, and took up his bed, and walked"]{.class_4500} (vs. 9). Notice this is a case like Mark 3:5 where the sick man is absolutely unable to obey the command---but obeys it. When Jesus Christ speaks, it was like Paul's preaching:[ "My speech and my preaching was not with enticing words of man's wisdom, but in DEMONSTRATION OF THE SPIRIT AND POWER"]{.class_4500} (1 Cor. 2:4).

Verse 10 needs no exposition, especially if you had read Matthew 12:2 and Mark 3:1--6 before reading John. Hypocrites are always more concerned about the ONE ceremonial law in the Ten Commandments (Exod. 20:8--10) than the nine MORAL laws. It was never a ["sign"]{.class_4500} to []{#6g89t5Y_Rh2_sEm6hxUWsg1155.xhtml_page_129}4,000,000,000 Gentiles on the earth the last six centuries; nor is it now a ["sign"]{.class_4500} for any German, American, Frenchman, Italian, Austrian, Chinaman, Japanese, Russian, Irishman, Arabian, Mexican, South African, South American, Cuban, Scotchman, Swede, Indian, Filipino, or Polak alive anywhere on earth today, SAVED or LOST (see [Why I Am Not A Seventh-day Adventist,]{.class_4501} 1997).

Verses 11 and 12 need no exposition.

["Wist not"]{.class_4500} (vs. 13) is the old English for "knew," as in Exodus and other places. It is related to the old Germanic ["Wissen,"]{.class_4501} as in "Ich weiss."

Verse 14 intimates that this man's particular infirmity was sin-connected; somewhere, like his "buddies" in Romans 1:27 had done, he had received a "just recompense" in his body because of sin. This was NOT the blind man's case in John 9:1--3, which see. Verses 13 and 14 are self explanatory. The healed man was not disobeying any order from the Lord. He had been given no order like the one Christ gave in Mark 1:40--44. He simply wanted to give them the information for which they asked.

["My Father worketh hitherto, and I work"]{.class_4500} is hardly any way to defend yourself having been accused of a felony that merits capital punishment (see John 10:30--33), but that is how Christ handled it. Christ's ["Father"]{.class_4500} didn't quit working every Sabbath day after Genesis 2. Thus it is that Jesus, here, is again associating Himself with Deity: He is claiming to be more than just a Jewish prophet, for all Jewish males had to rest on the Sabbath (see Num. 15:32 and Exod. 16:29--30).

The words under question begin with verse 3, ["waiting for the moving\..."]{.class_4500} and end in verse 4 with ["whatsoever disease he had."]{.class_4500} That is the casting away of [forty-three words]{.class_4501} in the English text. 24 Verily, verily, I say unto you, He that heareth my word, and believeth on him that sent me, hath everlasting life, and shall not come into condemnation; but is passed from death unto life.

25 Verily, verily, I say unto you, The hour is coming, and now is, when the dead shall hear the voice of the Son of God: and they that hear shall live.

26 For as the Father hath life in himself; so hath he given to the Son to have life in himself;

27 And hath given him authority to execute judgment also, because he is the Son of man.

28 Marvel not at this: for the hour is coming, in the which all that are in the graves shall hear his voice,

29 And shall come forth; they that have done good, unto the resurrection of life; and they that have done evil, unto the resurrection of damnation.

30 I can of mine own self do nothing: as I hear, I judge: and my judgment is just; because I seek not mine own will, but the will of the Father which hath sent me.

31 If I bear witness of myself, my witness is not true.

# Chapter 6 {.heading_74}

6:1 After these things Jesus went over the sea of Galilee, which is the sea of Tiberias.

2 And a great multitude followed him, because they saw his miracles which he did on them that were diseased.

3 And Jesus went up into a mountain, and there he sat with his disciples.

4 And the passover, a feast of the Jews, was nigh.

These things take place around the time of the third Passover, which would be two and one-half years into Jesus' ministry (vs. 4). Two names are given here for the Sea of Galilee, the second one being ["Tiberias."]{.class_4500} The same body of water is also referred to as ["Chinneroth"]{.class_4500} (Josh 11:2).

Verses 2 and 3 are self explanatory.

5 When Jesus then lifted up his eyes, and saw a great company come unto him, he saith unto Philip, Whence shall we buy bread, that these may eat?

6 And this he said to prove him: for he himself knew what he would do.

7 Philip answered him, Two hundred pennyworth of bread is not sufficient for them, that every one of them may take a little.

8 One of his disciples, Andrew, Simon Peter's brother, saith unto him,

9 There is a lad here, which hath five barley loaves, and two small fishes: but what are they among so many?

10 And Jesus said, Make the men sit down. Now there was much grass in the place. So the men sat down, in number about five thousand.

This is another account of the famous "feeding of the five thousand" which was also recorded by Matthew (Matt. 14), Mark (Mark 6), and Luke (Luke 9).

And as surely as there is a supernatural hand guiding the Apostle John, his account occurs right after a statement on the unbelief of []{#6g89t5Y_Rh2_sEm6hxUWsg1395.xhtml_page_153}Bible scholars and religious leaders. You see "the feeding of the 5,000" is mentioned more times in the gospels than the Virgin Birth or the Ascension of Christ back to Glory. Luke omits the wise men from the East; Matthew omits the shepherds in the fields; Mark omits Gabriel talking with Mary; John omits the prodigal son and the rich man and Lazarus; Mark says nothing about the marriage supper or the wise and foolish virgins (nor does John). Only one account of the New Birth is recorded (Matthew, Mark, and Luke omit it); only one account contains the real "Lord's Prayer" (John 17)---Matthew, Mark, and Luke omit it. Neither Matthew nor Luke give you the details of the healing of the maniac of Gadara (Mark 5) and so forth; but\...!

But [ALL FOUR EVANGELISTS]{.class_4501} record a one-time feeding of 5,000 Jews in the wilderness (see comments on John 21:15).

Is that a "just balance"? Doesn't that look like one more of those "tricky things" the Lord delights in? Like, say for example, devoting twelve chapters to the construction of an old gray tent (Exod. 25--31, 35--40) and allotting only ONE chapter to the creation of the universe? How is that for a[ "false balance"]{.class_4500}?

What is so important about feeding some people in[ "a desert place" ]{.class_4500}(Luke 9:12) that makes it more important than the New Birth, the Virgin Birth of Christ, the Lord's Prayer, and the Ascension of our Lord? Any reply from over 300 highly-educated, highly-respected, greatly-honored, dedicated, qualified, recognized Christian scholars since 1800? Not a peep. Not a cough in a carload. Not one in 2,000 years.

Good! Now we can dump the whole crew with a clear conscience and actually COMMENT on the situation.

1\. The most important thing in the Book where the major emphasis is always found to be, is never on the New Birth, the Deity of Christ, the Virgin Birth, the Crucifixion, the plan of salvation, the sacraments, the Church, or anything any Christian scholar might suggest. It is on THE SECOND COMING OF JESUS CHRIST. For every verse on the seven things listed above (combined) there are TEN on the Second Advent.

2\. Being totally off balance, spiritually, and being completely lopsided, mentally, every corrector of the [King James']{.class_4501} text (for 390 []{#6g89t5Y_Rh2_sEm6hxUWsg1395.xhtml_page_154}years) simply rejected thirty verses that explain why "the feeding of the 5,000" is mentioned four times.

3\. The verses are as follows: Psalm 78:19--25; Hosea 2:14--18; Luke 4:4; Revelation 12:6, 14; Exodus 16:14--31; Psalm 74:14; and Micah 6:14.

The occurrence prefigures what will take place in the Tribulation, up beyond A.D. 2004.

["How can ye believe, which receive honour one of another, and seek not the honour that cometh from God only?"]{.class_4500} You can't. Neither could 320 Bible scholars who preceded you.

There are more than 100 verses in the Old Testament, like this passage, where an incident applies DOCTRINALLY to the Tribulation; but they are all carefully disguised in the [King James]{.class_4501} text so that if a man messes with that text [he loses the "key" to interpreting a dozen other verses.]{.class_4501} (For more than fifty examples see [The Bible Believer's Commentary on Proverbs,]{.class_4501} 1972, and [The Bible Believer's Commentary on First and Second Peter,]{.class_4501} 2004.)

11 And Jesus took the loaves; and when he had given thanks, he distributed to the disciples, and the disciples to them that were set down; and likewise of the fishes as much as they would.

12 When they were filled, he said unto his disciples, Gather up the fragments that remain, that nothing be lost.

13 Therefore they gathered them together, and filled twelve baskets with the fragments of the five barley loaves, which remained over and above unto them that had eaten.

14 Then those men, when they had seen the miracle that Jesus did, said, This is of a truth that prophet that should come into the world.

Most of the passage down to verse 14 is self explanatory. Notice that our Lord is asking questions again---see Luke 24:19---when He already knows the answer, ["Whence shall we BUY bread\...?"]{.class_4500}

Philip's unbelief spreads to Andrew (vs. 9), but who could blame either of them? The Lord doesn't rebuke either one of them. He just says "Get ready to eat" (["Make the men sit down,"]{.class_4500} vs. 10).

Verse 11 is described in Matthew 14 but adds that Jesus []{#6g89t5Y_Rh2_sEm6hxUWsg1395.xhtml_page_155}["brake"]{.class_4500} the bread, as does Mark's gospel (Mark 6:41). \[For the devotional and spiritual lessons in the feeding see the comments on Matthew 14 in that Commentary, 1970.\]

To really appreciate the miracle, you have to put yourselves into the shoes (or sandals) of one of the "waiters" (the apostles) who start out to feed over 5,000 people with WHAT? Did each disciple get less than half of one fish---one SMALL fish (vs. 9)? How would you like it for your Lord to give you one-quarter of one small fish and less than half a loaf of bread and then tell you to pass it out to 416 people?

Why, by the time you had served two people you would have been out of supplies! What happens then? Well, bless your soul, the only thing that could have happened would be for more fishes and more loaves to suddenly appear IN YOUR HANDS WHILE YOU WERE SERVING---as quickly as you got rid of them! Now fancy [that]{.class_4501} if you can! And 5,000 eyewitnesses SAW IT.

Now, how much trouble do you think the Lord Jesus would have had to go through in Luke 4 to feed just [Himself?!]{.class_4501}

Here the "supply" stopped with the "demand." Can you imagine the reaction of that young boy's [mother]{.class_4501} when he got home and showed her twelve baskets of barley loaves? Can you imagine what she must have thought? Why, she could have sworn he stole them. (We can suppose she got converted, although it would have a "tall story" to believe if you hadn't seen it.) Those who saw it said, ["This is of a truth that prophet that should come into the world"]{.class_4500} (vs. 14).

See ["THAT PROPHET"]{.class_4500}? THAT was the ["that prophet"]{.class_4500} of John 1:21---the prophet "like unto Moses" (Acts 3:22). Do you know why they reached that conclusion? Because it was MOSES who fed his people miraculously in the wilderness like [he ]{.class_4501}(Rev. 12) [will do again after the Rapture]{.class_4501} (see comments on Rev. 12 in that Commentary). They will be fed with MANNA. All Moslems swear ["that prophet"]{.class_4500} was Mohammed! [When did he produce ANY bread in ANY wilderness?]{.class_4501}

15 When Jesus therefore perceived that they would come and take him by force, to make him a king, he []{#6g89t5Y_Rh2_sEm6hxUWsg1395.xhtml_page_156}departed again into a mountain himself alone.

16 And when even was now come, his disciples went down unto the sea,

17 And entered into a ship, and went over the sea toward Capernaum. And it was now dark, and Jesus was not come to them.

18 And the sea arose by reason of a great wind that blew.

19 So when they had rowed about five and twenty or thirty furlongs, they see Jesus walking on the sea, and drawing nigh unto the ship: and they were afraid.

20 But he saith unto them, It is I; be not afraid.

21 Then they willingly received him into the ship: and immediately the ship was at the land whither they went.

We have commented on Christ's actions from time to time (like Luke 4:30; Mark 3:6--7, 1:44) where He avoids situations that would "rush" events. In this case, He knows that the only ["king"]{.class_4500} they are going to make Him is "king" of thieves (Matt. 26:55). At His first coming, He is to be crowned with THORNS (Isa. 53:7--8). His move, here, typifies the CHURCH AGE, as in Matthew 14:22--25, where the ["watch"]{.class_4500} in which Jesus Christ will return is said to be the ["FOURTH WATCH." ]{.class_4500}This is the ["morning" ]{.class_4500}watch in which the ["master of the house"]{.class_4500} returns (see Mark 13:35).

Naturally, [all]{.class_4501} of the commentators (1800--2000) miss the import of [BOTH passages.]{.class_4501} They were so busy riding their eternal "hobby horse" ("THE" Greek text and the "original autographs"), they overlooked the [main theme]{.class_4501} of the Bible [again]{.class_4501} (see p. 192), SOP in the Alexandrian Cult.

This present dispensation is likened to "the old ship of Zion" passing through "stormy waters" while its ["captain"]{.class_4500} (Heb. 2:10) is "upstairs" (Heb. 12:22) on ["Mt. Zion"]{.class_4500} (vs. 15): the one described in Hebrews 12:23.

Naturally, all 320 Bible critics miss [all]{.class_4501} of the passages and [all ]{.class_4501}of the cross references. In this present dispensation (the Church Age, about to end shortly!), the Lord proves His Deity (vs. 21), proves that "positive thinking" about "self" is insufficient (Matt. 14:31), proves that He can control the elements (Matt. 14:33; Rom. 8:28), []{#6g89t5Y_Rh2_sEm6hxUWsg1395.xhtml_page_157}and gives the believer absolute assurance of eternal salvation (vs. 21).

22 The day following, when the people which stood on the other side of the sea saw that there was none other boat there, save that one whereinto his disciples were entered, and that Jesus went not with his disciples into the boat, but that his disciples were gone away alone;

23 (Howbeit there came other boats from Tiberias nigh unto the place where they did eat bread, after that the Lord had given thanks:)

24 When the people therefore saw that Jesus was not there, neither his disciples, they also took shipping, and came to Capernaum, seeking for Jesus.

25 And when they had found him on the other side of the sea, they said unto him, Rabbi, when camest thou hither?

26 Jesus answered them and said, Verily, verily, I say unto you, Ye seek me, not because ye saw the miracles, but because ye did eat of the loaves, and were filled.

27 Labour not for the meat which perisheth, but for that meat which endureth unto everlasting life, which the Son of man shall give unto you: for him hath God the Father sealed.

When I was passing my language tests at Bob Jones University for an earned Ph.D., it was this portion of Scripture in a Greek New Testament (Nestle's, naturally!) I had to translate: John 6:22--24, along with Acts 25:11--16. In those days (1935--1979), a Ph.D. candidate had to master three foreign languages well enough to take any passage in the Bible and translate it at sight, without the aid of a dictionary or lexicon. I had to translate a Hebrew passage, a Greek passage, and a French passage. Marshall Neal and Barton Payne (who were on the [NIV]{.class_4504} committee) selected two passages, and this one was a "dilly" for its Greek construction: it was John 6:22--24).

When you get the mess unraveled, you find that the people on the ["other side of the sea"]{.class_4500} are in Tiberias on the east bank of Galilee. They saw the disciples get into the same boat they had []{#6g89t5Y_Rh2_sEm6hxUWsg1395.xhtml_page_158}arrived in, "minus" the Lord Jesus. They had seen them get into that boat and set sail for Capernaum before the storm. The Lord was not there because He had gone up into a mountain on the east side of the lake (see Matt. 14:22, ["to go BEFORE HIM"]{.class_4500}).

# Chapter 7 {.heading_74}

7:1 After these things Jesus walked in Galilee: for he would not walk in Jewry, because the Jews sought to kill him.

2 Now the Jews' feast of tabernacles was at hand.

3 His brethren therefore said unto him, Depart hence, and go into Judaea, that thy disciples also may see the works that thou doest.

4 For there is no man that doeth any thing in secret, and he himself seeketh to be known openly. If thou do these things, shew thyself to the world.

5 For neither did his brethren believe in him.

6 Then Jesus said unto them, My time is not yet come: but your time is alway ready.

7 The world cannot hate you; but me it hateth, because I testify of it, that the works thereof are evil.

8 Go ye up unto this feast: I go not up yet unto this feast; for my time is not yet full come.

The word ["Jewry"]{.class_4500} is another word for "Judea." John is always laying the emphasis on the JEWS (Judean Jews) being Christ's enemies. This "bait," for anti-Semitics to grab, has been used by Gentiles for centuries. Most of the time John will use simply ["JEWS"]{.class_4500} for Pharisees, Sadducees, and scribes, as he does here. The scholars keep forgetting that John is writing twenty years [AFTER]{.class_4501} the destruction of Jerusalem.

The ["feast of tabernacles"]{.class_4500} dates---without any commentator being aware of it---the First and Second Advents (see comments on Matt. 17:1--7 in that Commentary). All 320 of them (for 200 years) miss all the cross references, since they believed in correcting every word that would tie them together (Psa. 19:4--5; Zech. 14:16--17,19; Matt. 17; Luke 9:33; 1 Kings 8:2--4; Acts 15:16; Lev. 23). ["Tabernacles,"]{.class_4500} as well as ["trumpets"]{.class_4500} and the day of ["atonement,"]{.class_4500} all occur in the seventh month (Sept.--Oct.), as well as Rosh Hoshanna: the New Year.

Thus the Lord has seven "landmarks" to fix His Son's First and Second Advents, for the Passover commemorates His []{#6g89t5Y_Rh2_sEm6hxUWsg1670.xhtml_page_185}["death till he come"]{.class_4500} (1 Cor. 11:26).

The ["brethren"]{.class_4500} are the members of His family (see Mark 6 and Psa. 69). ["For neither did his brethren believe in him."]{.class_4500} That is Psalm 69:8 illustrated.

["My time is not yet come, but your time is always ready"]{.class_4500} (vs. 6). The idea is that His entire life is controlled by His Father, and their lives are not. [He]{.class_4501} must desist from certain things, abstain from certain things, deal with certain things, and avoid certain things, because it is His Father's WILL that is involved (see 7:17, 6:40, 5:30).

His ["brethren"]{.class_4500} can shift about as they please, so there is nothing preventing them from going to the feast. (All the males in Palestine, by the way, were required to attend the feast (see Deut. 16:16 and Exod. 23:17).

["Me it hateth, because I testify of it, that the works thereof are EVIL"]{.class_4500} (vs. 7). So Paul refers to it simply as ["this present EVIL world"]{.class_4500} (Gal. 1:4).

Don't ever forget those verses, along with Luke 16:15 and 1 John 2:15--17.

19 Did not Moses give you the law, and yet none of you keepeth the law? Why go ye about to kill me?

20 The people answered and said, Thou hast a devil: who goeth about to kill thee?

21 Jesus answered and said unto them, I have done one work, and ye all marvel.

22 Moses therefore gave unto you circumcision; (not because it is of Moses, but of the fathers;) and ye on the sabbath day circumcise a man.

23 If a man on the sabbath day receive circumcision, that the law of Moses should not be broken; are ye angry at []{#6g89t5Y_Rh2_sEm6hxUWsg1670.xhtml_page_192}me, because I have made a man every whit whole on the sabbath day?

25 Then said some of them of Jerusalem, Is not this he, whom they seek to kill?

27 Howbeit we know this man whence he is: but when Christ cometh, no man knoweth whence he is.

30 Then they sought to take him: but no man laid hands on him, because his hour was not yet come.

31 And many of the people believed on him, and said, When Christ cometh, will he do more miracles than these which this man hath done?
