To tell the truth, Moses rejected circumcision for his firstborn son (Exod. 2:22), and Zipporah had to perform it for him (Exod. 4:24--25). ["Of the fathers"]{.class_4500} would be Abraham, Isaac, Jacob, et al.; not Moses.[ "Whit"]{.class_4500} is the old English for "bit."

["Judge not according to the appearance"]{.class_4500} because God doesn't judge that way; see 1 Samuel 16:7. ["Judge not according to the appearance"]{.class_4500} because His Son is not going to judge that way when He shows up, according to Isaiah 42:19--20 (see 1 Kings 9:4).

Verse 25 is self explanatory.

Verses 26--27: ["He speaketh boldly"]{.class_4500} as Paul (Eph. 6:20) and Peter and John (Acts 4:13) and Isaiah, Jeremiah, Michaiah, Amos, et al. ["Do the rulers KNOW\...?"]{.class_4500} Well, probably not. Religious "rulers" are usually blind as a bat when it comes to Biblical prophecy. No dumber amateurs ever lived than Philip Schaff (head of the [ASV]{.class_4501} committee), Dr. A. T. Robertson (Greek teacher at the Southern Baptist Seminary in Louisville), and Machen, Warfield, and []{#6g89t5Y_Rh2_sEm6hxUWsg1670.xhtml_page_194}the Hodges from Princeton, or the Catholic Popes when it came to understanding ["the HOLY SCRIPTURES."]{.class_4500}

Note that, by far, the greatest enemy that God and Christ have on this earth are sinners who depend upon religious "RULERS" to give them the truth (["Have any of the RULERS\...believed on him,"]{.class_4500} vs. 48).

How could the Lord Jesus be telling the truth if the religious RULERS (take for example, Mohammed \[with his ulamas and imams\], the Greek Orthodox "patriarchs," the Hebrew and Greek scholars in Christian universities or the priests, bishops, and archbishops in Catholic countries \[Mexico, Spain, Austria, Italy, France, Brazil, Honduras, Cuba, Nicaragua, Croatia, Poland, etc.\] who are Catholics be wrong? How could all of their [rulers ]{.class_4501}who are Catholics be wrong? Why, they would have to be right!

So far they have been [wrong]{.class_4501} on more than [5,000 verses]{.class_4501} in the Bible. No Roman Catholic anywhere on earth has ever had a Biblical understanding of the New Birth, the local church, Mary's nature or her family, the Biblical offices of pastor and "elder," the Rapture, the restoration of Israel, eternal security, the Biblical form of water baptism, the Judgment Seat of Christ, the Great Tribulation, the Millennium, what constitutes a Biblical "marriage," the Lord's Supper, or even how to enter Christ's "body."

There went 3,000 verses slap out of the window.

That is the mental level of the "Holy See," ruled by the "Holy Father." He is about as "holy" and about as spiritual as Stalin, Lenin, or Adolph Hitler.

["Howbeit we know this man whence he is"]{.class_4500} (vs. 27). You do on earth, for He was on the Roman roll call for taxes as being born in Bethlehem, and His hometown was given as Nazareth. But look out for the ["whence."]{.class_4500} That is what put Pilate into a psychotic mess (John 19:9) that he never got out of. It caused him to have an innocent man executed, a man whom he had just declared to be faultless---[THREE TIMES]{.class_4501} (John 18--19).

Every "ruler" in Jerusalem knew the place of the Messiah's birth; they gave it to Herod (Matt. 2:6).

Don't tell me that they didn't check Joseph and Mary's name in the register when ["all the world ]{.class_4500}\[was\][ taxed"]{.class_4500} (Luke 2:1). That is why Joseph took Mary to [Bethlehem]{.class_4501}[]{#6g89t5Y_Rh2_sEm6hxUWsg1670.xhtml_page_195} (Luke 2:3).

(There are hypocrites and then there are hypocrites.)

Verses 28--29: ["Ye both KNOW me, and ye KNOW whence I am"]{.class_4500} (vs. 28). Compare that with John 8:42 and John 5:38 if you want an alibi to reject the Bible and say it is full of "contradictions." Go on break your fool neck on it. I'll guarantee more than 4,000,000 highly-educated jackasses have [before]{.class_4501} you were born. I said "jackasses" because they were to have their necks BROKEN (Exod. 13:13) as when a hanged man (Deut. 21:22--23) has his neck broken (Esther 7:9).

["He that sent me is true, whom ye know not"]{.class_4500} (vs. 28). This is Christ's constant "theme" (see John 6:38).

["But I know him"]{.class_4500} (vs. 29). And so ["WE"]{.class_4500} know Him (1 John 5:19--20), and that is why ["WE"]{.class_4500} know we are saved [permanently]{.class_4501} (1 John 5:9--13). By ["WE"]{.class_4500} here, I am making no reference to 900,000,000 Christians who don't know for sure where they are going when they die. I am making reference to Bible-believing Christians who ignore what the ["rulers"]{.class_4500} (see above) think about Hebrews 3, 6, 10; Matthew 25; Revelation 12,14, 22; Matthew 24:13; James 2:24; 2 Peter 2:22; and John 15:1--6).

Verse 30 is self explanatory. We have already commented on verse 31 above.

32 The Pharisees heard that the people murmured such things concerning him; and the Pharisees and the chief priests sent officers to take him.

33 Then said Jesus unto them, Yet a little while am I with you, and then I go unto him that sent me.

34 Ye shall seek me, and shall not find me: and where I am, thither ye cannot come.

35 Then said the Jews among themselves, Whither will he go, that we shall not find him? will he go unto the dispersed among the Gentiles, and teach the Gentiles?

36 What manner of saying is this that he said, Ye shall seek me, and shall not find me: and where I am, thither ye cannot come?

Verse 32 is self explanatory. In verses 33 and 34, the Lord Jesus []{#6g89t5Y_Rh2_sEm6hxUWsg1670.xhtml_page_196}announces His departure from this world (Acts 1) ahead of time. To this day, all "space-age scientists" and "atomic-age astronauts" are still trying to make lies out of His speech in verse 34. In direct violation of Romans 10:6--7, all "modern men" desire to make a liar out of Jesus Christ. After 2,000 years they haven't learned one blessed thing about ["THE TRUTH"]{.class_4500} (2 Tim. 3:7).

It was their ["father"]{.class_4500} who kept mankind busy for 2,000 years (Isa. 14:13), so the Christians among them deified Satan in that passage (Isa. 14:12---[NIV]{.class_4501}). You see, it has always been man's eternal ambition since Genesis 11:1--9 to build a tower ["whose top may reach unto heaven."]{.class_4500} Phallic symbols bear universal testimony to man's belief in [himself]{.class_4501} as the [author]{.class_4501} of all creation and [physical life;]{.class_4501} hence, he (in his own mind) should be where God is (Matt. 5:34--35; Isa. 57:15, 14:12--15). He winds up where volcanic lava is (Isa. 30:33), [UNDER the earth]{.class_4501} (Matt. 12:40; Jonah 2; Deut. 32:33). The first man is of the earth: EARTHY (1 Cor. 15:47).

A little later, the Lord Jesus adds to this statement that not only will these religious rulers be unable to come where He is, they will DIE "in their sins" [where they are]{.class_4501} (John 8:21,24). There are in America, today, more than 10,000,000 college graduates who will deny that statement in less than five seconds. Which means nothing more than a pig or a dog denying that he (or she; 2 Pet. 2:22) is a pig or a dog.

The crowd guesses that perhaps, by His declaration (vs. 34), Jesus will go to the Jews of the dispersion. [Half right.]{.class_4501} He eventually does go to them to teach them (Rom. 15:16; 2 Tim. 1:11), but He goes in the person of the Apostle Paul.

["To whom ye forgive any thing, I forgive also: for if I forgave any thing, to whom I forgave it, for your sakes FORGAVE I IT IN THE PERSON OF CHRIST"]{.class_4500} (2 Cor. 2:10).

["Now then we are ambassadors for Christ, as though God did beseech you by us: we pray you IN CHRIST'S STEAD, be ye reconciled to God"]{.class_4500} (2 Cor. 5:20).

["Who now rejoice in my sufferings for you, and fill up that which is behind of the afflictions of Christ IN MY FLESH for his body's sake, which is the church"]{.class_4500} (Col. 1:24).

And there went the whole Roman Catholic system out the window again. Peter was not called to teach [any]{.class_4501} Roman []{#6g89t5Y_Rh2_sEm6hxUWsg1670.xhtml_page_197}[anything. ]{.class_4501}He never fed a Roman ["sheep"]{.class_4500} (John 21:16) after he fed Cornelius and several Roman proselytes who came to Jerusalem again (Acts 2:10).

7:37 In the last day, that great day of the feast, Jesus stood and cried, saying, If any man thirst, let him come unto me, and drink.

38 He that believeth on me, as the scripture hath said, out of his belly shall flow rivers of living water.

39 (But this spake he of the Spirit, which they that believe on him should receive: for the Holy Ghost was not yet given; because that Jesus was not yet glorified.)

["In the last day, that great day of the feast\...."]{.class_4500} This will be the [Feast of Tabernacles]{.class_4501} (see notes under 7:2). Jesus gives the crowd the same proposition that He gave to the adulteress at the well (John 4). Again He offers sinners (vs. 38) the ["LIVING WATER"]{.class_4500} (see John 4:10--14). He is prophesying a [dual prophesy:]{.class_4501} the first application is to John 20:22 where the ["eleven"]{.class_4500} receive the Holy Spirit, without entering Christ's ["body"]{.class_4500} (1 Cor. 12:13; Gal. 3:27); the second application is to Pentecost, where a [simultaneous]{.class_4501} giving, receiving, anointing, baptism, and filling all take place (Luke 24; Acts 2) at one time.

This explains why every Charismatic and Pentecostal went "bonkers" when he got to Acts 2 (along with all Catholics and Campbellites on Acts 2:38). The "Baptist Briders" followed them by interpreting 1 Cor. 12:13 in the light of Acts 2:38. That would make [three denominational "washouts"]{.class_4501} in a row. The Baptists confused the baptism of 1 Corinthians with Acts 2:38, because in Acts 2:38, the "local church" WAS the Body of Christ, at that time. It was never the BODY of Christ again [for the next 1,970 years]{.class_4501} (see comments on Acts 1 and 2 in that Commentary, 1974).

Note that the Holy Spirit is identical with the Holy Ghost (vs. 39), and both are identical to ["THE COMFORTER"]{.class_4500} (John 14:16) and ["THE SPIRIT OF TRUTH"]{.class_4500} (John 16:13) and ["THE LORD"]{.class_4500} (2 Cor. 3:17).

And there went the whole Pentecostal-Holiness-Charismatic-[]{#6g89t5Y_Rh2_sEm6hxUWsg1670.xhtml_page_198}Promise Keepers-ecumenical mess down the tube, and Islam with them.

Acts 2 threw them off the highway; at least it has since A.D. 1900.

Obviously Christ's descriptions in verse 38 and 39 are [figurative]{.class_4501}---exactly like they were in John 6:53, which see. No Christian walks around with "rivers" coming out of his "belly" and spraying the ground in front of him, but the [figure]{.class_4501} is accurate. Water is a type of the Holy Spirit, but (!) it is never to be confused with the Holy Spirit as all Catholics and Campbellites do when they hit John 3:5, and all Baptist Briders do when they mangle 1 Corinthians 12:13.

For a marvelous revelation, run back to Genesis 1 where ["WATER"]{.class_4500} and the ["SPIRIT"]{.class_4500} first show up. The [three essentials to human life]{.class_4501} are: air, water, food. That is how they occur in Genesis 1. Verse 2: the Spirit of God comes first (see Gen. 2:2; Ezek. 37). Then comes the water (vs. 2), and then the food shows up (vss. 11--12).

The "thirst" in verse 37 is a thirst for [righteousness]{.class_4501} (Matt. 5:6). Since the [spiritual]{.class_4501} kingdom is ["righteousness, and peace, and joy in the HOLY GHOST" ]{.class_4500}(Rom. 14:17), all of Christ's audiences were told ["Seek ye FIRST the kingdom of God]{.class_4500} \[Spirit\][, and all these things]{.class_4500} (food and clothing) [shall be added to you"]{.class_4500} (Matt. 6:33).

It is the indwelling Holy Spirit in the body of the believer (["which they that BELIEVE on him should receive,"]{.class_4500} vs. 39) who satisfies the thirst for ["RIGHTEOUSNESS" ]{.class_4500}(first: see Heb. 1:9, 7:2); ["PEACE, AND JOY"]{.class_4500} (Rom. 14:17).

SATISFIED

All my life long I had panted

For a draught, from some clear spring,

That I hoped would quench the burning

Of the thirst I felt within.

Well of water, ever springing,

Bread of life so rich and free,

Untold wealth that never faileth,

My Redeemer is to me.

Chorus:

Hallelujah! I have found Him

Whom my soul so long has craved!

Jesus satisfies my longings---

Thru His blood I now am saved.

40 Many of the people therefore, when they heard this saying, said, Of a truth this is the Prophet.

41 Others said, This is the Christ. But some said, Shall Christ come out of Galilee?

42 Hath not the scripture said, That Christ cometh of the seed of David, and out of the town of Bethlehem, where David was?

43 So there was a division among the people because of him.

44 And some of them would have taken him; but no man laid hands on him.

The prophet referred to in verse 40 is the one mentioned in John 1:21 and located in Acts 3:22.

Verse 42 shows they knew [exactly]{.class_4501} from where the Messiah was to come. Why did they say verse 27? ["Others" ]{.class_4500}certainly did know why ["some"]{.class_4500} professed not to know where He was from. The rulers and the Pharisees had messed up on Galilee (see p. 257). ["So there was a division among the people because of Him"]{.class_4500} (vs. 43). Thus it was (Luke 12:51), and thus it ever shall be (Matt. 10:34--36). Jesus Christ did [not]{.class_4501} come to bring mankind into one big global family; He came not to ["send PEACE]{.class_4500} (positive)[, but a SWORD"]{.class_4500} (negative), in spite of the Catholic Church ([and the Moslems)]{.class_4501} lying about it since they were founded (about A.D. 500 and A.D. 600).

The ["oneness"]{.class_4501} Christ speaks of in John 17 is the "oneness" that Paul emphasized in His epistles (Eph. 4:3--5; Gal. 6:1--2; 1 Cor. 12:6--20). It is never the "oneness" preached by Mohammed (A.D. 600) or the Popes (A.D. 500--2000) or the UN or the League of Nations or the European Economic Community or the Hansetic League or the Holy Roman Empire or the Fifth Kingdom Monarchists or the "Promise Keepers" [or any news media outlet in the last 500 years.]{.class_4501}

God's eternal purpose with "man" since the tower of Babel (Gen. []{#6g89t5Y_Rh2_sEm6hxUWsg1670.xhtml_page_200}11) is to keep the races and nations SEGREGATED, DIVIDED, and SEPARATED (Deut. 32:8--9; Acts 17:26--27); [contrary]{.class_4501} to the dreams, plans, wishes, aspirations, and attempts of [every]{.class_4501} major political leader since Babel (Sennacherib, Pharaoh, Nebuchadnezzar, Alexander the Great, Tamerlane, the Caesars, all Popes, most European kings, Attila, Genghis Khan, Mohammed, FDR, Adolph Hitler \[Pan Germany\], Napoleon \[Pan France\], and the UN) to make a liar out of the Holy Spirit.

The only "UNITY" found anywhere in 7,957 verses in the New Testament's twenty-seven books is a unity of TWICE BORN (see John 3:3--5), Bible-believing (see Luke 24:25; Acts 24:14) followers of the Lord Jesus Christ. Not even when the whole world "gets together," in the Millennium, and every knee bends and every head bows to Jesus Christ is there any real unity. What unity there is is not [permanent.]{.class_4501} (Read comments on Rev. 20:9--10 in that Commentary.)

45 Then came the officers to the chief priests and Pharisees; and they said unto them, Why have ye not brought him?

46 The officers answered, Never man spake like this man.

47 Then answered them the Pharisees, Are ye also deceived?

48 Have any of the rulers or of the Pharisees believed on him?

49 But this people who knoweth not the law are cursed.

50 Nicodemus saith unto them, (he that came to Jesus by night, being one of them,)

51 Doth our law judge any man, before it hear him, and know what he doeth?

52 They answered and said unto him, Art thou also of Galilee? Search, and look: for out of Galilee ariseth no prophet.

53 And every man went unto his own house.

The "temple officers" (in any Church-State religion) are religious leaders who can use [armed troops]{.class_4501} to enforce their "religion." These dupes return to their "masters" (["the chief priests and Pharisees"]{.class_4500}[]{#6g89t5Y_Rh2_sEm6hxUWsg1670.xhtml_page_201}: the ones urging Christ's death in Mark 15:3, 10 and Luke 23:23) without the prisoner they were told to take. When asked "Why have ye not brought him," the officers give posterity a "dying truth" that is as fixed today as it was when spoken here in A.D. 32.

["Never man spake like this man"]{.class_4500} (vs. 46).

[Absolute truth;]{.class_4501} nothing "relative" about it.

Not one religious leader who walked on this earth in six millennia claimed to be [sinless]{.class_4501} in public. The ["only begotten Son,"]{.class_4500} that God begat, [proved it]{.class_4501} by doing what not one religious leader ever did [before]{.class_4501} He showed up or [after]{.class_4501} He left. Darwin's "evolution" came to a "screeching halt" when Jesus Christ showed up. Stepping from Jesus Christ in A.D. 33 [down]{.class_4501} and backwards to Mohammed in A.D. 590 is like taking a step off the Empire State Building from the fortieth floor.

1\. He spoke with [Majestic Authority:]{.class_4501} see John 5 and 8.

He didn't hesitate to claim equality (Phil. 2) with the [Creator]{.class_4501} who made the nebulas, star clusters, galaxies, and living creatures. Mohammed didn't dare, although he claimed an equal authority with [Jesus]{.class_4501} as a prophet and a [superior place]{.class_4501} as a "Messenger" or "Apostle."

2\. He spoke with [Complete Originality.]{.class_4501} His "golden rule" did not come from Brahmanism or Hinduism; their "golden rule" was NEGATIVE. He borrowed nothing from the "wisdom of ancients" in India or Egypt or anywhere else, and He no more bothered to quote Thales or Anaximander or Socrates or Plato or Aristotle or Pythagoras than if they had never been born.

Everything you, or anyone else, read in pagan folklore or Greek, Roman, or Egyptian mythology that [looked]{.class_4501} like something Christ is recorded to have said came right out of Genesis--Malachi, with 80 percent of that being written [before 1000 B.C.]{.class_4501} (Solomon, David, Moses, Samuel, et al.).

3\. He spoke with [Infinite Compassion:]{.class_4501} look at His utterances in Matthew 11:28--30; Luke 15:18--22; and John 10:11--15.

No Pharisee, Sadducee, or scribe spoke that way anywhere in four gospels. This is a GOOD shepherd who cares for the sheep (John 10). He is not at all like the Roman Catholic Pope in Zechariah 11:17, whose sins were covered up by Elmer Towns, Jerry Falwell, Truman Dollar, and Curtis Hutson in their [New King James Version]{.class_4501} (Zech. 11:17). That ["IDOL shepherd"]{.class_4500} was a ["hireling"]{.class_4500}[]{#6g89t5Y_Rh2_sEm6hxUWsg1670.xhtml_page_202} (John 10:13).

4\. He spoke with [Childlike Simplicity:]{.class_4501} there are no ["great swelling words of vanity"]{.class_4500} (2 Pet. 2:18; Jude 16), since the speaker had no ["respect of persons"]{.class_4500} in any audience (Luke 10:21; 1 Cor. 2:4) to whom He spoke. Jesus talked about dogs and wolves, pigs and sheep. He talked about birds and lilies and farmers and crops. He spoke of children and servants, maidens and widows, death and sin, Hell and judgment, faith and God, etc. No one has to have a [high school education]{.class_4501} to follow any discourse Jesus Christ ever preached. How unlike modern twentieth and twenty-first century "ministers"!

The greatest preachers all modeled themselves after Him. Luther, Whitefield, and J. Frank Norris spoke with [majestic authority.]{.class_4501} Sam Jones, Billy Sunday, and Lester Roloff were completely [original.]{.class_4501} Phillips Brooks, John Knox, and William Booth spoke with [infinite compassion,]{.class_4501} and the [childlike simplicity]{.class_4501} of Bob Jones Sr.'s sermons (and Dallas Billington's sermons) were legendary.

[Dwight Moody]{.class_4501} was a pleading father.

[Jonathan Edwards]{.class_4501} was a severe debater.

[R. A. Torrey]{.class_4501} was a legalist lawyer with a "brief."

[John Bunyan]{.class_4501} was a sublime allegorist.

[DeWitt Talmage]{.class_4501} was a fabulous, orating poet.

[George Whitefield]{.class_4501} was an overwhelming actor.

[Billy Sunday]{.class_4501} riveted red hot texts.

[Luther]{.class_4501} was a cannonade of theology.

But! ["Never man spake like THIS man"]{.class_4500} (vs. 46)!

What man on earth would dare say ["I am THE way, THE truth, and THE life"]{.class_4500} and ["no man cometh unto the Father, but by ME"]{.class_4500} and get anyone to believe him!?

No man could say it and get any sane person on earth to [believe it.]{.class_4504} As it turned out, more than 900,000,000 people, since A.D. 33, [believed]{.class_4504} Jesus Christ. They then put Him "to the test" (see John 7:17 and comments) and found out He told [the truth.]{.class_4504}

["But this people who knoweth not the law are cursed"]{.class_4500} (vs. 49).

This is a typical, asinine statement from a bunch of dangerous []{#6g89t5Y_Rh2_sEm6hxUWsg1670.xhtml_page_203}blockheads who know no more about their own Mosaic Law than the Secretary General of the United Nations. "Knowing the law" has nothing to do with whether you are cursed or not. ["By the law is the knowledge of sin." "As many as are of the works of the law are under the curse: for it is written\...."]{.class_4500} The Mosaic Law contained cursing and blessing according to Deuteronomy 28:1--40; Leviticus 26:1--35; Deuteronomy 30:15--20, 27:12--26.

"Knowing it" (vs. 49) would no more get you out from under its curse than as if you had never known it; as a matter of Biblical [fact,]{.class_4501} any sinner was better off with NO law (Rom. 4:15) and no knowledge of sins (Rom. 5:13). That is how Adam and Eve were before they fell. But here they are!---the most "doubly separated," clean-living, [Bible-searching,]{.class_4501} religious [leaders]{.class_4501} of their day talking exactly as they do in A.D. 2004: like blind Bartimaeus on a weekend drunk.

Now Nicodemus (with his heart sinking down to his sandals) puts "his foot in his mouth" and tries to defend the Lord Jesus (vs. 50--51). He does it by appealing to the Mosaic Law; the reference is to Deuteronomy 25:1, 17:4--9.

They "snap his head off." As true, dogmatic, narrow-minded, blind bigots, they refuse even to deal with their own Law. Nicodemus asked them what the Law said (vs. 51). They immediately abandoned the entire Law (see that double standard stuff---7:22--23) and screamed (while slobbering at the mouth): ["Art thou also of Galilee? Search, and look ]{.class_4500}\[they had done neither\][:]{.class_4500} [for out of Galilee ariseth no prophet."]{.class_4500} Wrong again; you forgot to search 2 Kings 14 and look at verse 25. Gathhepher [is]{.class_4501} in Galilee. Jonah was the only prophet that Jesus chose to signify His own death, burial, and resurrection (Matt. 12:40; Jonah 2:1--10).

Verse 53: "Meeting adjourned!" Forty "elders" left and the door only slammed once.

[]{#6g89t5Y_Rh2_sEm6hxUWsg1869.xhtml}

# Chapter 8 {.heading_74}

1 Jesus went unto the mount of Olives.

2 And early in the morning he came again into the temple, and all the people came unto him; and he sat down, and taught them.

3 And the scribes and Pharisees brought unto him a woman taken in adultery; and when they had set her in the midst,

4 They say unto him, Master, this woman was taken in adultery, in the very act.

5 Now Moses in the law commanded us, that such should be stoned: but what sayest thou?

6 This they said, tempting him, that they might have to accuse him. But Jesus stooped down, and with his finger wrote on the ground, as though he heard them not.

7 So when they continued asking him, he lifted up himself, and said unto them, He that is without sin among you, let him first cast a stone at her.

8 And again he stooped down, and wrote on the ground.

9 And they which heard it, being convicted by their own conscience, went out one by one, beginning at the eldest, even unto the last: and Jesus was left alone, and the woman standing in the midst.

10 When Jesus had lifted up himself, and saw none but the woman, he said unto her, Woman, where are those thine accusers? hath no man condemned thee?

11 She said, No man, Lord. And Jesus said unto her, Neither do I condemn thee: go, and sin no more.

As you might have guessed after reading John 7:47--52, the best Greek scholars behind every godless, depraved, perverted, "newer translation"---any of 214 English translations between 1880 and 2004---decided that you should cut thirteen verses out of the Holy Scriptures: you should pretend that every word found between ["and every man went"]{.class_4500} (7:53) to ["go, and sin no more"]{.class_4500} (8:11) should be cut out with Jehudi's penknife (Jer. 36) and cast into the fire.

In keeping with "original sin" (see Eve, Gen. 3:3), OMISSIONS compose the "critical canon" of the Alexandrian Cult; see Balaam's case in [The Unknown Bible,]{.class_4501} chapter 1, 1996. So! Out went more than 225 words from the Authorized text.

The depraved, conceited, stumbling, bungling apostates who did this (Lachmann, Tischendorf, Griesbach, Nida, Nestle, Aland, Bruce, Metzger, Custer, Bob Jones III, et al.---they all upheld [Sinaiticus]{.class_4501} and [Vaticanus]{.class_4501} at this point as being true to the "original" Greek text) didn't even notice that if they made the deletions their text would jump from a [private meeting]{.class_4501} of the temple officers with Nicodemus and the Sanhedrin [inside]{.class_4501} a building (John 7:46--52) to Jesus Christ being present talking to THEM---[in the Sanhedrin]{.class_4501} (look at 8:12)!

Such are the ways of sin, death, and Hell when dealing with "highly qualified," "recognized authorities" on [Puss in Boots]{.class_4501} and [Ali Baba and the Forty Thieves.]{.class_4501}

Now there is no need at this point to bore our readers to death with the "jots" and "tittles" of the manuscript evidence compiled by the Lachmann-Tischendorf-Griesbach-Hort-Aland-Metzger crew: they are all one unit. They were the leaders in the final apostasy of the Body of Christ since their bungling guesswork was accepted by the faculties and staffs of the top fifty Christian colleges and universities (and seminaries) in America and Europe; they constitute the "avant garde" of a mass of carnal, worldly, pseudo-intellectual [dimwits]{.class_4501} who produced the [RV, ASV, RSV, NASV, NKJV, NRSV, ]{.class_4501}and the [NIV.]{.class_4501} We might call them "The Blockheads Brigade" or "The Bastion of Bull Shooters."

To be as brief as possible, before rejecting 100 percent of their "findings," along with [all]{.class_4501} of their allegations, opinions, interpretations, theories, and guesses; the passage is referred to as a "pericope." It is omitted by the two most corrupt uncial manuscripts extant ([Sinaiticus]{.class_4501} and [Vaticanus:]{.class_4501} Aleph and B). Only uncial manuscript D ([Cantabrigiensis]{.class_4501}) is more sloppily transcribed.

In addition to these are uncial manuscripts A ([Alexandrianus]{.class_4501}), C ([Ephraemi Rescriptus]{.class_4501}), T, X, and Delta. About seventy uncials omit the "pericope." Now watch how the Jewish leaders duck and sidestep the attack while they quickly change the subject. They will not discuss the ["WORDS"]{.class_4500} of Scripture: they will pretend that some individual is the "issue."

48 Then answered the Jews, and said unto him, Say we not well that thou art a Samaritan, and hast a devil?

49 Jesus answered, I have not a devil; but I honour my Father, and ye do dishonour me.

50 And I seek not mine own glory: there is one that seeketh and judgeth.

51 Verily, verily, I say unto you, If a man keep my saying, he shall never see death.

52 Then said the Jews unto him, Now we know that thou hast a devil. Abraham is dead, and the prophets; and thou sayest, If a man keep my saying, he shall never taste of death.

How often have I seen that tactic among Fundamental Baptists since 1949. "Let's discuss 'Ruckman,' not the Book." The problem is "Ruckman" or "Ruckmanites," not the Book. "Let's talk about 'Ruckman,' but don't keep talking about that [Book!"]{.class_4504}

"Thou art a Samaritan, and hast a devil?"

Hey, you crawfishing backslider! What happened to those ["WORDS"]{.class_4500} you were accused of not hearing (vs. 47)? Dropped the discussion like a hot rock, didn't you, baby? Ich denke ja!

["I have not a devil, etc."]{.class_4500} (vs. 49). All is clear. Jesus faces issues, immediately, and answers.

Verse 50, see comments under John 5:23, 30.

["He shall never SEE death"]{.class_4500} (vs. 51). The thing is absolutely irreconcilable with the fact that all men die, saved or lost. But note the wording: ["shall never SEE death."]{.class_4500} It is David who says that he is only going to see the ["SHADOW"]{.class_4500} of death (Psa. 23:4), although he may "taste it" (see Heb. 2:9).

Our Lord had many "sayings" (vs. 51). To which one is He referring here? The commentators pretend it is a plural by referring to verse 31. There, the reference would be to His teachings and sayings (plural). However, the ["saying,"]{.class_4500}[]{#6g89t5Y_Rh2_sEm6hxUWsg1869.xhtml_page_230} undoubtedly, is the one in verses 32, 35, and 36. Note the unity of these expressions: ["shall die in your sins," "Will he KILL himself?" "I said\...ye shall die in your sins\...ye shall die in your sins," "if a man keep my saying he shall never see death," "the truth shall make you free," "if the Son\...shall make you free," "the Son abideth forever," "but ye seek to kill me," "HE SHALL NEVER SEE DEATH"]{.class_4500} (vss. 21, 22, 24, 51, 32, 36, 35, 37, 5).

The ["saying"]{.class_4500} they are to keep in order to be FREE from death is believing that Jesus [is]{.class_4501} Jehovah; ["Die\...if ye believe NOT that I AM HE"]{.class_4500} (vs. 24). That this is the saying is absolutely certain, for He repeats the statement in verse 55 and then in stronger terms in verse 58: ["Before Abraham was I AM."]{.class_4500}

His adversaries misquote Him (vs. 52). He didn't say the believer would not ["TASTE death"]{.class_4500}; He said He would not ["SEE DEATH."]{.class_4500}

53 Art thou greater than our father Abraham, which is dead? and the prophets are dead: whom makest thou thyself?

54 Jesus answered, If I honour myself, my honour is nothing: it is my Father that honoureth me; of whom ye say, that he is your God:

55 Yet ye have not known him; but I know him: and if I should say, I know him not, I shall be a liar like unto you: but I know him, and keep his saying.

56 Your father Abraham rejoiced to see my day: and he saw it, and was glad.

57 Then said the Jews unto him, Thou art not yet fifty years old, and hast thou seen Abraham?

58 Jesus said unto them, Verily, verily, I say unto you, Before Abraham was, I am.

59 Then took they up stones to cast at him: but Jesus hid himself, and went out of the temple, going through the midst of them, and so passed by.

We have commented already on verse 54. Verse 53 is self explanatory.

["Your father Abraham REJOICED TO SEE MY DAY"]{.class_4500} (vs. []{#6g89t5Y_Rh2_sEm6hxUWsg1869.xhtml_page_231}56).

The verse is used by all apostate Fundamentalists to prove that God gave Abraham a "video" of the events that took place on Calvary. Their unholy purpose in doing this was to prove that Abraham's salvation---and then David's salvation under the Mosaic Law---was ["by grace\...through faith,"]{.class_4500} exactly as it is found in the Church Age in the [Pauline Epistles.]{.class_4501} By pretending that Romans 4:22--24 means Abraham's salvation was IDENTICAL with ours (which it certainly was NOT), instead of a TYPE of ours (which it certainly was), the apostate, Laodicean Christian colleges and universities [invented]{.class_4501} a heresy called "Ruckmanism" which they condemned on the grounds that: "All the Old Testament saints were saved by looking [forward]{.class_4501} to the cross (Abraham's "video"), and all New Testament saints are saved by looking [back]{.class_4501} at the cross" (without a video, I suppose)!

Thus "Ruckman" became a heretic for believing there is a [difference]{.class_4501} between the Old Testament and the New Testament. For example: Romans 10:1--8; John 1:17; Galatians 1:8­--12; Romans 2--4; 2 Corinthians 3; Ezekiel 18. Having done this, 100 percent of these Bible-rejecting, "historic position" traditionalists threw away the key to Tribulation salvation and claimed that Tribulation saints also will be saved ["by grace\...through faith"]{.class_4500} in ["the]{.class_4500} [gospel of the grace of God"]{.class_4500} as found in the Pauline epistles.

This stupid and [blasphemous]{.class_4504} private interpretation was done in the face of Matthew 24:13, Revelation 12:17, 14:12, 22:14. The blinding, shattering revelation is that there are TWO DIFFERENT BODIES OF SAVED PEOPLE IN THE TRIBULATION (Rev. 7:3--9). Furthermore, the ["gospel"]{.class_4500} preached [there]{.class_4504} (Rev. 14:6) doesn't mention Jesus Christ coming or living or dying for anyone, nor does it mention the regeneration of [anyone ]{.class_4504}by any means, let alone ["by grace\...through faith."]{.class_4500}

"And I saw another angel fly in the midst of heaven, having the everlasting gospel to preach unto them that dwell on the earth, and to every nation, and kindred, and tongue, and people,

Saying with a loud voice, Fear God, and give glory to him; for the hour of his judgment is come: and worship []{#6g89t5Y_Rh2_sEm6hxUWsg1869.xhtml_page_232}him that made heaven, and earth, and the sea, and the fountains of waters.

[And there followed another angel, saying, Babylon is fallen, is fallen, that great city, because she made all nations drink of the wine of the wrath of her fornication" ]{.class_4500}(Rev. 14:6--8).

According to the Pauline epistles, THAT angel was ["cursed"]{.class_4501} (Gal. 1:8--9). [He certainly was NOT cursed.]{.class_4501}

There is just one more great shining example of the theme song we have sung for fifty-five years: "Always correct the Greek with the English," and in a minor key: "All professional liars in the Alexandrian Cult are [cloned robots]{.class_4501} programmed by the same jackass," "the bigger the belfry the more room for the bats," and "when in doubt always throw ALL scholarship out. O-U-T! [OUT!"]{.class_4501}

How do you solve that blatant contradiction which is just as "real as rain"? Well, no "HISTORIC POSITION" by any Baptist could solve it. Then how can it be solved?

"Elementary, my dear apostate!" You simply remove [two words]{.class_4501} from all the new Bibles in 2 Timothy 2:15: ["STUDY"]{.class_4500}---that way they can never solve the problems---and ["DIVIDING"---]{.class_4500}that way they cannot see the differences between the Church Age and the Old Testament and the Tribulation. So said, so done! Every edition of the [Authorized Version]{.class_4501} since 1611---excluding its [one]{.class_4501} grossly corrupt edition put out by Jerry Falwell, Curtis Hutson, A. V. Henderson, Elmer Towns, Hindson, Harold Ockenga, Wally Criswell, et al. ([NKJV]{.class_4501})---reads as follows:

["Study to shew thyself approved unto God, a workman that needeth not to be ashamed, rightly dividing the word of truth" ]{.class_4500}(2 Tim. 2:15).

You will not find THAT verse in 214 corrupt English counterfeits published by Christians between A.D. 1880 and 2000.

If a man "rightly divided" Revelation 7 and 14 FROM Galatians 3:13 and 1 Corinthians 1:6--7 and Galatians 1:8--10, he would know that FAITH and WORKS are the "plan of salvation" in both the Old Testament and the Tribulation. "IGNORANCE IS BLISS." The only problem is every one of these Bible-rejecting, Bible-correcting [apostates]{.class_4501}---and that is exactly what they are---are not satisfied for []{#6g89t5Y_Rh2_sEm6hxUWsg1869.xhtml_page_233}bluffing and lying their way through. They insist in devoting their entire lives to bringing YOU down to THEIR OWN level of [ignorance.]{.class_4501} That has been the "stock-and-trade" of the faculties and staffs of every major Christian seminary, college, and university in America, England, and Europe since A.D. 1800.

Now! Ready for some "Bible" instead of some cloned robots's "historic position"?

How did Abraham know about the crucifixion when no one "staked" victims out till the Assyrians put it in vogue more than 500 years after Abraham was dead? How did Abraham "look forward to the cross" when not even Simon Peter knew that it was a blood atonement that "blotted out" sins (Col. 2:14) BEFORE the Second Advent (Heb. 8; Rom. 11; Acts 3)? How did Peter and John FAIL to see "Christ's day" even after He arose from the dead (John 20:9)? Abraham SAW that, did he? And then he kept his mouth shut about it when bringing up Isaac and Jacob? And then they failed to pass it on to Gad, Naphtali, Dan, Asher, Benjamin, Levi, Judah, et al? Sure, sonny, sure. Here, take some TYLENOL.

Peter, James, and John didn't even know what the ["rising from the dead" ]{.class_4500}meant (Mark 9:10) while being told about it by the Man they followed for forty-two months.

And Abraham SAW the death, burial, and resurrection of Christ, did he?

He "saw it" like Mohammed saw an "angel" with 600 wings.

To see Christ's "day" and "rejoice in it" was Abraham's actions in Genesis 22 (one of the clearest pictures of John 3:16 in the Bible). There you learned that he believed in a resurrection (see his words in Gen. 22:5 to his attendants), and you learned of his JOY when "God provided himself" a lamb (Gen. 22:8) so he didn't have to actually kill his son.

Abraham was never "born again."

Neither was Issac.

Abraham was [not]{.class_4501} justified when he got imputed righteousness (cf. Gen. 15:1--6, 22:10--11; James 2:21). Neither was Isaac.

Abraham was never "ADOPTED" (Eph. 1:5). Neither was Isaac or Jacob.

Abraham was [never]{.class_4501} in the BODY of Christ (1 Cor. 12).

Abraham was [never]{.class_4501}[]{#6g89t5Y_Rh2_sEm6hxUWsg1869.xhtml_page_234} spiritually circumcised (Col. 2).

Abraham did [not]{.class_4501} go to the third heaven when he died (2 Cor. 12).

Abraham had [none]{.class_4501} of his sins "taken away" (Heb. 10:4).

Abraham was [not]{.class_4501} "cleared," although he was "forgiven" (Exod. 34:7).

David could [lose]{.class_4501} the Holy Spirit (Psa. 51:11).

David committed two "mortal" sins (Psa. 51:16).

David was [never]{.class_4501} in Christ's "body" (1 Cor. 12:13--23).

David did not go to the third heaven when he died (2 Cor. 12:1--4).

David was [not]{.class_4501} spiritually circumcised (Col. 2).

David was [never]{.class_4501} a "son" of God (Rom. 8) by the New Birth.

David preached salvation by WORKS (Psa. 24, 22:22--26).

Salvation in the Old Testament is certainly [not]{.class_4501} like salvation under the New Testament in this age. [Anyone who thinks they are identical is a VIABLE HERETIC.]{.class_4501} The people who will "die in their sins" (right here in your text: John 8:21, 24), die in their sins [AFTER]{.class_4501} living a righteous life, in Ezekiel 18:16--24.

Is that enough for you in one day? Believe me, it is too much for Bob Jones III or Chuck Swindoll. [Look up the references this time.]{.class_4504} This time YOU do some studying instead of me doing it for you. ["Study to shew THYSELF approved unto God."]{.class_4500}

["Hast thou seen Abraham?"]{.class_4500} (vs. 57).

You betcha your booties He "saw Abraham." He saw him in Ur of the Chaldees (Gen. 12). He saw him in Haran, Bethel, and Hai (Gen. 12); and He saw him in Beersheba, Hebron, and Gerar (Gen. 14--24).

But our Lord, here, has lost patience with His tormentors. Instead of saying "Yes, I saw him," He "cuts the deck and deals."

"BEFORE ABRAHAM WAS, I AM!"

That bowling ball knocked over all ten pins set up by Judge Rutherford and Pastor Russell (the Jehovah's Witnesses), for He did not say "I WAS." Jesus said ["I AM."]{.class_4500} That is the ["tetragrammaton"]{.class_4501} of the Jewish faith.

By saying that, Jesus Christ claimed not only to have seen [Abraham,]{.class_4501} but all of his [forefathers:]{.class_4501} Nahor, Eber, Arphaxad, Shem, Noah, Lamech, Methuselah, Enoch, Jared, Enos, Seth, and ADAM.

In short---to wrap up the bundle nicely---Jesus Christ, with that utterance (["I AM"]{.class_4500}), claimed to be the [Creator of the universe]{.class_4501}[]{#6g89t5Y_Rh2_sEm6hxUWsg1869.xhtml_page_235} (Gen. 1; Col. 1; John 1) with no "ifs," "buts," "ands," "howevers," or "may have beens" to it. He claimed what Thomas [confessed]{.class_4501} Him to be (John 20) and what John stated He [was]{.class_4501} (1 John 5:20).

With that one breath, Jesus Christ claimed that all of the following sinners were lying, false prophets: Caiaphas, Ananias, Judas, Mohammed, Charles Darwin, Albert Einstein, Gautama Buddha, Karl Marx, Winston Churchill, Abraham Lincoln, Thomas Jefferson, Ben Franklin, Mao Tse-tung, Hitler, Lenin, Stalin, Norman Vincent Peale, Mark Twain, Earl Stanley Jones, Mahatma Gandhi, Dean Weigle, James Jones, Norman Mailer, FDR, JFK, Bobbie and Ted Kennedy, Yassir Arafat, Saddam Hussein, John Dewey, Benito Mussolini, Leonard Bernstein, George Bernard Shaw, Plato, Aristotle, William James, Karl Menninger, Jung, Picasso, Pavlov, Glasser, Horace Mann, the leaders of the NEA, the members of the ACLU, and every Deist and Theist who ever believed what devils believe: ["ONE GOD"]{.class_4500} (James 2:19).

So! So they ["took\...up stones to cast at him"]{.class_4500} (vs. 59).

They understood exactly WHO He professed to be.

He professed to be the One who [wrote]{.class_4501} the Ten Commandments on tables of stone and brought Israel up out of the land of Egypt (see Acts 7:45 in the [King James]{.class_4501} English text of 1611).

At that point Jesus simply "melts into the crowd" (vs. 59). His enemies cannot throw a rock at Him without hitting someone else (see 8:2--3 and 10:10, 31).

[]{#6g89t5Y_Rh2_sEm6hxUWsg2173.xhtml}

# Chapter 9 {.heading_74}

This chapter is one of the most unusual chapters in the Bible when one considers the Lord's sense of "balance" (Prov. 11:1, 20:23). It is a peculiarity of God's nature, if one is a "modern man" trying to analyze God, because He seems to be "off balance" (see pp. 190, John 6) if weighed in the scales of science, religion, philosophy, and intellectuality. The Lord, here, takes time out to devote [forty-one verses]{.class_4504} to His Son's dealing with [one sinner]{.class_4504} back in A.D. 33. He does this after allotting [thirty-one verses]{.class_4504} to the creation of the universe (Gen. 1).

Think about that two or three years. God took more time to tell you about an adulteress woman (John 4---twenty-eight verses) than He did to describe a [New Birth]{.class_4501} that would keep you out of Hell.

9:1 And as Jesus passed by, he saw a man which was blind from his birth.

2 And his disciples asked him, saying, Master, who did sin, this man, or his parents, that he was born blind?

3 Jesus answered, Neither hath this man sinned, nor his parents: but that the works of God should be made manifest in him.

The question His disciples ask Him shows a common train of thought that nearly all human beings follow when seeing someone "down-and-out" or "getting it in the neck." We assume it must be because of [sin]{.class_4504} (cf. Acts 28:4, for example). The blind man must have been born blind either because God knew he was [going to sin]{.class_4504} after his birth or because his parents sinned [before his birth]{.class_4504} (vs. 2). This type of immediate judging may be wrong, but it certainly must have a basis, or it wouldn't be so universal and perennial. It was universal in Job's day (1,800 years before this), for all three of Job's buddies attributed his misfortunes to his "misconduct." As in Job's case, this blind man (vs. 2) had been blinded so the ["works of God should be]{.class_4500} [made manifest in him"]{.class_4500} (vs. 3); ditto Job (Job 41--42).

4 I must work the works of him that sent me, while it is day: the night cometh, when no man can work.

5 As long as I am in the world, I am the light of the world.

Here is a repetition of the theme we found in John 1:4--5, 7, 9, 3:19--21, 8:12). John speaks of it again in his first epistle (1 John 1:5, 7--8). If you "need some light" on something, you will get more "light" on it (see Col. 2:3) by coming to ["the light of the world"]{.class_4500} than you will spending thirty years in post-grad work in Medicine, Law, Astronomy, Geology, Biology, Chemistry, Astronomy, or Physics.

["The night cometh"]{.class_4500} (vs. 4). Using daylight (6 AM to 6 PM) as a picture of the Church Age where a Christian can work (see comments on Matt. 20 in that Commentary), the "night that cometh" will be the Great Tribulation. If you use "nighttime" (6 PM to 6 AM) as a picture of the Church Age (see comments on Matt. 24:42--43 in that Commentary), then "sunrise" is the Millennium (see comments on Matt. 13:43 in that Commentary).

This means that, HERE, the Lord is preparing them for a rapture in Acts 7 and a Tribulation which will start in less than three years after His return (Acts 1) to glory (check the references on John the Baptist being "Elijah" given at length under John 1:21).

["He\...spat on the ground, and made clay of the spittle and anointed the eyes of the blind man" ]{.class_4500}(vs. 6).

I heard a great "old-time" Scotch preacher (James McGinlay) commenting on this verse and he said: "Wouldn't it have been a howl if the three blind men mentioned here and in Mark 8:22 and Luke 18:42 had come together and discussed the proper way to get healed of blindness?" He went on to imagine them founding three different denominations for their followers: 1. The Spitters. 2. The Clay Anointers. 3. The Speakers.

Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown (commentators), just as blind as a bat backing up in broad daylight, remarks, "It is absurd to IMAGINE" that the clay and spit had anything to do with "the effect that followed."

Like all 300 blind, Bible-correcting scholars before them, they forgot that when God made man out of the ["dust of the ground"]{.class_4500} it was covered with water (["a mist\...watered the whole face of the ground,"]{.class_4500}[]{#6g89t5Y_Rh2_sEm6hxUWsg2173.xhtml_page_238} Gen. 2:6--7). Adam's name was "mud." He came from a CLAY mud ball made UNDERGROUND (Psa. 139:15) where ["gold\...dust"]{.class_4500} (Job 22:24) was found (Gen. 2:11--12). \[But we must forebear here again. "Gold Finger" and John 9:6 is really more than "Midas" and the Scholars' Union can handle.\]

["Go, wash in the pool of Siloam"]{.class_4500} (vs. 7). This pool is found in Isaiah 8:6 (spelled ["Shiloah"]{.class_4500}). The interpretation given by John (["which is by interpretation, Sent"]{.class_4500}) points to the Healer, for this word ["sent"]{.class_4500} has just popped up FIVE times in the preceding chapter (vss. 16, 18, 26, 29, 42). See also John 4:34, 5:23, 30, 37, 6:38--40,44.

The blind man is healed (vs. 7).

8 The neighbours therefore, and they which before had seen him that he was blind, said, Is not this he that sat and begged?

9 Some said, This is he: others said, He is like him: but he said, I am he.

10 Therefore said they unto him, How were thine eyes opened?

11 He answered and said, A man that is called Jesus made clay, and anointed mine eyes, and said unto me, Go to the pool of Siloam, and wash: and I went and washed, and I received sight.

12 Then said they unto him, Where is he? He said, I know not.

Verses 8--10 are self explanatory.

The blind man's testimony is so straight forward and simple no one could possibly misunderstand it or misinterpret it. ["Go\...wash\...I went\...washed\...I received sight"]{.class_4500} (vs. 11). Thus any real Christian's testimony is easily understood by anyone with an IQ of 80. "I was a sinner. I accepted Jesus Christ as my Saviour. I trusted His death on the cross to take away my sins. I asked Him for salvation and He saved me. I now have eternal life as a gift from Him."

Later, the blind man reduces this to ["Whereas I WAS blind, NOW I see"]{.class_4500} (vs. 25). That has been the true experience of more []{#6g89t5Y_Rh2_sEm6hxUWsg2173.xhtml_page_239}than 500,000,000 sinners since A.D. 33. It is worth singing about: "I once was lost but now I'm found, was blind but NOW I see!"

13 They brought to the Pharisees him that aforetime was blind.

14 And it was the sabbath day when Jesus made the clay, and opened his eyes.

15 Then again the Pharisees also asked him how he had received his sight. He said unto them, He put clay upon mine eyes, and I washed, and do see.

16 Therefore said some of the Pharisees, This man is not of God, because he keepeth not the sabbath day. Others said, How can a man that is a sinner do such miracles? And there was a division among them.

17 They say unto the blind man again, What sayest thou of him, that he hath opened thine eyes? He said, He is a prophet.

18 But the Jews did not believe concerning him, that he had been blind, and received his sight, until they called the parents of him that had received his sight.

19 And they asked them, saying, Is this your son, who ye say was born blind? how then doth he now see?

20 His parents answered them and said, We know that this is our son, and that he was born blind:

21 But by what means he now seeth, we know not; or who hath opened his eyes, we know not: he is of age; ask him: he shall speak for himself.

22 These words spake his parents, because they feared the Jews: for the Jews had agreed already, that if any man did confess that he was Christ, he should be put out of the synagogue.

23 Therefore said his parents, He is of age; ask him.

The Sabbath issue arises again; both cases occur during a feast. The last time it occurred was in the spring, during the [Passover]{.class_4504} (see John 5:16); now it occurs at the close (John 7:37) of the [Feast of Tabernacles]{.class_4504} in the Fall.

The Pharisees put the man "on the spot" (vs. 15), and he answers []{#6g89t5Y_Rh2_sEm6hxUWsg2173.xhtml_page_240}for the second time (vs. 15). Again, there is "division" as to whether or not Jesus Christ is a man ["of God"]{.class_4500} (vs. 16) or just a sinner deserving death (see Num. 15:32--35). ["How can a man that is a SINNER do such miracles?"]{.class_4500} (vs. 16).

Here, one should take note of the difference between a ["sinner"]{.class_4500} under the Law and a "sinner" in the Pauline epistles. Under grace, everything that walks and breathes is a "sinner" (Rom. 3, 5; Eph. 2). Under the Law, a ["sinner"]{.class_4500} was a "bad" person---an "evil doer." No Pharisee or Sadducee or scribe would classify himself as a ["sinner"]{.class_4500} (see Luke 7:39). ["Publicans and sinners"]{.class_4500} (see Matt. 9:10--11) would mean that a Jewish tax gatherer (Zachaeus for example, Luke 19) was as rotten as a whore or a thief (see Matt. 21:31) in the eyes of the Pharisee.

Check out Luke 7:37,39. One time, [only,]{.class_4501} in the Pauline epistle does Paul use the word as an [orthodox Jewish rabbi]{.class_4501} would use it, and that is when he is talking with a full-blooded Jew (Simon Peter) about [Gentiles]{.class_4501} (Gal. 2:15). It is essential that the reader grasp this, for if he does not, then when he lands upon John 9:31 he will pick up the "historic position" of 50,000 Bible-correcting [fools]{.class_4501} who misled more than 400,000 preachers on the verse since 1611. God certainly DOES hear ["sinners."]{.class_4500} If He didn't, there would be about ten times as many of them dead and in Hell as there are right now.

Verse 17: this will be the [third time]{.class_4501} the same question was asked. It was answered so plainly the first time there should not have been a repeat unless some twisted, perverted, fault-finding, gnat-straining hypocrite was trying to "build a case." Maybe he could get the healed man to contradict his own testimony.

Since the man's parents didn't answer the question, they return to the blind man and put him on the spot again (vs. 24).

Someone is "working overtime."

["He said, He is a prophet"]{.class_4500} (vs. 17).

Correct (Acts 3:22). That is exactly what He was (Deut. 18).

"Mohammed" was Allah's prophet (the [Koran]{.class_4501}). Poor ole' Allah got dealt a "short hand" when it came to picking "prophets." He must have been pretty stupid to pick an epileptic, fornicating polygamist who was unable (in more than fifty years) to make [one]{.class_4501} historical prophecy to anyone that he didn't borrow from the Old Testament. Jesus Christ makes more than fifty []{#6g89t5Y_Rh2_sEm6hxUWsg2173.xhtml_page_241}[historical prophecies in ten chapters.]{.class_4501} The [Koran]{.class_4501} was unable to record one in [114 chapters.]{.class_4501}

The man's parents duck the question (vs. 21), although they knew perfectly well how he was healed. Their motive was FEAR (vs. 22) as it is explained in John 12:42--43, as well as here. ["THEREFORE said his parents, He is of age; ask him."]{.class_4500}

24 Then again called they the man that was blind, and said unto him, Give God the praise: we know that this man is a sinner.

25 He answered and said, Whether he be a sinner or no, I know not: one thing I know, that, whereas I was blind, now I see.

26 Then said they to him again, What did he to thee? how opened he thine eyes?

27 He answered them, I have told you already, and ye did not hear: wherefore would ye hear it again? will ye also be his disciples?

28 Then they reviled him, and said, Thou art his disciple; but we are Moses' disciples.

29 We know that God spake unto Moses: as for this fellow, we know not from whence he is.

30 The man answered and said unto them, Why herein is a marvellous thing, that ye know not from whence he is, and yet he hath opened mine eyes.

31 Now we know that God heareth not sinners: but if any man be a worshipper of God, and doeth his will, him he heareth.

32 Since the world began was it not heard that any man opened the eyes of one that was born blind.

The reason that "good men" (and many of them are nice, good, polite, friendly, gracious, cooperative personalities) get sucked into the Laodicean apostasy and begin to recommend material that is obviously [Satanic,]{.class_4501} is because their own educations prepared them for only one thing (saved or lost, Liberal or Fundamentalist): to [USE a Book they did not believe was ]{.class_4501}["the holy scriptures"]{.class_4500} (2 Tim. 3:15--16).

Take what you like, what you understand, or what you can use and [discard]{.class_4501} the rest.

10:1 Verily, verily, I say unto you, He that entereth not []{#6g89t5Y_Rh2_sEm6hxUWsg2305.xhtml_page_250}by the door into the sheepfold, but climbeth up some other way, the same is a thief and a robber.

1\. 7 Then said Jesus unto them again, Verily, verily, I say unto you, I am the door of the sheep.

8 All that ever came before me are thieves and robbers: but the sheep did not hear them.

9 I am the door: by me if any man enter in, he shall be saved, and shall go in and out, and find pasture.

10 The thief cometh not, but for to steal, and to kill, and to destroy: I am come that they might have life, and that they might have it more abundantly.

11 I am the good shepherd: the good shepherd giveth his life for the sheep.

12 But he that is an hireling, and not the shepherd, whose own the sheep are not, seeth the wolf coming, and leaveth the sheep, and fleeth: and the wolf catcheth them, and scattereth the sheep.

13 The hireling fleeth, because he is an hireling, and careth not for the sheep.

What follows is neither a [parable]{.class_4501} or an [allegory.]{.class_4501} It is a series of illustrations of Jesus Christ as a Shepherd and His ["flock"]{.class_4500} of saved sinners and their enemies.

["I am the DOOR"]{.class_4500} (vs. 7). The ["door"]{.class_4500} to a Bedouin sheepfold was sometimes a makeshift thing if the flock were traveling across pastures (see Gen. 30:36, 37:12--16; 1 Sam. 25:6--7, 15). The shepherd usually built a circle out of large boulders and left an opening in the circle about six feet across. Once he had the sheep in the "sheepfold" he would lay down across the opening with his rod and his staff. In David's case, a sling also. The idea was simple: a beast couldn't get into the fold without dealing with him. An animal large enough to jump over the rocks or tear them down (see 1 Sam. 17:34--35) would make enough racket to wake the shepherd up. All "home base" sheepfolds had walls around them. Both at home and abroad, they had "sticker bushes" on them (see Hos. 2:6; Prov. 22:5; Isa. 27:4). This was the ancient "barbed wire."

["All that ever came before me"]{.class_4500}: that would be Buddha, Thales, Anaximander, Pythagoras, Pharaoh, Socrates, Plato, Sennacherib, Philo of Alexandria, Nebuchadnezzar, Prometheus, Adonis, Tammuz, Cyrus, Bacchus, Mithras, Xerxes, Hermes, Horus, Seb, Alexander, Isis, Osiris, Brahma, Krishna, Siva, Kali, et al. They were THIEVES, ROBBERS, and [LIARS]{.class_4501}[]{#6g89t5Y_Rh2_sEm6hxUWsg2305.xhtml_page_254} (vs. 8 and John 8:40--44).

["The sheep did NOT hear them"]{.class_4500} (vs. 8) because of verse 4. They know the Shepherd's voice when they hear it. An Arabic sheepherder said, "The only time they will follow a stranger is when they are SICK." This explains what happened to the Body of Christ in America, between 1900 and 2000. They followed the counsel, advice, recommendations, and fellowship of highly-educated PROFESSIONAL [LIARS]{.class_4501} within their own ranks; see Acts 20:30 for Paul's comment. (See also 2 Tim. 3:7 and 4:3--4.)

I knew of a missionary to Israel who said that he tried to imitate the call of a shepherd to his flock while they were grazing. The Arab (1920) told him what was the word to use and demonstrated it once or twice. So the "Yankee" tried it. He stood on a raised portion of ground and called out (in his best "Arabic!") "Meen-ahhh!" About thirty sheep out of forty stopped grazing, looked up at him, blinked, and lowered their heads and went right on feeding. After three unsuccessful "calls" the "owner," smiling, got up and called out the same words---"Menn-ahhh!" The males quit eating and assembled on the "rise." The females came with them and little lambs followed.

["FOR THEY KNOW HIS VOICE"]{.class_4500} (vs. 4). Christ's bride knows that voice; look at it in Song of Solomon 2:10--14.

Pentecostals and Charismatics (95 percent of them) wouldn't know it from the voice of a demon (1 John 4:1--3).

Jesus said if you love me, [feed]{.class_4501} my sheep. You are not supposed to beat them! You are supposed to FEED them." J. Harold Smith grinned at him and said, "True, brother, but I found out a long time ago there is only one thing you can do with a dead sheep---SKIN 'EM!"

22 And it was at Jerusalem the feast of the dedication, []{#6g89t5Y_Rh2_sEm6hxUWsg2305.xhtml_page_262}and it was winter.

23 And Jesus walked in the temple in Solomon's porch.

24 Then came the Jews round about him, and said unto him, How long dost thou make us to doubt? If thou be the Christ, tell us plainly.

25 Jesus answered them, I told you, and ye believed not: the works that I do in my Father's name, they bear witness of me.

26 But ye believe not, because ye are not of my sheep, as I said unto you.

27 My sheep hear my voice, and I know them, and they follow me:

28 And I give unto them eternal life; and they shall never perish, neither shall any man pluck them out of my hand.

29 My Father, which gave them me, is greater than all; and no man is able to pluck them out of my Father's hand.

30 I and my Father are one.

31 Then the Jews took up stones again to stone him.

32 Jesus answered them, Many good works have I shewed you from my Father; for which of those works do ye stone me?

33 The Jews answered him, saying, For a good work we stone thee not; but for blasphemy; and because that thou, being a man, makest thyself God.

34 Jesus answered them, Is it not written in your law, I said, Ye are gods?

35 If he called them gods, unto whom the word of God came, and the scripture cannot be broken;

36 Say ye of him, whom the Father hath sanctified, and sent into the world, Thou blasphemest; because I said, I am the Son of God?

This feast (the Feast of Dedication) took place around the twentieth of December and was originally a celebration set up by Judas Maccabaeus to commemorate the purification of the temple from the profanations of Antiochus Epiphanes (165 B.C.). # Chapter 11 {.heading_74}

11:1 Now a certain man was sick, named Lazarus, of Bethany, the town of Mary and her sister Martha.

2 (It was that Mary which anointed the Lord with ointment, and wiped his feet with her hair, whose brother Lazarus was sick.)

3 Therefore his sisters sent unto him, saying, Lord, behold, he whom thou lovest is sick.

4 When Jesus heard that, he said, This sickness is not unto death, but for the glory of God, that the Son of God might be glorified thereby.

Bethany is on the southeast corner of the Mount of Olives; the word means "date house."

["It was that Mary\...whose brother Lazarus was sick"]{.class_4500} (vs. 2). The commentators identify ["Mary"]{.class_4500} with the woman who came into Simon's house in Bethany with the alabaster box. If this is true, there is a strange insertion in John 12:4 where Judas is said to be ["Simon's son"]{.class_4500} (John 6:71). This statement could be isolated, but if "Simon's house" was where John 12:4 took place, it is a strange thing to notice that if in that chapter Simon was Judas' father, [he was a LEPER]{.class_4501} (Mark 14:3).

This is probably not the case, so Mark 14:3 is probably not a reference to Martha's sister. In one case, the anointing is "two days before the Passover" (Matt. 26:2,7--12), and the other is ["six days before the passover"]{.class_4500} (John 12:1). It is true the accounts could be [supplemental]{.class_4504} when it is pointed out that one anointing was on his ["head"]{.class_4500} (Matthew) and the other was on His ["feet"]{.class_4500} (John 12). Matthew 26:9 and John 12:5 are [probably]{.class_4504} supplemental. But it is hard to imagine the same response twice to the same incident happening only [four days apart.]{.class_4504} That isn't all. The wording of John 12:7 cannot be made to be a denial of Matthew 26:12. Here Bullinger seems to overstretch his "dividing abilities" ([Companion Bible,]{.class_4504} App. 158). He says in one account that Jesus "directs the ointment to be reserved for his burial"---which He does not. He simply tells them [why]{.class_4504} she had kept it. In Matthew, Bullinger says, "he declared it had been kept for that purpose." True, but that is [exactly]{.class_4504}[]{#6g89t5Y_Rh2_sEm6hxUWsg2491.xhtml_page_271} what he said in John in different words; [He certainly did NOT direct any woman to keep any ointment for His burial.]{.class_4504}

["This sickness is not unto death"]{.class_4500} (vs. 4).

Of course it WAS: look at verse 14.

Watch the commentators have a ball with that one! Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown simply pretend He didn't say the first part of verse 4. They are as quiet as a turkey farm on Thanksgiving afternoon. Freerkson says it actually means "the final RESULT of Lazarus' sickness will not be death."[ Of course that is exactly what the text did NOT say.]{.class_4501} You see, when Jesus said ["Lazarus is dead," ]{.class_4500}He ["spake of his DEATH"]{.class_4500} (vs. 13), which WAS the result of his ["sickness"]{.class_4500} (vs. 4). Freerkson's "final result" was just a cover-up for his ignorance.

The Scholars' Union "has a time of it," don't they? When Freerkson hits verse 14 and realizes that he [lied]{.class_4501} about verse 4, he does what all the commentators do. He zips his mouth shut and refuses to comment. He disconnects it from verse 4 and verse 13.

You may have noticed too that the sickness [did]{.class_4501} finally "result" in death, for Lazarus DIED AGAIN [after]{.class_4501} he came out of the grave. The thought didn't occur to any commentator.

If the verse had to be expounded to match the other two, it would have to be "This sickness, at this time, is not going to kill Lazarus [permanently;]{.class_4501} THIS sickness is so God can show you how death is going to be overcome." Of course, that is "paraphrasing over the moon." But that is what is going on. He says ["sleepeth"]{.class_4500} AFTER Lazarus drops dead (vs. 11 cf. vs. 14). The BODY is asleep, as in Matthew 27:52 and 1 Thessalonians 4:13--15, but it IS the sleep of [death]{.class_4501} (look at Isa. 26:19).

["And when he had said this, he FELL ASLEEP"]{.class_4500} (Acts 7:60).

Nobody "woke" Stephen up; [he stayed DEAD]{.class_4501} (Acts 8:1--2).

The truth of the matter is that God Almighty, knowing all things (Acts 15:18), will often give a skeptic a Scripture that contradicts itself or another verse and doesn't make any sense at all, and every attempt by the smart aleck "think tanks" to explain them fails, for they are [designedly]{.class_4501} obscure. If you doubt this for a minute, go back []{#6g89t5Y_Rh2_sEm6hxUWsg2491.xhtml_page_272}and study that John--Elijah stuff (John 1:21) or that "hath seen me" "hath not seen God" stuff (John 1:18, 5:37, 8:19, 8:58, 10:30, 12:45).

Not even the explanation resolves [the difficulty]{.class_4501} in the wording. A knowledge of Greek and Hebrew, in many cases, yields no light at all but only ADDS to the confusion.

At Jerry Falwell's Liberty University, the commentators habitually run to Greek genders, numbers, and tenses in order to "give you light" [on their own ignorance.]{.class_4501} All they do is [add]{.class_4501} to your ignorance (see, for example, the [Liberty Bible Commentary,]{.class_4501} pp. 210, 211, 215, 217, 231, 223, 223, 701, 699, 690, 689, 683, 676, 677, 675, 673, 539, 530, 783, 782, 725, 727, 726, 722, 394, 395, 401, 416, and a hundred other places).

The truth is, in verse 4 Jesus Christ is speaking [doctrinally,]{.class_4501} knowing what is going to take place. He speaks the same way in verse 11. In verse 14, He is speaking [practically.]{.class_4501} (Notice exactly the same thing in John 4:18 when you compare it with the [doctrinal]{.class_4501} statements found in Eph. 5:31; 1 Cor. 6:16--18; and Matt. 19:4--5.) If you want a real "blockbuster," try Luke 21:18. Jesus said that after telling them that some of them would ["be put to DEATH"]{.class_4500} (vs. 16). Work that one out with the "original Greek" and then try it on Matthew 10:23, you blind, stupid, shifting, dodging, ducking, Bible-rejecting "experts"!

When Jerry Falwell's "top kicks" (Woodrow Kroll, Ed Hindson, and James Borland) take on Luke 21:18, all of their typewriters and word processors "go on the blink," all of their ink dries up, they lose all of their ball-point pens, and don't even SUGGEST an interpretation for the verse ([Liberty Bible Commentary,]{.class_4501} p. 195).

When Hindson faces Matthew 10:23, he feels forced to tell the truth, but the truth he tells would get him FIRED from Liberty University if anyone, [including the president,]{.class_4501} dared preach what he [says]{.class_4501} the verse says. Hindson teaches that Peter, James, John, Matthew, Thomas, Bartholomew (Matt. 10:2--3) will all be resurrected [after the Rapture and will preach in the Tribulation]{.class_4501} (ibid. p. 44).

It's a "trip," ain't it?

5 Now Jesus loved Martha, and her sister, and Lazarus.

6 When he had heard therefore that he was sick, he abode two days still in the same place where he was.

7 Then after that saith he to his disciples, Let us go into Judaea again.

8 His disciples say unto him, Master, the Jews of late sought to stone thee; and goest thou thither again?

9 Jesus answered, Are there not twelve hours in the day? If any man walk in the day, he stumbleth not, because he seeth the light of this world.

10 But if a man walk in the night, he stumbleth, because there is no light in him.

["Now Jesus loved Martha, and her sister, and Lazarus"]{.class_4500} (vs. 5). This is a [human love]{.class_4501} showing "favoritism," exactly as Jesus showed it to John (John 13:23) and, for a few minutes, to a rich young ruler (Mark 10:21). This is NOT the ["love"]{.class_4500} found in the passages that deal with the Crucifixion (see comments on John 3:36 and 3:18). God's love for "sinners," in the sense of New Testament salvation, is always manifested in the PAST tense, aimed at ONE DAY in history. A [present tense situation]{.class_4501} like Romans 8:28 and 2 Corinthians 9:7 is plainly [limited]{.class_4501} to a born-again Christian: [there are no unsaved sinners in either passage.]{.class_4501}

Do you see the difference between DOCTRINAL and PRACTICAL statements?

Notice the remarkable statement that "because Jesus loved Martha and Mary" that He refused to come to help them until Lazarus was dead (["When he had heard THEREFORE\...he abode two days still in the same place"]{.class_4500})! Now, how is THAT for an illustration of Romans 8:28?!

Verses 7 and 8 are self explanatory.

The "gist" of verse 9 is "you can't go till your number is up" (American koine: circa early twentieth century). The classic anti-Calvinistic statement was made by some black infantryman in World War II. When his buddy tried to raise his morale so he wouldn't desert, he said, "Quit worryin' 'bout it, man! Dey ain't no bullet what is gwine to hit you, less it's got yo name writtin' on it!" The other replied, "Ah knows dat! Ah knows dat! Dey ain't de bullets dat bothah []{#6g89t5Y_Rh2_sEm6hxUWsg2491.xhtml_page_274}me. De ones what bothes me am dem what's got 'to whom it may concern' writ on 'em!" (Amen and amen!)

The rest of verse 9 would be entirely enigmatic to His disciples, for it is the same refrain that He gave all unbelievers in 9:39--41, 8:12, 5:35, 3:19--20, 1:4--9, 12:36, 46.

[Doctrinally,]{.class_4501} the statement to His disciples would mean that He was about to leave them and they would not be able to walk in "daylight" after He was gone. This came to pass. [Judas]{.class_4501} committed suicide, [Thomas]{.class_4501} lost his faith, [Peter]{.class_4501} lost his discipleship (see Mark 16:7), and the other nine were afraid to assemble in public (John 20:19; Luke 24) for three days.

The verses also can be applied to "any man" (saved or lost in any dispensation), for the darkness of ["night"]{.class_4500} (vs. 10) can be the darkness of John 1:5 and John 3, as well as the disciples' darkness. The light is not "IN" the man being discussed; that plainly has to be SPIRITUAL. He is not referring to a searchlight or candle or lamp or flashlight or torch or a lantern. This would be a prophecy because the ["light IN him"]{.class_4500} is a reference to ["THE LIGHT OF THIS WORLD"]{.class_4500} (vs. 10). Jesus Christ Himself was not IN the body of any believer until Acts 2 (see this prophecy again in John 17:21--26).

11 These things said he: and after that he saith unto them, Our friend Lazarus sleepeth; but I go, that I may awake him out of sleep.

12 Then said his disciples, Lord, if he sleep, he shall do well.

13 Howbeit Jesus spake of his death: but they thought that he had spoken of taking of rest in sleep.

14 Then said Jesus unto them plainly, Lazarus is dead.

15 And I am glad for your sakes that I was not there, to the intent ye may believe; nevertheless let us go unto him.

16 Then said Thomas, which is called Didymus, unto his fellowdisciples, Let us also go, that we may die with him.

We have commented on verse 11 under verse 4. Verse 12 is self explanatory. We commented on verses 13 and 14 under verse 4, which see.

["I am glad for your sakes that I was not there"]{.class_4500} (vs. 15).

Wild Book, isn't it? He is saying "I'm [glad]{.class_4501}[]{#6g89t5Y_Rh2_sEm6hxUWsg2491.xhtml_page_275} I wasn't there, because if I had been our buddy [wouldn't have died]{.class_4501} (see vs. 21), but now he's [dead"]{.class_4501} (vs. 14).

How is [that]{.class_4501} for Romans 8:28?

The purpose was obviously to make BELIEVERS out of twelve disciples. You see, they hadn't [really]{.class_4501} grasped Christ's Deity until John 16:29--31, and even then THAT belief has a [relapse]{.class_4501} between Friday and Wednesday and and Saturday (see comments on Matt. 12:40 in that Commentary). Most readers forget that when Jesus asked His own disciples to [believe on Him]{.class_4501} He used the same words that He did in dealing with [unsaved Pharisees]{.class_4501} (see the text: John 14:11). The [Liberty "Bible" Commentary ]{.class_4501}completely misses this and thereby accuses Christ's killers of just being ["weak in faith"]{.class_4500} (ibid. p. 244). Typical modern "Funnymentalist" scholarship engaged in training "Champions for Christ," etc. Typical twentieth-century "gaffed act."

"Doubting" Thomas now speaks up (vs. 16), where normally it would have been Simon Peter (see comments on Matt. 16:22 in that Commentary). You can say one thing for Thomas; he was just as ready to die with Christ (vs. 16) as Peter was (Matt. 26:35).

17 Then when Jesus came, he found that he had lain in the grave four days already.

18 Now Bethany was nigh unto Jerusalem, about fifteen furlongs off:

19 And many of the Jews came to Martha and Mary, to comfort them concerning their brother.

20 Then Martha, as soon as she heard that Jesus was coming, went and met him: but Mary sat still in the house.

21 Then said Martha unto Jesus, Lord, if thou hadst been here, my brother had not died.

22 But I know, that even now, whatsoever thou wilt ask of God, God will give it thee.

The distance to Bethany is short: about two miles. If you ever needed to notice any Greek word (and God knows you don't need one Greek word in a chapter, on an average), you should notice that the word for ["GRAVE"]{.class_4500} here (vs. 17) is not "Gk.: [hades]{.class_4501}," and it is not "Heb.: [Sheol.]{.class_4501}" It is "Gk.: [mnemeion]{.class_4501}." "[Sheol]{.class_4501}[]{#6g89t5Y_Rh2_sEm6hxUWsg2491.xhtml_page_276}," in the Old Testament, was not a grave in a cemetery at all; it was a grave for SOULS. The Hebrew "grave" for a cemetery "grave" is "[QEBER]{.class_4501}" often found as "[QEBERIM]{.class_4501}" in the plural. "[Sheol]{.class_4501}" is never in the plural.

Verses 19 and 20 are self explanatory.

["Martha\...went out and met him: but Mary"]{.class_4500} (vs. 20).

Still the same old Mary, right (Luke 10:42)? So Martha meets the Lord on His way and starts a conversation with Him (vs. 21). She says, ["But I now that even NOW whatsoever thou wilt ask of God God will give it thee."]{.class_4500} This plainly reveals she believes He can raise her brother out of a grave after he has lain there four days. Why? For the obvious reason she knew about Jairus' daughter (Luke 8:55) and the widow of Nain (Luke 7:11).

23 Jesus saith unto her, Thy brother shall rise again.

24 Martha saith unto him, I know that he shall rise again in the resurrection at the last day.

25 Jesus said unto her, I am the resurrection, and the life: he that believeth in me, though he were dead, yet shall he live:

26 And whosoever liveth and believeth in me shall never die. Believest thou this?

27 She saith unto him, Yea, Lord: I believe that thou art the Christ, the Son of God, which should come into the world.

Here, again, we witness the utter incompetency and total bankruptcy of modern [higher Christian education]{.class_4501} as exhibited by Falwell's Liberty University. 28 And when she had so said, she went her way, and called Mary her sister secretly, saying, The Master is come, and calleth for thee.

29 As soon as she heard that, she arose quickly, and came unto him.

30 Now Jesus was not yet come into the town, but was in that place where Martha met him.

31 The Jews then which were with her in the house, and comforted her, when they saw Mary, that she rose up hastily and went out, followed her, saying, She goeth unto the grave to weep there.

32 Then when Mary was come where Jesus was, and saw him, she fell down at his feet, saying unto him, Lord, if thou hadst been here, my brother had not died.

33 When Jesus therefore saw her weeping, and the Jews also weeping which came with her, he groaned in the spirit, and was troubled,

34 And said, Where have ye laid him? They said unto him, Lord, come and see.

35 Jesus wept.

36 Then said the Jews, Behold how he loved him!

Mary finally gets up and goes about one-fourth of the two miles to meet Christ. Martha had broken off her conversation with Him (vs. 28) and goes to get Mary. 12:1 Then Jesus six days before the passover came to Bethany, where Lazarus was which had been dead, whom he raised from the dead.

4 Then saith one of his disciples, Judas Iscariot, Simon's son, which should betray him,

6 This he said, not that he cared for the poor; but because he was a thief, and had the bag, and bare what was put therein.

8 For the poor always ye have with you; but me ye have not always.
