# Chapter 13 {.heading_74}

13:1 Now before the feast of the passover, when Jesus knew that his hour was come that he should depart out of this world unto the Father, having loved his own which were in the world, he loved them unto the end.

2 And supper being ended, the devil having now put into the heart of Judas Iscariot, Simon's son, to betray him;

3 Jesus knowing that the Father had given all things into his hands, and that he was come from God, and went to God;

4 He riseth from supper, and laid aside his garments; and took a towel, and girded himself.

5 After that he poureth water into a bason, and began to wash the disciples' feet, and to wipe them with the towel wherewith he was girded.

John is the only gospel writer who devotes five chapters to events that took place in less than six hours: chapters 13--17 all take place in the "upper room" of Mark 14:14--16 and the path from there to Gethsemane.

Verse 1 takes place around 6 PM Tuesday evening. ["Having loved his own\...he loved them unto the end"]{.class_4500} (vs. 1). Immediately, you are informed that "one of his own" was a DEVIL, operating under the influence of his "father" (["THE DEVIL having now put into the heart of Judas Iscariot\...to betray him"]{.class_4500} (vs. 2). [Heart trouble]{.class_4501}---again.

In John 17:12, Jesus acknowledges Judas as an apostle that God "gave" to Him, but that none of the others were lost. ["None of them is lost,"]{.class_4500} BUT THAT ONE (["but the son of perdition"]{.class_4500}). He chose Judas (John 6:70) after praying about it (Luke 6:12--16).

["Mine own familiar friend"]{.class_4500} (Psa. 41:9) is a reference to Judas, so He calls him ["friend"]{.class_4500}! (["FRIEND, wherefore art thou come," ]{.class_4500}Matt. 26:50).

Verse 3 is self explanatory, as is verse 4

In these verses, Christ is demonstrating the great theological truths of Philippians 2:7--8. He is waiting on, and ministering to, SINNERS, [as one of them.]{.class_4501} He is preparing to receive []{#6g89t5Y_Rh2_sEm6hxUWsg2882.xhtml_page_313}["the wages of sin,"]{.class_4500} as a [sinner ]{.class_4501}(2 Cor. 5:21), although He was sinless. He lined Himself up ["with the transgressors"]{.class_4500} (Mark 15:28) the day He let John baptize Him (Matt. 3:6, 8, 13--15).

Ahhh! How fortunate for YOU and ME! Ahhh, what God Almighty did when He did [that!]{.class_4504} (See Heb. 2:9,14--15, 5:7--9!) Ohhh, that day when Allah---[you did say that "Allah" was just another name for the God of Abraham, did you not?]{.class_4504}---All you humanists and Moslems? You [did,]{.class_4504} did you not?---the day that "Allah" became like [His creatures,]{.class_4504} which [He created,]{.class_4504} so He could sympathize [with them]{.class_4504} and put up with what [they]{.class_4504} had to put up with and then (glory to God!) die like [they died:]{.class_4504} blood running out, heart stopping, lungs ceasing to breathe, and then a corpse going into a tomb. "Oh happy day that fixed my choice on THEE my Saviour and my God!"

["He\...began to wash the disciples' feet"]{.class_4500} (vs. 5) like a sinner had anointed His feet (12:3).

6 Then cometh he to Simon Peter: and Peter saith unto him, Lord, dost thou wash my feet?

7 Jesus answered and said unto him, What I do thou knowest not now; but thou shalt know hereafter.

8 Peter saith unto him, Thou shalt never wash my feet. Jesus answered him, If I wash thee not, thou hast no part with me.

9 Simon Peter saith unto him, Lord, not my feet only, but also my hands and my head.

10 Jesus saith to him, He that is washed needeth not save to wash his feet, but is clean every whit: and ye are clean, but not all.

11 For he knew who should betray him; therefore said he, Ye are not all clean.

Down the row He goes, around the table, washing feet, and then He comes to His usual problem (see Peter in Matt. 16:22, 26:33--35): [Simon.]{.class_4501} ["Then cometh he to Simon Peter"]{.class_4500} (vs. 6).

["Dost THOU wash MY feet?"]{.class_4500} (vs. 6). The question is a challenge. If you wanted to really "update" the "archaic English," it would have come out like this: "What are you trying to do? Do you []{#6g89t5Y_Rh2_sEm6hxUWsg2882.xhtml_page_314}think for a minute that I am going to let YOU wash MY dirty feet?" That is the "original autograph" if the author was a commercial fisherman; believe me, that is it (minus a four-letter word or two!).

["What I do thou knowest NOT now"]{.class_4500} (vs. 7).

Which means that if anyone thinks that "footwashing" should be a church ordinance they have lost their marbles somewhere on the way to the game. Of course Peter knew "what He was doing"; [all of them did. ]{.class_4501}He was washing their feet.[ "But thou shalt know hereafter"]{.class_4500} (vs. 7). Like when? He says the same words to Pete in verse 36: ["but thou shalt follow me afterwards."]{.class_4500} Well, that was fulfilled when John 21:18 took place. The first postponement (vs. 7) became a reality in John 21:15 when Peter had to get washed again (by the word!) after losing his discipleship (cf. Mark 16:7).

["If I wash thee not, thou hast no part with me"]{.class_4500} (vs. 8).

Peter has just flatly contradicted the Lord again (like he did in Matt. 26:33--35). ["Thou shalt never wash my feet!"]{.class_4500} (Wanna bet?) But you will have to "take your hat off" to Simon Peter. If his [head ]{.class_4501}was messed up occasionally, his HEART never was. His response to verse 8 is, "Well, if THAT'S the case I want the full load; lay it on me. If it has to do with fellowshipping with [You,]{.class_4501} then add my arms, legs, hands, chest, back, and head to the feet!" (American "original autograph," circa 1990)

["He that IS washed needeth not save to wash his FEET, but is clean every whit: and YE ARE CLEAN, but not all"]{.class_4500} (vs. 10). ["For he knew who would BETRAY HIM"]{.class_4500} (vs. 11).

And there is the key to interpret the last seven verses.

If anyone doubted it, let him read Ephesians 5:26 and then look at this: ["Now ye are CLEAN through the WORD which I have spoken unto you"]{.class_4500} (John 15:3). ["Wherewithal shall a young man CLEANSE his way"]{.class_4500} (Psa. 119:9)? Do you know the answer? Even a [disciple]{.class_4501} needs to be CLEANSED and FORGIVEN after Calvary; see 1 John 1:3--10.

It is at that point that the "ultra-grace" preachers in the twentieth century (the Dry Cleaners: Bullinger, Stam, O'Hare, Baker, Moore, Watkins, Sharpe, et al.) fall out of line permanently. They made the mistake of thinking that since the blood atonement at Calvary covered all sins and obtained redemption and remission of all sins that no [further]{.class_4501}[]{#6g89t5Y_Rh2_sEm6hxUWsg2882.xhtml_page_315} asking for cleansing or forgiveness were necessary. They are for ["fellowship"]{.class_4500} (see 1 John 1:3--10).

All ultra-grace preachers get DIRTY FEET, [daily,]{.class_4501} from living in America. True, if they are saved they have been WASHED (vs. 10) and so are CLEAN (vs. 10). But ohhh! that "daily WALK" (Rom. 6:1, 8:1,13, 14:15; 1 Cor. 3:3; 2 Cor. 5:7, 10:2--3; Gal. 5:16; Eph. 2:2,10, 4:1,17, 5:2,8; Col. 1:10, 2:6, 3:7; 1 Thess. 2:13, 4:1, etc.).

The great lessons of salvation, sanctification, cleansing, and purification are all here. The saved sinner who puts his faith in the sacrificial lamb on the altar of the Tabernacle still had to wash his [feet]{.class_4501} if he ministered in the Tabernacle. Although Christ's one, effectual sacrifice for sinners was [permanent]{.class_4501} (Heb. 10:8--12), we still have to SPEAK to Him to get WATER FOR CLEANSING.

See where Moses "messed up" (Exod. 17:6; Num. 20:10--11)? "Christ smitten" doesn't have to be smitten again like a Catholic priest does fifty-two weeks a year, but you still ["speak\...unto the rock"]{.class_4500} [after it is smitten]{.class_4501} (1 Cor. 10:1--4; 1 Pet. 2:4--8; 1 John 1:8--10).

The bathtub and the shower (["He that is washed,"]{.class_4500} vs. 10) does not keep your [feet]{.class_4501} clean, especially when you walk barefoot or with sandals, as in the New Testament days. You wash your feet because you get DIRTY in your daily walk (see 1 Tim. 5:10 and Luke 7:44).

That is the great lesson of verse 14, which most of the commentators miss, though some of them are able to grasp the spiritual significance of daily cleansing.

12 So after he had washed their feet, and had taken his garments, and was set down again, he said unto them, Know ye what I have done to you?

13 Ye call me Master and Lord: and ye say well; for so I am.

14 If I then, your Lord and Master, have washed your feet; ye also ought to wash one another's feet.

15 For I have given you an example, that ye should do as I have done to you.

16 Verily, verily, I say unto you, The servant is not greater than his lord; neither he that is sent greater than he that sent him.

17 If ye know these things, happy are ye if ye do them.

The unclean disciple at the table is Judas (vs. 10). He is not a "saved devil" who "loses salvation" (see all Charismatic and Pentecostal nonsense). So as soon as our Lord reminds the eleven (again: see vs. 7) that they do not know exactly what He has done to them (vs. 12), He says, ["Ye call me MASTER and LORD\...for so I am"]{.class_4500} (vs. 14).

Why did He not just say ["Lord"]{.class_4500}? Wouldn't that have done it? Well, it would have, but here, the Lord is taking dead aim through the cross hairs at ONE disciple. It is the one who called Him ["MASTER"]{.class_4500} instead of ["LORD."]{.class_4500} Look at Matthew 26:25 and 26:49. [It is Judas.]{.class_4501}

Here in John 13, at verse 13, are [thirteen ]{.class_4501}words, without the italics, and thirty-nine letters: three times [thirteen]{.class_4501}. The man referred to is Judas (five letters) Iscariot (eight letters): [five plus eight is thirteen.]{.class_4501} ["Being ONE of the TWELVE"]{.class_4500} (John 6:71). (One and twelve are [thirteen]{.class_4501}.) We have [thirteen ]{.class_4501}in the upper room: Jesus and the twelve.

(Coincidence, naturally: [seven in a row.]{.class_4501})

When Judas shows up as the ["son of perdition,"]{.class_4500} he shows up in Revelation 13 at verse 18, which is six plus six plus six---"666." So since Friday is the sixth day of the week, you had better beware of Friday the 13th. (Coincidence, of course---[three more of them.]{.class_4501})

["For I have given you an example]{.class_4500} \[not a "sacrament" or "ordinance"\],[ that YE should do as I have done to you"]{.class_4500} (vs. 15). But He had not been just washing their feet, even though every commentator since 1800 stopped with that exposition.

Further, the ["example"]{.class_4500} was not just that they should "serve each other" or even to "humble themselves"; the commentators missed the real [spiritual lesson]{.class_4504} in the ["example."]{.class_4500} It was that they should minister the WORD to teach others to help their "brothers" to stay clean: note Hebrews 10:22; 1 Corinthians 14:3; 2 Corinthians 6:7, 10:8, 13:10; Romans 14:19; Ephesians 4:16, 29, 5:26; Colossians 3:16; 1 Thessalonians 4:18; and 1 Timothy 4:6.

["If ye know these things]{.class_4500} \[notice the plural\],[ happy are ye if ye do them"]{.class_4500} (vs. 17). Verse 16 is perfectly clear.

18 I speak not of you all: I know whom I have chosen: but that the scripture may be fulfilled, He that eateth bread with me hath lifted up his heel against me.

19 Now I tell you before it come, that, when it is come to pass, ye may believe that I am he.

20 Verily, verily, I say unto you, He that receiveth whomsoever I send receiveth me; and he that receiveth me receiveth him that sent me.

21 When Jesus had thus said, he was troubled in spirit, and testified, and said, Verily, verily, I say unto you, that one of you shall betray me.

22 Then the disciples looked one on another, doubting of whom he spake.

["I speak not of you all"]{.class_4500} (vs. 18). Obviously the "traitor" is [not]{.class_4501} clean, so he cannot help anyone else get clean or stay clean. Here, the Lord quotes Psalm 41:9. Verse 19 is clear. Fulfilled prophecy (in this case, Judas' treachery) is [the test of Deity]{.class_4501} according to twenty-five verses in one Book (Isa. 41--50). Notice a similar thing in John 2:17, 19:34--39, and Luke 22:61.

Mohammed's ability to "prophesy" anything that happened in history was so limited that apart from guessing that the European Roman powers would not be able to drive Arabs out of the Middle East---which was prophesied in Daniel 2 and 7, more than 1,000 years before Mohammed was born---Mohammed prophesied NOTHING. He was no more a "prophet" than Benny Goodman or Joe Louis. To say that Mohammed was Allah's "ONE prophet" is to "short sheet" Allah and make him look ridiculous. Not even a fallen angel (called a ["god"]{.class_4500} in Psa. 82) would be that [stupid.]{.class_4501} When a "prophet" can't make as many historical prophecies in a book containing 114 chapters as you can find in ONE chapter of Isaiah, no "god" would chose him to do anything more important than shelling peas.

Verse 20 is another verse emphasizing Christ's Deity.

["One of you shall betray me"]{.class_4500} (vs. 21). Matthew adds what He said immediately after this: ["It had been good for that man if he had not been born"]{.class_4500} (Matt. 26:24). Judas hung himself because he found himself placed slap-dab into the middle of Job's "moccasins" []{#6g89t5Y_Rh2_sEm6hxUWsg2882.xhtml_page_318}(Job 3:3--11, 16).

23 Now there was leaning on Jesus' bosom one of his disciples, whom Jesus loved.

24 Simon Peter therefore beckoned to him, that he should ask who it should be of whom he spake.

25 He then lying on Jesus' breast saith unto him, Lord, who is it?

26 Jesus answered, He it is, to whom I shall give a sop, when I have dipped it. And when he had dipped the sop, he gave it to Judas Iscariot, the son of Simon.

27 And after the sop Satan entered into him. Then said Jesus unto him, That thou doest, do quickly.

28 Now no man at the table knew for what intent he spake this unto him.

29 For some of them thought, because Judas had the bag, that Jesus had said unto him, Buy those things that we have need of against the feast; or, that he should give something to the poor.

30 He then having received the sop went immediately out: and it was night.

["Leaning on Jesus' bosom"]{.class_4500} (vs. 23) shows the "bed" nature of the first century dining chairs. There were not really chairs, although da Vinci painted some in; nor was there a "bench" of any kind. These feasting "recliners" are called ["BEDS"]{.class_4500} in the Old Testament (see Esth. 1:6, 7:8). The diners lay on their stomachs (or right and left sides) so their stomachs wouldn't be cramped while eating, as they are in all modern restaurants and dining rooms when one is "sitting." These apostles are "reclining," and John has leaned over and laid his head three inches [from the source of all life in this universe]{.class_4501} (see John 1:2--7 and comments).

Verse 22 was accompanied by voices, for in their doubting they said, ["Is it I?\...Is it I?"]{.class_4500} (Mark 14:19). Here it is that Judas asks ["Is it I?"]{.class_4500} (Matt. 26:25), along with the rest. Observe that when John asks Jesus who the guilty party is, John does NOT ask, ["Lord is it I?"]{.class_4500} (Matt. 26:22). John asks, ["LORD, WHO IS IT?"]{.class_4500} The "beloved apostle" had a conscience as clear as a soap-washed plate glass []{#6g89t5Y_Rh2_sEm6hxUWsg2882.xhtml_page_319}window.

["He it is, to whom I shall give the sop, when I have dipped it"]{.class_4500} (vs. 26). You see, when Christ told Judas ["Thou hast said"]{.class_4500} (Matt. 26:25), it is said quietly (and directly) to [him.]{.class_4501} This means Judas must have been eating right next to Christ on Christ's LEFT side (see Zech. 3:1--2). If Satan is on a culprit's RIGHT HAND (see Psa. 109:6), then Satan is on the Lord's [LEFT HAND;]{.class_4501} hence the Antichrist, who has a useless [right hand,]{.class_4501} could be called a "southpaw." You see, [the animal]{.class_4501} that pictures "666" is a CAT (leopard: Rev. 13); [cats have PAWS.]{.class_4501}

Ahhh! the unfathomable riches of the infallible King James Bible! How unsearchable is its third-grade English and its absolute truths past finding out!

We know where John was reclining: he was Jesus Christ's "right hand man," and Peter was next to John on his right. This explains what takes place in verses 24, 28, and 29. I do not know if Leonardo da Vinci ever painted them in that order, but I would be pleasantly SHOCKED if he happened to have gotten them right. After all, he was a devout Catholic; so devout that he turned an orthodox [Judean Jew]{.class_4501} from the tribe of [Judah]{.class_4501} into a reddish-blond, Irish "Mick."

["And when he had dipped the sop"]{.class_4500} (vs. 26).

The modern equivalent is "donut dunking" or dipping Tostidas into salsa mixtures. In the Middle East, this was considered to be showing a guest respect (cf. Ruth 2:14 for a good illustration). The English word chosen (["sop"]{.class_4500}) has been carefully removed from the [ASV, NASV, RSV, NRSV, NIV,]{.class_4501} and the Notorious King Jimmy Version, supposedly to "help the reader" understand the passage better. A funny thing happened when they did this. The word ["sop,"]{.class_4500} connected with Judas, just accidently happened to be an [acronym]{.class_4501} for ["Son Of Perdition."]{.class_4500} (Coincidence, of course, of course!)

Who do you suppose would want to cover up that "clue"?

After that, Satan enters as a DEVIL (diabolos: John 6:70)---[NOT]{.class_4501} a demon. Here, Judas does not become "the devil incarnate" (see comments on Gen. 3:15--16 in that Commentary). Satan simply enters him like he entered Ananias (Acts 5:3).

We have already commented on verses 28 and 29. ["He\...went immediately out: and it was NIGHT"]{.class_4500} (vs. 30).

["The night cometh, when no man can work"]{.class_4500}[]{#6g89t5Y_Rh2_sEm6hxUWsg2882.xhtml_page_320} (John 9:4). ["The light of the world"]{.class_4500} is about to be extinguished. The Christian, in this age, is a child of ["light"]{.class_4500} (see 1 Thess. 5:5). He is ["of the day." ]{.class_4500}The lost people are said to be ["of the NIGHT,"]{.class_4500} and they ["are drunken in the NIGHT"]{.class_4500} (1 Thess. 5:7). That second "night" will be the Tribulation.

31 Therefore, when he was gone out, Jesus said, Now is the Son of man glorified, and God is glorified in him.

32 If God be glorified in him, God shall also glorify him in himself, and shall straightway glorify him.

33 Little children, yet a little while I am with you. Ye shall seek me: and as I said unto the Jews, Whither I go, ye cannot come; so now I say to you.

34 A new commandment I give unto you, That ye love one another; as I have loved you, that ye also love one another.

35 By this shall all men know that ye are my disciples, if ye have love one to another.

For verse 31, see comments under John 12:28--31.

Verse 33 is a reference to John 8:21.

["A new commandment I give unto you"]{.class_4500} (vs. 34). This [new]{.class_4501} commandment sums up the old "Ten" according to the Apostle Paul (see comments on Rom. 13:8,10 in that Commentary). John harps on it in his epistle (1 John 2:7--8; 3:10--11,22--23, 4:7,11,20--21, 5:2--3).

["By this shall all men know that ye are my disciples"]{.class_4500} (vs. 35). That saying is only attested to by [Christian historians]{.class_4501} who describe the early days of Roman persecutions. If you are looking [in the Scriptures]{.class_4501} for a case where any group of men (["all men"]{.class_4500}) get converted by watching how Christians love each other, you will not find it.

Agrippa is ["almost"]{.class_4500} converted by the [boldness]{.class_4501} of the preacher (Acts 26:27--28). The same thing held true when Paul preached to Felix (Acts 24:25). The same thing was true when Paulus got converted (Acts 13:12). The way the Pharisees spotted Christ's disciples was by the fact that they were as [bold]{.class_4501} and [brave]{.class_4501} when []{#6g89t5Y_Rh2_sEm6hxUWsg2882.xhtml_page_321}preaching theology as any Rabbi Th.D. or Ph.D. who ever graduated from Rabbinical schools that taught the Talmud (Acts 4:13), although none of them had finished grade ("primary") school. BOLDNESS, not "love" was the mark of the disciples who spent any time with Jesus Christ (Acts 4:13).

Nevertheless the Lord's words were true. Take Romans 16:3­--4 for an example. Take 2 Timothy 1:16­--18 for another example.

36 Simon Peter said unto him, Lord, whither goest thou? Jesus answered him, Whither I go, thou canst not follow me now; but thou shalt follow me afterwards.

37 Peter said unto him, Lord, why cannot I follow thee now? I will lay down my life for thy sake.

38 Jesus answered him, Wilt thou lay down thy life for my sake? Verily, verily, I say unto thee, The cock shall not crow, till thou hast denied me thrice.

We have commented on verse 36 under 13:7.

Here comes Pete again, arguing with His Lord! ["Lord, why cannot I follow thee now?"]{.class_4500}

1\. "Because I didn't come to start an [armed insurrection]{.class_4501} (John 18:36; Matt. 12:19) to set up my kingdom" (John 6:15).

2\. "Because I need to be ['numbered with the transgressors']{.class_4500} (Isa. 53), and they must be THIEVES since I will come again ['as a THIEF in the night']{.class_4500} (1 Thess. 5:2) and STEAL someone's valuables (Mark 13:35; Matt. 24:43). [You ain't stole nothin'!"]{.class_4501}

3\. "Because I need someone to [feed my sheep]{.class_4501} after I leave, and since you're the big mouth in the group, I gave you the keys (Matt. 16) that open the doors to preaching the Gospel (Acts 2, 10). If you followed [me]{.class_4501} tomorrow, you wouldn't be around to feed nobody nothin', including yourself!"

But! (that's Pete!) But: ["I will lay down my life for your sake"]{.class_4500} (vs. 37). True (John 10), but not [tonight]{.class_4501} (John 18:11). "Izzat so?" (Street Greek for ["Wilt thou lay down\...etc."]{.class_4500})

Our Lord tells Simon Peter "before the cock crow" (a period of time called the ["third watch," ]{.class_4500}see Mark 13:35), Peter will deny Him three times. And so it comes to pass (John 18:27). For full details on the ["thrice" "before the cock crow"]{.class_4500} (Luke 22:61) and ["before the cock crow TWICE"]{.class_4500}[]{#6g89t5Y_Rh2_sEm6hxUWsg2882.xhtml_page_322} (Mark 14:30) see comments under Matthew 26:75 in that Commentary.

[]{#6g89t5Y_Rh2_sEm6hxUWsg2985.xhtml}

# Chapter 14 {.heading_74}

14:1 Let not your heart be troubled: ye believe in God, believe also in me.

2 In my Father's house are many mansions: if it were not so, I would have told you. I go to prepare a place for you.

3 And if I go and prepare a place for you, I will come again, and receive you unto myself; that where I am, there ye may be also.

4 And whither I go ye know, and the way ye know.

5 Thomas saith unto him, Lord, we know not whither thou goest; and how can we know the way?

Note, in verse 1, that Jesus is saying that even up to here the disciples have not yet [completely]{.class_4501} believed on Him, although they are "Theists." That is nothing to brag about for all [devils]{.class_4501} are Theists (James 2:19) like Mohammed, but most of them believe in the Deity of Christ (Mark 1:24, 5:7). Obviously, any [demon ]{.class_4501}is wiser than [Mohammed]{.class_4501}.

Come down to verses 9, 10, and 12 and note that Jesus uses the same appeal to His "own" as He used in dealing with the [unsaved Pharisees.]{.class_4501}

At verse 2, the entire Alexandrian Cult and the Scholars' Union, along with every apostate Fundamentalist in America who is organizing a wrecking crew, breaks down the gates of New Jerusalem and then destroys approximately 900,000,000 transparent gold "palaces" (by any earthly standard) and---following the Grand Old Opry's hillbilly song ("Jes give me a cabin in the corner of Gloryland!"), they replace them with some "rooms" built out of wood, hay, and stubble?

Unwittingly, they [stole]{.class_4501} Uncle Tom's [mansion]{.class_4501} from him, for in [Uncle Tom's Cabin]{.class_4501} (written back in the 1850s), "Uncle Tom" thinks he is going to get a MANSION when he dies! Poor, beknighted "Afro-American," "Whitey" tore down his home before he could move into it. Will the NAACP do nothing about this "vandalism"?

How well I remember an incident that happened in a Texas []{#6g89t5Y_Rh2_sEm6hxUWsg2985.xhtml_page_324}prison where I preached, back in the 1990s. A group of saved colored women had been able to get a Bible class together, on a daily basis, to study the Bible. In a group of about twenty, one young woman carried an [NIV.]{.class_4501} The rest of them carried Holy Bibles ([Authorized Versions]{.class_4501}). Naturally when they studied and turned to a verse, comparing Scripture with Scripture, it got kind of "touchy," for there were scores of places where the [NIV]{.class_4501} carrier couldn't find the verses: [they weren't in her NIV;]{.class_4501} for example: Acts 8:37, 9:5--6, 10:6; John 5:3--4, 9:56; Matthew 5:44, 6:13, 12:35, 13:51, 15:8, 17:21, 18:12; Mark 6:11, 7:16, 15:28; Luke 8:43, 9:56, 23:17; Revelation 1:11, 12:12, 14:5, 21:24; 2 Peter 1:12, 17, 20, 2:17, 3:10; 1 Thessalonians 1:1, etc., etc.

The Nutty Idiot's Version actually is missing [254 words]{.class_4501} in the first three gospels!

Well, the [NIV]{.class_4501} lady was called "Lucy," and her sisters in Christ would always kid her about the cheap piece of junk she was carrying about. She got very self indignant about it and defended it loudly, even where it couldn't be defended. She finally threatened to quit coming to the Bible study if they didn't quit harassin' her with "Lucy, dat ain't no Bible you got dare," "Git yoself a real Bible," and "You call dat thang a Bible!" and "Law me, chile, dat ain't no Bible. Go git chou a [King James Bible!"]{.class_4501}

The last study she was going to attend came around, and when she got there one of her closest friends said, "You know somethin', Lucy? You oughta bettah leeb our Bible study cause [we]{.class_4501} is bettah dan [you]{.class_4501} is!" Lucy yelled, "What you men [you]{.class_4501} is bettah than [ah]{.class_4501} is?! Weah you get dat kinda talk from!?" "Right heah in dee Bible, honeychile! You see dat deah?" And she held up a large print Bible with John 14:2 right in front of Lucy's eyes.

"See deah? We mus be a sight bettah dan you, cause we gets a MANSION when we gets to heben, and all you gits is an ole' ROOM!"

"Yous lyin', yous lyin!" screamed Lucy as she popped open her Nutty Idiot's Version to check John 14:2. And then you know what happened? She took one look at that fake Bible, threw it down on the floor, and went to the chaplain's office and got her a [King James Holy Bible.]{.class_4501} She did not leave the Bible study class.

"Out of the mouths of babes and sucklings thou hast []{#6g89t5Y_Rh2_sEm6hxUWsg2985.xhtml_page_325}perfected praise!"

If the Lord wasn't building a mansion for you, He ["would have told you"]{.class_4500} (vs. 2). You wouldn't have needed some [demon-possessed,]{.class_4501} backslidden, apostate Nicolaitan to tell you anything.

"I go to prepare a PLACE for you."

Do you think any man who had been a carpenter for eighteen years (see Luke 2--4) would have anything less than a mansion for ["his own"]{.class_4500} (John 13:1)? New Jerusalem is a prepared city for a prepared people, and Hell is a prepared place (see Matt. 25:41) for a prepared people (2 Pet. 2:3--4; Jude 13).

["I will come again"]{.class_4500} (vs. 3).

There lie four of the greatest words ever spoken by any man on this earth since the foundation of the heavens and the earth (Gen. 1:1). Jesus either told the truth or else He lied. (Make up your mind; it is that simple.)

There is not one political or religious or military leader in 191 nations, in downtown New York, that would dare even DISCUSS [those four words]{.class_4501} in public. You have not heard one Pope, since 1880, talk about the Second Coming of Jesus Christ to reign over this world from the Dome of the Rock in Jerusalem. You have not heard [one ]{.class_4501}preacher, minister, evangelist, shaman, witch doctor, Imam, ulama, bishop, nun, monk, archbishop, cardinal, guru, rabbi, patriarch, cantor, or ambassador pray the last prayer in the Bible (Rev. 22:20) anywhere [in public]{.class_4501} since 1800 and you [never will.]{.class_4501}

["I will come again, and receive you to myself; that where I am, there ye may be also"]{.class_4500} (vs. 3).

The promise will not be fulfilled, [literally,]{.class_4501} until the Second Advent. You are not going to have the Lord Jesus popping up and down from the right hand of the Father every time a Christian dies, to come and get his soul (Acts 7:56). The Christian DEPARTS when he dies (Phil. 1:21--23), and his soul goes straight up into the third heaven (as Paul's did; see comments on 2 Cor. 12:1--4 in that Commentary), so he is ["absent from the body, and\...present with the Lord" ]{.class_4500}(2 Cor. 5:1--8).

[This]{.class_4501} "coming again" (John 14:3) is a coming in the clouds to meet the Lord in the air (1 Thess. 4:13--17); hence, the BODIES of Peter, James, John, Thomas, Bartholomew, et al., along with the BODIES of []{#6g89t5Y_Rh2_sEm6hxUWsg2985.xhtml_page_326}Chrysostom, St. Pat, Luther, Columba, Knox, Wesley, Moody, Whitefield, Billy Sunday, Charles Finney, Jack Hyles, Adoniram Judson, Billy Bray, et al., go up to be "where He is" (see 1 Thess. 4:17, ["so shall we EVER be with the Lord"]{.class_4500}).

For the [fourth time,]{.class_4501} Freerkson (Liberty University, Lynchburg, Virginia) misses the Rapture. [Four times at bats:]{.class_4501} struck out every time. # Chapter 15 {.heading_74}

15:1 I am the true vine, and my Father is the husbandman.

2 Every branch in me that beareth not fruit he taketh away: and every branch that beareth fruit, he purgeth it, that it may bring forth more fruit.

3 Now ye are clean through the word which I have spoken unto you.

4 Abide in me, and I in you. As the branch cannot bear fruit of itself, except it abide in the vine; no more can ye, except ye abide in me.

5 I am the vine, ye are the branches: He that abideth in me, and I in him, the same bringeth forth much fruit: for without me ye can do nothing.

6 If a man abide not in me, he is cast forth as a branch, and is withered; and men gather them, and cast them into the fire, and they are burned.

7 If ye abide in me, and my words abide in you, ye shall ask what ye will, and it shall be done unto you.

8 Herein is my Father glorified, that ye bear much fruit; so shall ye be my disciples.

The ["true vine"]{.class_4500} is compared with the Roman Catholic vine of Deuteronomy 32:32---a vine which is connected to a Roman Church that uses fermented liquor in their "mass" (Deut. 32:33) and adopts Satan (Matt. 16:23) as their ["rock"]{.class_4500} on which their church is built (Deut. 32:31).

This Satanic ["rock"]{.class_4500} (see Matt. 16:23; Deut. 32:31) is not the ["Rock"]{.class_4500} that begat (Deut. 32:18) the nation of Israel (Exod. 4:23). It is not the New Testament ["Rock"]{.class_4500} that Peter and Paul speak of (see 1 Cor. 10:1--4; 1 Pet. 2:4--8). Subsequently, the false ["vine"]{.class_4500} is like the one in Ezekiel 15 that came from a corrupted vineyard which was originally planted correctly: see Isaiah 5:1--7. Jesus is ["the TRUE vine"]{.class_4500}; never associate Him with the twenty-first century Roman Church or apostate Judaism.

Their ["grapes"]{.class_4500} (Deut. 32:32) are bitter grapes that will be put into a vine vat and then STOMPED ON till [THEIR BLOOD]{.class_4501}[]{#6g89t5Y_Rh2_sEm6hxUWsg3187.xhtml_page_348} (see Deut. 32:14 for wine as blood) runs two feet high (Rev. 14:20) for nearly seventy-five miles. It is the Lord Jesus ---not Julia Ward Howe (["Battle Hymn of the Republic"]{.class_4501}) who will "tread out the vintage where the [grapes of WRATH]{.class_4501} are stored." They will not be [Southern young men]{.class_4501} from Alabama, Tennessee, Virginia, Mississippi, etc., although Julia Ward Howe pretended they were. They will be 200,000,000 UN, Catholic, Moslem, Hindu, and atheist "peace keeping forces" sent to be "peace keepers" in the "Middle East" (Rev. 14, 19; Joel 2; Isa. 63).

["Every branch in me\...he purgeth it, that it may bring forth more fruit"]{.class_4500} (vs. 2). You "purge" a vine by cleaning it of bugs and diseases. The main way you do this is by hard, close [pruning;]{.class_4501} as any vinekeeper knows, dead limbs are buzzed off (vs. 2).

Now with this similitude, over 1,000,000,000 Christians in 2004 were taught that a born-again believer IN Christ can be CUT OUT of Christ and then burned in Hell; see verse 6. This, then, is one of those typical "double-barreled bear traps" for the unwary (see remarks under John 5:25--28).

Before making any spiritual applications of a devotional nature---and there are plenty between verses 2 and 8---let us get our "sound doctrines" (2 Tim. 4) down before the Nicolaitan teachers, looking for "itching ears," drag us away with the Catholics, J.W.'s Mormons, Charismatics, Seventh-day Adventists, Methodists, Lutherans, Pentecostals, Campbellites, and other apostate, backslidden, Laodicean "Christians" in their anti-biblical theologies.

1\. Not one disciple present was "IN" Jesus Christ at this time.

2\. Not one of them was "abiding in Christ," and He was not "abiding" in any of them (see John 14:16--17).

3\. No [angels]{.class_4501} burn anyone in the fire (as in Matt. 13:40). ["Men"]{.class_4500} gather these branches and burn them (vs. 6).

4\. Many Christians alive now (and for the last twenty centuries) have been "IN Christ" with the Holy Spirit "abiding" in them (vs. 5), but they DID NOT bring forth ["much fruit."]{.class_4500} To tell the truth, about 70 percent of them brought forth [little]{.class_4501} or [no]{.class_4501} fruit in their lifetimes.

You see, when you study the Scriptures by comparing Scripture with Scripture, you don't find any New Testament Christian since []{#6g89t5Y_Rh2_sEm6hxUWsg3187.xhtml_page_349}A.D. 70 "losing his (or her) salvation" anywhere in the passage. It is simply an ILLUSTRATION likening His eleven disciples to branches extending from HIM. He is the[ "VINE."]{.class_4500} This is NOT the equivalent of the ["church"]{.class_4500} being His ["body"]{.class_4500} (see 1 Cor. 12:13--27). After Pentecost, believers are "bone of His[ ]{.class_4500}bone and flesh of His flesh" (Eph. 5), not appendages [stuck on to Him]{.class_4501} as branches are stuck on a tree.

No tree dies if you cut off a dozen branches every other year while pruning it. Try cutting off anything that "BRANCHES" out from your body and see how long it is before you bleed to death. Anyone who has grown blue Concord grapes or "Scuppernongs" knows you can prune fifty branches off a vine every year and it has no detrimental effects on the VINE at all; branches are just to CARRY the fruit [produced by the vine.]{.class_4501}

["Now ye are CLEAN through the word"]{.class_4500} (vs. 3).

There is the matchmate for John 13:10,15, which see. The instrument for pruning here is a TWO-EDGED SWORD: ["THE WORD which I have spoken unto you."]{.class_4500} It can pierce you to the ["marrow"]{.class_4500} (Heb. 4:12--13). Notice the constant repetition of ["IN me"]{.class_4500} in the passage: verse 2, verse 4 (twice), verse 5, verse 6, and verse 7. But notice that none of this could take place DOCTRINALLY until [after]{.class_4501} His prayer in John 17:21--22, and especially verse 23 (["I in them, and thou in me"]{.class_4500}).

Once these truths are applied it is no longer a 1. Husbandman, 2. A Vine, and 3. Branches; it is ["That they all may be one; as thou, Father, art in me, and I in thee\...I in them, and thou in me"]{.class_4500} (John 17:21,23).

Now, from a [devotional standpoint,]{.class_4501} there are many truths found in the illustration.

One: it is the reading and memorizing of the [word]{.class_4501} (see vs. 7) that keeps a Christian clean (see Psa. 119:9).

Two: God can [destroy]{.class_4501} a Christian who does not bear fruit; see Romans 8:13 and 1 Corinthians 3:17, 10:10.

Three: Good fruit comes in three sizes: ["Fruit"]{.class_4500} (vs. 2), ["more fruit" ]{.class_4500}(vs. 2), and ["much fruit"]{.class_4500} (vs. 5).

These three classes of Christians will be found again in Revelation 12:11 and 17:14. They are also illustrated in the lives of the twelve []{#6g89t5Y_Rh2_sEm6hxUWsg3187.xhtml_page_350}apostles (fruit); Peter, James, Andrew, and Phillip (more fruit); and John the "Beloved" (much fruit). One might say that there are "Apprentice" Christians, "Journeymen" Christians, and "Master" Christians; coincidently, one finds the Bachelor's degree, the Master's Degree, and the Doctor's Degree. (Or in Judaism, Rab, Rabbi, and Rabbak.)

Four: A Christian does not [produce]{.class_4501} fruit; he BEARS the fruit [the vine]{.class_4501} produces (see Gal. 5:22). We are, as the song says, "Only a branch in the vine." It is Jesus Christ "IN HIM" that fulfills the Law (see Rom. 8:4 in that Commentary) in a Christian; we do not fulfill it [ourselves.]{.class_4501}

Five: there are not only "sick" Christians, "weak" Christians, and "dead" Christians (see 1 Cor. 11:30 in that Commentary), but "withered" Christians (vs. 6) who are cut out of the vine. They are like ["salt"]{.class_4500} that has lost its seasoning. MEN (see Matt. 5:13)---not [angels]{.class_4501} (Matt. 13:39, 41)---cast them out ["to be trodden under foot of MEN."]{.class_4500}

Notice that the word ["abide"]{.class_4500} here is the one used in John 14:16. It is a reference to a permanent place of fellowship (see comments on 1 John 2:10, 27, 2:6, 3:24 in that Commentary), not just someone who "comes and goes."

Note, further, that the ["branch"]{.class_4500} is cast forth (vs. 6) ["AS"]{.class_4500} a branch. It can be [doctrinally]{.class_4501} an unsaved man who has never been in fellowship with Christ and so is not a [real]{.class_4501} "branch." The illustration cannot be applied to a Christian [doctrinally,]{.class_4501} for TWO possibilities are in the passage: if you are "in Christ" you are bearing fruit and abiding and getting your prayers answered (vs. 7), and if you are not bearing fruit and not getting your prayers answered you were never "IN HIM" in the first place. As we have said before, this does not match the ["in Christ"]{.class_4500} passages in the Pauline Epistles that deal with the BODY of Christ.

15 Henceforth I call you not servants; for the servant knoweth not what his lord doeth: but I have called you friends; for all things that I have heard of my Father I have made known unto you.

# Chapter 16 {.heading_74}

16:1 These things have I spoken unto you, that ye should not be offended.

2 They shall put you out of the synagogues: yea, the time cometh, that whosoever killeth you will think that he doeth God service.

3 And these things will they do unto you, because they have not known the Father, nor me.

4 But these things have I told you, that when the time shall come, ye may remember that I told you of them. And these things I said not unto you at the beginning, because I was with you.

5 But now I go my way to him that sent me; and none of you asketh me, Whither goest thou?

6 But because I have said these things unto you, sorrow hath filled your heart.

Verse 1 is telling them "I am warning you ahead of time about the opposition you are going to get if you faithfully witness for me. You are going to get it hard, so don't be surprised if you do. Don't get 'offended.' Take it for granted and expect more to come."

It is kind of like preparing men for combat, at least in an "outfit" that means business and knows what it is going to get into. The trick is to give the "rank and file" the worst possible scenario you can give them, making life just as miserable and uncomfortable for them as you can. The motive is to save their lives; you can only do it by preparing them for the worst.

["They shall put you out of the synagogues: yea, the time cometh, that whosoever killeth you will think that he doeth God service"]{.class_4500} (vs. 2). Look what the Old Testament saints went through (Heb. 11:33--37). Church Age saints (A.D. 500--A.D. 1700) went through ten times that much on a scale 100 times that size. As in the []{#6g89t5Y_Rh2_sEm6hxUWsg3304.xhtml_page_361}killing of Stephen (Acts 6:11--13), the attempted murder of Paul (Acts 21:28), and the arrest of Christ (Mark 14:61), the bloody killers always talk PIOUSLY and are always "concerned" about the ["welfare]{.class_4501} of others" (see Jer. 38:4; Acts 21:28; John 11:50--52). [All bloody religious killers are pious and "religious."]{.class_4501}

Everything was done in the "Blessed Name of Mary the Mother of God" or the "Confraternity of the Sacred Blood," or the "Holy Sacrament" on the altar of the "One, True, HOLY, Apostolic Church," etc.

The first persecutions have always come from orthodox religious leaders, as it will today: Roman Catholic, Greek Orthodox, Islam, etc.

Verses 5 and 6 are self explanatory.

7 Nevertheless I tell you the truth; It is expedient for you that I go away: for if I go not away, the Comforter will not come unto you; but if I depart, I will send him unto you.

8 And when he is come, he will reprove the world of sin, and of righteousness, and of judgment:

11 Of judgment, because the prince of this world is judged.

Again, He speaks of the work and ministry of ["the Comforter,"]{.class_4500} so again, every Moslem on this continent must blaspheme the Holy Ghost in order to make "Allah's Messenger" get the glory. 15 All things that the Father hath are mine: therefore said I, that he shall take of mine, and shall shew it unto you.

18 They said therefore, What is this that he saith, A little while? we cannot tell what he saith.

25 These things have I spoken unto you in proverbs: but the time cometh, when I shall no more speak unto you in proverbs, but I shall shew you plainly of the Father.

29 His disciples said unto him, Lo, now speakest thou plainly, and speakest no proverb.

30 Now are we sure that thou knowest all things, and needest not that any man should ask thee: by this we believe that thou camest forth from God.

31 Jesus answered them, Do ye now believe?

["Be of good cheer; I HAVE OVERCOME]{.class_4500} (past tense)[ the world"]{.class_4500} (vs. 33). This explains how John (the same author) uses the same term in 1 John 4:4 and 5:5. # Chapter 17 {.heading_74}

17:1 These words spake Jesus, and lifted up his eyes to heaven, and said, Father, the hour is come; glorify thy Son, that thy Son also may glorify thee:

2 As thou hast given him power over all flesh, that he should give eternal life to as many as thou hast given him.

3 And this is life eternal, that they might know thee the only true God, and Jesus Christ, whom thou hast sent.

# Chapter 18 {.heading_74}

18:1 When Jesus had spoken these words, he went forth with his disciples over the brook Cedron, where was a garden, into the which he entered, and his disciples.

2 And Judas also, which betrayed him, knew the place: for Jesus ofttimes resorted thither with his disciples.

3 Judas then, having received a band of men and officers from the chief priests and Pharisees, cometh thither with lanterns and torches and weapons.

4 Jesus therefore, knowing all things that should come upon him, went forth, and said unto them, Whom seek ye?

5 They answered him, Jesus of Nazareth. Jesus saith unto them, I am he. And Judas also, which betrayed him, stood with them.

6 As soon then as he had said unto them, I am he, they went backward, and fell to the ground.

7 Then asked he them again, Whom seek ye? And they said, Jesus of Nazareth.

8 Jesus answered, I have told you that I am he: if therefore ye seek me, let these go their way:

9 That the saying might be fulfilled, which he spake, Of them which thou gavest me have I lost none.

You may not have noticed it, but everything you found recorded in John 15--17 was spoken to walking disciples who were [walking ]{.class_4501}beside Jesus as He talked. You see, He left the "upper room" back at 14:31. His lengthy instructions (sixty verses) in chapters 15--16 and His high priestly prayer (John 17) are spoken before He and the eleven cross the bridge over the Kidron Valley, going east to Mt. Olivet.

Now, here in verse 1 is the ["garden."]{.class_4500} The name of this garden was ["Gethsemane,"]{.class_4500} which means "an olive press." This means that God chose a place for His Son to have His life [squeezed out of Him like olive oil. ]{.class_4501}That is what is involved in Luke 22:43--44. So (naturally!) it has been thrown out of the Greek text used for the [RV, RSV, ASV, NASV, NIV]{.class_4501}, and 200 other counterfeit "Bibles." All []{#6g89t5Y_Rh2_sEm6hxUWsg3560.xhtml_page_388}modern versions that used Nestle's text or Aland and Metzger's text or Tischendorf's text or Griesbach's text, declared that all [twenty-six Greek words]{.class_4501} should be dropped from Luke 22, as the Bible perverting scoundrels who produced their text declared that they were "KNOWN not to have been in [the originals."]{.class_4501}

Yeah, like they knew Sitting Bull wore lace underwear.

Olive oil represents a Holy [Root]{.class_4501} when dealing with nations (Rom. 11) and eternal life when dealing with [trees]{.class_4501} (Gen. 1--2; Rev. 22). It is ["GOLDEN"]{.class_4500} oil in Zechariah 4:12 where the [AV]{.class_4501} translators made a guess on the italics that sealed a Bible doctrine so far above their own heads (and 320 of the most brilliant Christian scholars who followed them) that we cannot even document the details here. ["The oil of gladness"]{.class_4500} (Heb. 1:9) that anointed God's Son was used by Samuel in anointing Saul (1 Sam. 10:1) and David (1 Sam. 16:13). It was [olive oil.]{.class_4501} And if ["the LIFE of the flesh is in the BLOOD"]{.class_4500} then Christ had eternal life in His blood (Acts 20:28). Then His "life's blood" would be likened to ["GOLDEN OIL."]{.class_4500}

Gold, "in solution," is [red.]{.class_4501}

See the comments on Adam's GOLD covering from "gold dust" in the Commentaries on Job, Genesis, and Revelation (also [Black is Beautiful,]{.class_4501} 1996, pp. 191--193).

We have already commented on verse 1.

Verses 2 and 3 need no explanation. Notice ["weapons"]{.class_4500} in verse 3. This explains why Peter drew the sword on them (vs. 10), and it explains why he fled with the rest of the disciples: he wanted to stay and [fight,]{.class_4501} but when Jesus gave him the command in verse 11, He put him into the position of an UNARMED fisherman facing [ARMED TROOPS.]{.class_4501}

All is clear in verse 4. He walks into His own doom: ["Jesus\...WENT FORTH."]{.class_4500}

In verse 5 when Jesus asks them for whom they are looking, they give the right answer. Jesus then says, ["I AM HE."]{.class_4500} When He does this, He gets the results that a master Aikido Sensei would get upon "projecting Ki." His enemies fall backward on the ground (vs. 6). The reason for this is apparent if you simply note the "italics" for ["he"]{.class_4500} in verses 5--6. They are properly placed, but "in the raw," they say, ["I AM"]{.class_4500} (note how the Greek pronoun "ego" can be used for "I" or "I []{#6g89t5Y_Rh2_sEm6hxUWsg3560.xhtml_page_389}am").

Jesus has put the "Tetragrammaton" on them (Heb.: [JHVH]{.class_4501}). It is the same one He uses in John 6:35, 8:12, 10:7, 9, 11, (Gk.: [ego eimi]{.class_4501}); only this time, He manifests His Deity when He says the two words. The force of those [two words]{.class_4501} can knock a mob flat. A double pronouncement: ["I AM THAT I AM"]{.class_4500} (Exod. 3:14) would have knocked Moses clean off Sinai if the Lord had put any power into the proclamation. The important thing to notice here is the remarkable thing that took place [after]{.class_4501} they were knocked down; for after being knocked flat in the dirt, Judas gets up (he was leading them; vss. 2--3,5), steps forward, and kisses the One who just [knocked him flat ]{.class_4501}(Matt. 26:47--49). Imagine it if you can.

The "marvel of the ages" is not the "Sovereignty of God"---you would expect an infinite, eternal, omniscient God to be in charge of things. The marvel of the ages is [man's depravity,]{.class_4501} illustrated here and in dozens of places in the Bible. Here you can see the acme or zenith of total depravity. Judas has just seen and FELT a demonstration of the power and wrath of a Being in whose presence he (Judas) had been for forty-two months. [Now he had Jesus Christ's Deity demonstrated right to his face;]{.class_4501} it was aimed at [him]{.class_4501} to prevent him from doing what he was doing. He simply brushed it aside like Pharaoh brushed aside forty-two months of plagues, diseases, deaths, property destruction, dead cattle, ruined crops, sores, and storms, and went right on his wicked way (Exod. 13--14) after burying his heir to the throne.

Note that this will be the response of the UN to THE TRUTH in the next ten years in the twenty-first century: read Revelation 6:4, 6, 8, 12, 8:5--13, 9:3--6, 9:18--21). Like Pharaoh, they are all "positive thinkers."

["Neither repented they of their murders, nor of their sorceries, nor of their fornication, nor of their thefts"]{.class_4500} (Rev. 9:21).

That was Hitler's response after losing France, Poland, Sweden, Norway, Denmark, Austria, Hungary, North Africa, and Russia, and then Berlin and Germany. [He repented of nothing.]{.class_4501} He thought "positively" to the end.

Judas has confronted Deity and "tangled with it" and even been []{#6g89t5Y_Rh2_sEm6hxUWsg3560.xhtml_page_390}knocked flat by it, but he still gets up and goes right on "sinning." Ditto about 2,000,000,000 sinners in the twenty-first century.

How many times God has dealt with some men, in no uncertain terms, and yet they have ignored it, I wouldn't venture to say; but in the ministry (I've been "at it" fifty-five years at the time of this writing), I have known and have talked with man after man after man whose history, before he came to Christ (or before he surrendered to the call to preach after coming to Christ), was nothing but one long series of disasters. They began with little things like broken arms or legs, or getting whippings or getting kicked out of school (or the Army), or losing jobs, all the way to lethal gun fights, lethal car wrecks, lethal airplane crashes (where someone else was killed), burying young wives and children, homes burnt down, debilitating or deadly diseases, bankruptcy, and imprisonment.

One time, "down South," a farmer told his "hired help" to get a donkey to pull a wagon. After fifteen minutes of kicking, whipping, cursing, and slapping the animal, his owner said, "Don't do that! Jes' use WILL power, man. Ya gotta use WILL power." The dark one said: "Yeahsush boss, but you oughtta see dis hyeah animal's WONT power!"

# Chapter 19 {.heading_74}

19:1 Then Pilate therefore took Jesus, and scourged him.

2 And the soldiers platted a crown of thorns, and put it on his head, and they put on him a purple robe,

3 And said, Hail, King of the Jews! and they smote him with their hands.

4 Pilate therefore went forth again, and saith unto them, Behold, I bring him forth to you, that ye may know that I find no fault in him.

5 Then came Jesus forth, wearing the crown of thorns, and the purple robe. And Pilate saith unto them, Behold the man!

6 When the chief priests therefore and officers saw him, they cried out, saying, Crucify him, crucify him. Pilate saith unto them, Take ye him, and crucify him: for I find no fault in him.

In one last desperate effort to save the life of an innocent man, Pilate (at last) does some [negative thinking]{.class_4501} and decides that a "blood lust" has seized the mob and if they just see a little shed blood it will cool 'em off and appease them so they will let him release "the prophet." So he has Jesus Christ scourged within an inch of His life: see Psalm 129:3; Isaiah 53:5, and 50:6.

These lashings turn out to be the ["stripes"]{.class_4500} of Isaiah 53 and 1 Peter 2:24, whereby ["ye were healed."]{.class_4500}

When they "lay it on Him" with a cat-of-nine tails, they are laying YOUR sins and MY sins on Him; yet not one man in the Praetorium guards­man's hall knows what is even taking place (Mark 15:16). After the whipping, they make a mock crown out of thorns and slam it down tight on His skull with bamboo rods (Mark 15:19). They stick a reed in His hand to [imitate]{.class_4501} the ["sceptre"]{.class_4500} that shall not depart from ["Judah"]{.class_4500} (Gen. 49:10), bow their knees to Him to [ridicule]{.class_4501} Philippians 2:10, and then spit upon Him to show their contempt. (Note how the spitting and buffeting had already been done by the Sanhedrin \[Matt. 26:67\] before Caiaphas turned Christ over to []{#6g89t5Y_Rh2_sEm6hxUWsg3834.xhtml_page_419}Pilate. At that time \[see Matt. 26:68\], His enemies played a little game of "Blind man's bluff," pretending that Jesus Christ didn't know who was hitting Him. He could have called out the name of every man there, his birthday, his [marriage ]{.class_4501}status, the location of his residence, and his sins.

In Matthew 26 and John 19, sinners are "messing" with their Maker (see John 1:3, 9 and Col. 1:16--17). The night they captured Jesus He informed Simon Peter that He was perfectly able to raise up an army of ["twelve legions of angels"]{.class_4500} if He intended to deliver Himself (Matt. 26:53). P.S. [ONE angel can kill 185,000 armed soldiers in less than twelve hours]{.class_4501} (2 Kings 19:35).

Jesus Christ is "taking it" from sinners whom He did not have to "take" anything. He was---as the author of Hebrews put it (Heb. 12:3)---enduring a ["CONTRADICTION of sinners against himself."]{.class_4500}

On goes Lloyd Douglas' ["The Robe"]{.class_4501} (John 19:2). It was about as powerful and as "holy" and as sacred as a diseased [pig pen]{.class_4501}---notwithstanding the literature about it promoted by unsaved sinners in the NCCC and Hollywood. Christ never had a ["robe"]{.class_4500} in His lifetime. His clothes were called ["coat," "vesture," "raiment"]{.class_4500} (Heb. 1:11--12) and ["garments."]{.class_4500} Never "robe" or "robes." [That is Roman Catholic-apostate Protestant-religious DUNG.]{.class_4501}

The ["purple robe"]{.class_4500} here (19:5) could only remind you of the purple and scarlet clothing of the Roman Catholic Whore on Seven Hills (Rev. 17:4--5). [It was a Roman robe.]{.class_4501} When Bev Shea sings "May its glories endure forever," you should sing "May it stay in [the dumpster]{.class_4501} where it belongs" (see Luke 20:46).

[Third verdict]{.class_4501} (vs. 4). ["I find no fault in him."]{.class_4500} The judge, after TRIPLE JEOPARDY, gives the same verdict: [not guilty.]{.class_4501}

["Behold THE man"]{.class_4500} (vs. 5).

The [purple robe]{.class_4501} is stained with Christ's blood; it is the counterfeit of [His garment]{.class_4501} which will be dipped in the blood of His enemies (see Isa. 63:1--6).

The crown of thorns is a make-believe crown which will be [replaced]{.class_4501} with MANY CROWNS (Rev. 19:12), and those crowns will be torn off the heads of TEN KINGS (Rev. 17:12). They are first mentioned in Daniel 2:43 and are ["crowns"]{.class_4500} on the ["heads"]{.class_4500} of kings (Rev. 13:1).

"Behold THE man!"

Pilate is visibly impressed. He makes the announcement like there was only one [real]{.class_4501} man around at that time. The American concept of Jesus Christ as a "man," defined in the rock opera "Jesus Christ, Superstar," back in the 70s, is halfway between a fornicating civil rights worker who shacks up with Mary Magdalene while engaging in "civil disobedience" and a fairy standing in urine (National Endowment of the Arts, etc.) intoning ["Blessed are the peacemakers\...etc."]{.class_4500}

The "Jesus" in the rock opera is a teenaged jerk without a spiritual bone in his body. The kid probably served as a lifeguard for a whirlpool in a spa; he was so anemic he would have to do ten push-ups, after he was stabbed, to shed any blood. Most "rock stars" have biceps that look like a flea bite on a piece of spaghetti.

Everyone blithely (and oh, my God! how [piously!]{.class_4501}) forgets that the ["man"]{.class_4500} who slept on the ground at night (Luke 9:58) was a first century [carpenter]{.class_4501} for eighteen years (Luke 2--4), and His companions were [commercial fishermen.]{.class_4501} ["The man"]{.class_4500} is a "man's man"---not some mushy, sentimental, bass-speaking sissy swishing around in long robes and teaching "possibility thinking."

Behold His [life.]{.class_4504} Behold His [death.]{.class_4504} Behold His [enemies.]{.class_4504} Behold His [friends.]{.class_4504} Behold His [influence.]{.class_4504} Behold His [portrait ]{.class_4504}(Song of Sol. 5:11--12; Isa. 53:1--2). ["BEHOLD THE MAN!"]{.class_4500}

"Crucify him, crucify him!"

[Fourth verdict:]{.class_4501} quadruple jeopardy! ["I find no fault in him."]{.class_4500}

[Tried four times for the same offence.]{.class_4504} Illegal. Criminal. "Every human being has a spark of divinity in him," right? Like [Hitler]{.class_4504} or Anton Pavelich, perhaps? "Man is the measure of all things," right? Like Dean Coryl? Jim Jones and Ted Bundy, perhaps? Pilate and Herod, maybe? How about "your brothers in Christ"---Fatherhood of God, remember; and Brotherhood of Man---right? Charlie Manson and John Gotti, perhaps? How about Bin Laden? No? 7 The Jews answered him, We have a law, and by our law he ought to die, because he made himself the Son of God.

8 When Pilate therefore heard that saying, he was the more afraid;

9 And went again into the judgment hall, and saith unto Jesus, Whence art thou? But Jesus gave him no answer.

10 Then saith Pilate unto him, Speakest thou not unto me? knowest thou not that I have power to crucify thee, and have power to release thee?

11 Jesus answered, Thou couldest have no power at all against me, except it were given thee from above: therefore he that delivered me unto thee hath the greater sin.

12 And from thenceforth Pilate sought to release him: but the Jews cried out, saying, If thou let this man go, thou art not Caesar's friend: whosoever maketh himself a king speaketh against Caesar.

13 When Pilate therefore heard that saying, he brought Jesus forth, and sat down in the judgment seat in a place that is called the Pavement, but in the Hebrew, Gabbatha.

My, my, what a [study]{.class_4501} this turns out to be in regards to man's "evolution" since 2,000,000 B.C.! Aren't we really "at the top of the ladder," here, with the highest, most [respected,]{.class_4501} powerful rulers in the Roman Empire and the most [godly,]{.class_4501} educated, dedicated, qualified, religious leaders on earth? My, what a "change of pace" for them when [ONE MAN]{.class_4501} shows up!

["He ought to die, because he made himself the Son of God"]{.class_4500} (vs. 7). At last they told the truth. That was the [first charge]{.class_4501} they brought against Jesus when they kidnapped him at "spear point" (Mark 14:43).

Verse 8 is self explanatory. Pilate's original indifference (18:31) had gradually evolved into [anger]{.class_4501} (18:35, 38), and then the Barabbas incident had taken him on into "stress," tension, and nervousness. Now he is "flat scared" (as they say down South): ["he was the more AFRAID."]{.class_4500}

The problem is that a supernatural issue has now suddenly thrust itself into the original staged production. It is no longer a matter of "forbidding to pay taxes" and "stirring up the people" (Luke 23:2). ["GOD"]{.class_4500} has been thrust into the courtroom. Oops! "When Pilate therefore heard THAT saying\...."

20:1 The first day of the week cometh Mary Magdalene early, when it was yet dark, unto the sepulchre, and seeth the stone taken away from the sepulchre.

5 And he stooping down, and looking in, saw the linen clothes lying; yet went he not in.

8 Then went in also that other disciple, which came first to the sepulchre, and he saw, and believed.

# Chapter 21 {.heading_74}

21:1 After these things Jesus shewed himself again to the disciples at the sea of Tiberias; and on this wise shewed he himself.

2 There were together Simon Peter, and Thomas called Didymus, and Nathanael of Cana in Galilee, and the sons of Zebedee, and two other of his disciples.

3 Simon Peter saith unto them, I go a fishing. They say unto him, We also go with thee. They went forth, and entered into a ship immediately; and that night they caught nothing.

4 But when the morning was now come, Jesus stood on the shore: but the disciples knew not that it was Jesus.

5 Then Jesus saith unto them, Children, have ye any meat? They answered him, No.

6 And he said unto them, Cast the net on the right side of the ship, and ye shall find. They cast therefore, and now they were not able to draw it for the multitude of fishes.

7 Therefore that disciple whom Jesus loved saith unto Peter, It is the Lord. Now when Simon Peter heard that it was the Lord, he girt his fisher's coat unto him, (for he was naked,) and did cast himself into the sea.

Peter and his buddies have probably been awaiting the arrival spoken of in Mark 16:7, and since they are now in Galilee (["at the sea of Tiberias"]{.class_4500}) they are tired of waiting, so they go fishing. There are seven of them in the [two boats]{.class_4501} (Peter, James, John, Thomas, Nathanel, and ["two other of his disciples"]{.class_4500} (vs. 2).

Verse 3 is self explanatory. Many a fisherman has had many a ["night"]{.class_4500} just like this one. It is usually attributed to a number of things: a full moon, a neap tide, an owl hooting on a branch, an east wind, "not payin' the preacher," or just plain [bad luck.]{.class_4501}

Again, notice the "common" appearance that Christ makes in His post-resurrection body: ["the disciples knew not that it was Jesus."]{.class_4500} It takes the beloved apostle (John) to recognize Him and even he does not hazard a guess who it is until the miraculous []{#6g89t5Y_Rh2_sEm6hxUWsg4327.xhtml_page_476}draught of fishes (153). This reminds John Zebedee of his first encounter with Jesus Christ, way back in Luke 5:3--7.

The net is a "seine net" (Gk.: [diktuon]{.class_4501}) not a ["cast"]{.class_4501} net which is thrown into the water (Mark 1:16). This one will be a long net which is let off the boat and dragged through the water. The Galilean boys used [both]{.class_4501} kinds of nets. Our term "drag net" has been plagiarized from John 21:8. What has happened is the net is so heavy that Pete and John can't pull it up. The other four boys are in a "little ship" nearby (vs. 8) and they have to help Peter, James, and John DRAG the cast net ashore.

If the fishes had averaged even two pounds apiece, it would mean 306 pounds to haul, but you are informed that these were not even average sized fishes: they were ["GREAT fishes."]{.class_4500} Giving them four pounds apiece (plus the weight of the lead on the net), someone is having to pull over 612 pounds of wet weights 100 yards to shore (300 feet is 200 cubits).

Any fisherman would call a catch like that a "miracle," even if a [theologian]{.class_4501} wouldn't, and any commercial fisherman would be better informed on it than any theologian on earth.

Simon Peter, just as impetuous as ever, dives into the sea and swims the "220" in six minutes flat. The "coat" doesn't go on till you jump in the water. Normally you would take your coat OFF before you take a dip. The fisherman's coat here is a waterproof poncho, and though it cannot keep you dry [swimming]{.class_4501} it keeps the WIND off you when you come out on the deck or the beach. The idea is to keep you from getting pneumonia. If you don't believe me, ask any commercial fisherman between Biloxi, Mississippi, and Port St. Joe, Florida.

Peter was only "naked" in the sense that he was not fully dressed (see 2 Sam. 6:20). This coat is a ["ependutes,"]{.class_4501} considered to be "upper clothing" which covers the arms, neck, chest, stomach, and thighs.

8 And the other disciples came in a little ship; (for they were not far from land, but as it were two hundred cubits,) dragging the net with fishes.

9 As soon then as they were come to land, they saw a []{#6g89t5Y_Rh2_sEm6hxUWsg4327.xhtml_page_477}fire of coals there, and fish laid thereon, and bread.

10 Jesus saith unto them, Bring of the fish which ye have now caught.

11 Simon Peter went up, and drew the net to land full of great fishes, an hundred and fifty and three: and for all there were so many, yet was not the net broken.

12 Jesus saith unto them, Come and dine. And none of the disciples durst ask him, Who art thou? knowing that it was the Lord.

13 Jesus then cometh, and taketh bread, and giveth them, and fish likewise.

14 This is now the third time that Jesus shewed himself to his disciples, after that he was risen from the dead.

This is the ["third time"]{.class_4500} that Jesus shows Himself to these disciples (vs. 14); the other two times being the first Sunday night after the resurrection and a week later on the same night (John 20:19,26). At this time, only seven disciples are present out of the 11.

Verses 9 and 10 are self explanatory, and we have already commented on verse 11.

Again, [for the fourth time]{.class_4501} (Luke 24:18; John 20:14, 21:4), notice how "natural" Jesus appears. According to the text, which not one commentator dared handle, they are [tempted]{.class_4501} to ask "Who are you?" and didn't DARE to. Dr. Dods tries this: "each man felt convinced it was the Lord, and a new REVERENCE prevented them from questioning Him." You mean questioning [who He was]{.class_4501} don't you? They "QUESTIONED" Him plenty after breakfast (vss. 20, 23).

They know they are looking at the same one they looked at TWICE before (see above), but when they see the fire and the coals and His sudden appearance after ten to fourteen days, when the last time they saw Him was fifty miles south of there, they are "disturbed." Put it this way: "they are under stress." They are "coping" with "tension." At least that is how some college-educated, radical, left-wing, atheistic newspaper reporter would put it.

Breakfast in northern Israel is "bread and fish" (vs. 13), as in the feeding of the 5,000.

15 So when they had dined, Jesus saith to Simon Peter, Simon, son of Jonas, lovest thou me more than these? He saith unto him, Yea, Lord; thou knowest that I love thee. He saith unto him, Feed my lambs.

16 He saith to him again the second time, Simon, son of Jonas, lovest thou me? He saith unto him, Yea, Lord; thou knowest that I love thee. He saith unto him, Feed my sheep.

17 He saith unto him the third time, Simon, son of Jonas, lovest thou me? Peter was grieved because he said unto him the third time, Lovest thou me? And he said unto him, Lord, thou knowest all things; thou knowest that I love thee. Jesus saith unto him, Feed my sheep.

18 Verily, verily, I say unto thee, When thou wast young, thou girdedst thyself, and walkedst whither thou wouldest: but when thou shalt be old, thou shalt stretch forth thy hands, and another shall gird thee, and carry thee whither thou wouldest not.

The next scene is an emotional one. Peter has proved that he is no longer "the rock" on which [any]{.class_4501} church could be built. Not only did he prove it [before]{.class_4501} the crucifixion, but again in Galatians 2, [after]{.class_4501} the Ascension. If your church is built on Simon (Matt. 16) Peter, it is built on "sinking sand." What is about to take place is a threefold confession of faith after a threefold [denial]{.class_4501} of the faith (see comments under John 18:25--27).

The dearly beloved brethren---any apostate Funnymentalist in the Alexandrian Cult---deeply resent what they take to be a mistranslation of ["feed my sheep"]{.class_4500} ([poimaino]{.class_4501}) "which should be 'TEND my sheep,'" and ["phileo,"]{.class_4501} which "should be [AGAPAO."]{.class_4501} Here we will find all of the destructive critics of the New Testament at their "Sunday best." This time Jerry Falwell's apostate "scholars" actually take out time to write down [twenty-seven lines]{.class_4501} correcting the [King James]{.class_4501} text. They did this after getting lockjaw on 20:17 and in making a contribution of [one line]{.class_4501} on John 18:6, regarding the Deity of Jesus Christ.

Now meditate on that "balance" for about a century.

Suddenly there is this magnificent "burst of inspiration" when the professor finds a way to get rid of [two words]{.class_4501} in the [AV]{.class_4501} text. Doesn't this sign, seal, and deliver every criticism I ever made of this Cult since 1950? What could possess any educated JACKASS, like this, to miss the following Scriptural revelations in [his own language]{.class_4501}---see the comments under John 1:1, 12, 16--17, 21, 25, 51, 2:3, 6, 10, 24--25, 3:5, 21, 29, 36, 4:18, 24, 5:24, 25, 28, 39, 6:63, 70, 7:17, 8:47, 9:24, 35, 10:1--6, 16, 34, 11:44, 51, 12:24, 13:13, 25, 38, 14:23, 15:6, 16:21, 26, 17:21, 18:9, 13, 20, 31, 37--38, 19:2, 9, 15, 23--24, 26, 30, 34, etc., and then [suddenly]{.class_4501} get so "filled with the Spirit" as a God-called ["man of God"]{.class_4500} (1 Tim. 6:11) who is ["APT TO TEACH"]{.class_4500} (1 Tim. 3:2) since he "labored in sound doctrine" (1 Tim. 5:17)---don't you know, baby!!---that he can write twenty-one lines to prove that "literal correspondence" of a foreign language is more important than "dynamic equivalents," while missing the main purpose of the entire text (John 21:15--17): the fact that Peter must make restitution by confessing [as many times]{.class_4501} as he had [denied.]{.class_4501}

That is twenty-first century Conservative, Evangelical, Protestant, Fundamentalism at its BEST.

Falwell's commentator (Dobson) is in perfect line with Donald Guthrie ([The New Bible Commentary,]{.class_4501} 1953). Watch Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown go into spasms and raptures of praise when ridding the reader of the [King James]{.class_4501}' text, "It has been observed that Trench who has some [beautiful]{.class_4501} remarks on this subject\...that the word here is [studiously]{.class_4501} changed from one meaning simply 'to feed' to one signifying 'to tend as a shepherd' denoting the ABIDING exercise of the 'pastoral vocation' and its highest 'function' (p. 484)."

The [AV]{.class_4501} translated the word ["poimaino"]{.class_4501} absolutely consistently throughout John 21:16; Acts 20:28; 1 Corinthians 9:7; 1 Peter 5:2; Jude 12; Revelation 12:6; and Luke 17:7--11. The reason is obvious if you compare Matthew 9:36 and 14:15--21. The proclivity of the destructive critics to get around the word ["FEED"]{.class_4500} is due to the fact that the FEEDING involves [supernatural food]{.class_4501} (Luke 4:4) that is to be [ministered]{.class_4501} (see John 13:7,15 and comments) to the flock. If anyone would know this, it would be Simon Peter, who got messed up on the "WORDS" in a Book (Matt. 26:31).

"Tending the flock" in the eyes of every nineteenth, twentieth, and twenty-first century, backslidden, destructive critic of the []{#6g89t5Y_Rh2_sEm6hxUWsg4327.xhtml_page_480}[AV]{.class_4501} text could [AVOID the main function.]{.class_4501} The truth is you cannot protect or "tend" or care for any flock that has been [starved to death.]{.class_4501} Fifty major Christian colleges, universities, and seminaries in America, between 1901--2000, DESTROYED more than 2,000 "flocks" by "tending them" [without ]{.class_4501}"feeding" them (see [The Last Grenade, Ruckman's Battlefield Notes,]{.class_4501} and [The Christian Liars' Library]{.class_4501}).

That is why they struck ["feed"]{.class_4500} out of verse 16.

They could all TEND without FEEDING.

They all forgot that the "FEEDING of the flock" was mentioned [seven times]{.class_4501} in the gospels and again in Revelation (Rev. 12).

God considered "FEEDING a flock" more than TWICE as important as the Virgin Birth, the Ascension of Christ, the Rich Man in Hell, the Prodigal Son, the Temptation of Christ, and the calling out of the twelve disciples. [None of those subjects can be found in all FOUR gospels.]{.class_4501}

No "early Christian" [baptized babies;]{.class_4501} no "early Christian" [sprinkled]{.class_4501} anyone; no "early Christian" pretended the Lord's Supper was a [sacrifice;]{.class_4501} no "early Christian" called any pastor a ["priest"]{.class_4501}; all early Christian bishops were [married;]{.class_4501} no "early Christian" prayed to [Mary]{.class_4501} or reverenced her; no "early Christian" believed in [Purgatory]{.class_4501} or [Limbo;]{.class_4501} no "early Christian" believed that God was through with Israel [permanently;]{.class_4501} no "early Christian" believed in Archbishops, nuns, or monks of any kind; no "early Christian" used the [sign of the cross]{.class_4501} for anything; no "early Christian" carried beads or said rosaries to anyone; no "early Christian" mistook[ "new wine"]{.class_4500} for fermented liquor; no "early Christian" believed that works contributed anything to New Testament salvation; no "early Christian" would even look at the [Apocryphal books]{.class_4501}[]{#6g89t5Y_Rh2_sEm6hxUWsg4327.xhtml_page_492} in every modern Catholic Bible; and no "early Christian" would connect ROME with anything but POLITICAL and MILITARY power by [PAGAN IDOLATORS.]{.class_4501}

If the phantom books HAD been written and were "extant" somewhere (already written), the Lord purposely [excluded them]{.class_4501} from His compilation of what He called ["the scripture of truth"]{.class_4500} (Dan. 10:21) and ["THE HOLY SCRIPTURES"]{.class_4500} (Rom. 1:2).

God Almighty was never slightly concerned with what ANY Christian, of ANY profession, said or thought about such matters. He is less concerned about those educated gentlemen in the [twenty-first]{.class_4501} [century ]{.class_4501}than He was in the[ first century]{.class_4501}. Note that the ["should"]{.class_4500} is the ["should"]{.class_4500} of John 3:16 and Acts 2:47. All Catholics imagined it was an [imperative]{.class_4501} instead of a [future.]{.class_4501} Blew it again. Remember, like those two "beauties" we studied in John 2:19; Acts 2:16; Matthew 16:18; Titus 3:5; and Eph. 3:2---["THIS"]{.class_4500} and ["OF"]{.class_4500}?

"Hath not God made foolish the wisdom of this world?"

No man, living or dead, could write a true "life of Christ," for He is still [alive]{.class_4501} and has several thousand eons "to go." Furthermore, John never said such writings WERE written or WOULD be written. He just said ["IF" ]{.class_4500}they [had been written,]{.class_4501} they couldn't tell you "the half of it" (see the Queen of Sheba: 1 Kings 10:7).
